source_lines.text: 9. Conclusion 2: So, to put it simply, older things were often built to endure,
while modern things are often built to adapt. Neither is automatically better in
every sense; it depends on what kind of value a person cares about most.
[總結來說，如果要簡單說，舊時代的東西往往是為了耐得住時間而造的，而現代的東西則
往往是為了適應生活而設計的。兩者並不能簡單地說哪一個在所有方面都更好，還是要看
一個人最重視的是哪一種價值。]

question.english: How well-respected are teachers in your society?
question.chinese: 在你的社會裡，老師受到多大程度的尊重？

source_lines.text: Topic: How well-respected are teachers in your society?
在你的社會裡，老師受到多大程度的尊重？

components.english: Overall, I’d say teachers are still generally well respected in Hong Kong, especially by families who see education as the backbone of a child’s future.
components.chinese: 整體來說，我會說老師在香港仍然普遍受到尊重，特別是在那些把教育視為孩子未來骨幹的家庭裡更是如此。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Overall, I’d say teachers are still generally well respected in Hong Kong,
especially by families who see education as the backbone of a child’s future.
[整體來說，我會說老師在香港仍然普遍受到尊重，特別是在那些把教育視為孩子未來骨幹
的家庭裡更是如此。]

components.english: The main reason is that teachers are often seen as doing far more than simply delivering lessons. They help shape discipline, confidence, values, and sometimes even a child’s sense of direction. In that sense, many people do not view teachers as mere employees standing in front of a whiteboard; they see them as guides who help young people find their footing in life.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，老師很多時候被視為不只是單純地教書而已。他們同時也在塑造紀律、自信、價值觀，有時甚至會影響孩子對人生方向的理解。從這個角度來看，很多人並不會把老師看成只是站在白板前工作的僱員；他們更像是幫助年輕人在人生裡站穩腳步的引路人。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that teachers are often seen as doing far
more than simply delivering lessons. They help shape discipline, confidence,
values, and sometimes even a child’s sense of direction. In that sense, many
people do not view teachers as mere employees standing in front of a
whiteboard; they see them as guides who help young people find their footing in
life.
[主要原因是，老師很多時候被視為不只是單純地教書而已。他們同時也在塑造紀律、自
信、價值觀，有時甚至會影響孩子對人生方向的理解。從這個角度來看，很多人並不會把老
師看成只是站在白板前工作的僱員；他們更像是幫助年輕人在人生裡站穩腳步的引路人。]

components.english: For example, when a student does well, many parents do not only praise the child; they also recognise the teacher’s effort behind the scenes. Likewise, at school events or graduation ceremonies, teachers are often thanked warmly because people know their influence goes well beyond exam results.
components.chinese: 例如，當一個學生表現得好時，很多家長不只會稱讚孩子，也會看到老師在背後付出的努力。同樣地，在學校活動或畢業典禮上，老師往往也會被真心感謝，因為大家都知道，他們的影響遠遠不只在考試成績上。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, when a student does well, many parents do not only
praise the child; they also recognise the teacher’s effort behind the scenes.
Likewise, at school events or graduation ceremonies, teachers are often thanked
warmly because people know their influence goes well beyond exam results.
[例如，當一個學生表現得好時，很多家長不只會稱讚孩子，也會看到老師在背後付出的努
力。同樣地，在學校活動或畢業典禮上，老師往往也會被真心感謝，因為大家都知道，他們
的影響遠遠不只在考試成績上。]

components.english: So yes, I would say teachers are still respected to a large extent, because they are closely associated with knowledge, growth, and opportunity.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說老師在很大程度上仍然是受尊重的，因為他們和知識、成長，以及機會這些東西緊緊連在一起。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say teachers are still respected to a large extent,
because they are closely associated with knowledge, growth, and opportunity.
[所以，是的，我會說老師在很大程度上仍然是受尊重的，因為他們和知識、成長，以及機會
這些東西緊緊連在一起。]

components.english: That said, I do not think that respect is always as automatic or unquestioned as it may have been in the past.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為今天人們對老師的尊重，仍然像過去那樣自然而然，或者毫無保留。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think that respect is always as automatic or
unquestioned as it may have been in the past.
[不過，我也不認為今天人們對老師的尊重，仍然像過去那樣自然而然，或者毫無保留。]

components.english: To be honest, modern teachers often work under a great deal of pressure, and they are sometimes placed under the microscope by parents, schools, and even social media. Some people still deeply respect the profession, whereas others treat teachers more like service providers who are expected to produce results on demand. In other words, the respect is still there, but it can feel more conditional and more fragile than before.
components.chinese: 老實說，現代老師很多時候都在很大壓力下工作，而且有時還會被家長、學校，甚至社交媒體放到放大鏡下審視。有些人仍然非常尊重這份職業；但也有些人會把老師看得更像一種服務提供者，好像只要結果不夠理想，就隨時可以被質疑。換句話說，那份尊重仍然存在，只是它有時比以前更有條件，也更脆弱。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, modern teachers often work under a great deal of
pressure, and they are sometimes placed under the microscope by parents,
schools, and even social media. Some people still deeply respect the profession,
whereas others treat teachers more like service providers who are expected to
produce results on demand. In other words, the respect is still there, but it can
feel more conditional and more fragile than before.
[老實說，現代老師很多時候都在很大壓力下工作，而且有時還會被家長、學校，甚至社交
媒體放到放大鏡下審視。有些人仍然非常尊重這份職業；但也有些人會把老師看得更像一
種服務提供者，好像只要結果不夠理想，就隨時可以被質疑。換句話說，那份尊重仍然存在
，只是它有時比以前更有條件，也更脆弱。]

components.english: For instance, if students’ grades drop or a school decision causes controversy, teachers may quickly become the target of blame, even when the situation is more complicated than it looks. So while society still values teachers, it does not always make their lives easy.
components.chinese: 例如，如果學生的成績下滑，或者學校某個決定引起爭議，老師很快就可能成為被責怪的對象，即使事情其實比表面看起來複雜得多。所以，雖然社會仍然重視老師，但並不代表老師的處境就一定輕鬆。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if students’ grades drop or a school decision causes
controversy, teachers may quickly become the target of blame, even when the
situation is more complicated than it looks. So while society still values teachers,
it does not always make their lives easy.
[例如，如果學生的成績下滑，或者學校某個決定引起爭議，老師很快就可能成為被責怪的
對象，即使事情其實比表面看起來複雜得多。所以，雖然社會仍然重視老師，但並不代表老
師的處境就一定輕鬆。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say teachers in Hong Kong are still respected, but the picture is more mixed than it used to be. Society still understands the importance of teachers, but real respect should not stop at words; it should also show up in understanding, trust, and genuine appreciation for the work they do.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港的老師仍然受到尊重，只不過整體情況比以前更複雜一些。社會仍然知道老師的重要，但真正的尊重，不應只停留在口頭上，也應該體現在理解、信任，以及對他們工作的珍惜上。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say teachers in Hong Kong are still respected, but
the picture is more mixed than it used to be. Society still understands the
importance of teachers, but real respect should not stop at words; it should also
show up in understanding, trust, and genuine appreciation for the work they
do.
[總結來說，我會說香港的老師仍然受到尊重，只不過整體情況比以前更複雜一些。社會仍
然知道老師的重要，但真正的尊重，不應只停留在口頭上，也應該體現在理解、信任，以及
對他們工作的珍惜上。]

question.english: Do you think teachers get paid enough money in your country?
question.chinese: 你認為在你地方，老師的薪酬足夠嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think teachers get paid enough money in your country?
你認為在你地方，老師的薪酬足夠嗎？

components.english: I’d say not really, or at least not fully, especially when you consider how much teachers are expected to do.
components.chinese: 我會說未必算足夠，或者至少不能說是完全足夠，特別是當你把老師被要求承擔的工作量也一併考慮進去的時候。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I’d say not really, or at least not fully, especially when you consider how
much teachers are expected to do.
[我會說未必算足夠，或者至少不能說是完全足夠，特別是當你把老師被要求承擔的工作量
也一併考慮進去的時候。]

components.english: In a place like Hong Kong, teachers do far more than just stand at the front and explain lessons. They prepare materials, mark assignments, manage classroom behaviour, deal with parents, handle school administration, and often provide emotional support as well. In other words, they wear many hats. So even if the salary may look reasonable on paper, it does not always feel like a fair reflection of the pressure, responsibility, and long hours involved.
components.chinese: 在像香港這樣的地方，老師做的事情遠遠不只是站在課室前面講課而已。他們要備課、批改功課、管理課堂秩序、和家長溝通、處理學校行政工作，而且很多時還要提供情緒上的支持。換句話說，他們其實身兼多職。所以，即使薪金在紙面上看起來也許不算太差，它很多時仍然未必真正反映那種壓力、責任，以及漫長工時。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In a place like Hong Kong, teachers do far more than just stand
at the front and explain lessons. They prepare materials, mark assignments,
manage classroom behaviour, deal with parents, handle school administration,
and often provide emotional support as well. In other words, they wear many
hats. So even if the salary may look reasonable on paper, it does not always feel
like a fair reflection of the pressure, responsibility, and long hours involved.
[在像香港這樣的地方，老師做的事情遠遠不只是站在課室前面講課而已。他們要備課、批
改功課、管理課堂秩序、和家長溝通、處理學校行政工作，而且很多時還要提供情緒上的支
持。換句話說，他們其實身兼多職。所以，即使薪金在紙面上看起來也許不算太差，它很多
時仍然未必真正反映那種壓力、責任，以及漫長工時。]

components.english: For example, a secondary-school teacher may spend the whole day teaching, then go home with piles of marking, lesson planning, and messages to reply to. By the time all that is done, the work has already spilled well beyond school hours. In that kind of situation, it is easy to feel that the pay does not tell the whole story.
components.chinese: 例如，一位中學老師可能白天整天都在上課，回到家之後還要面對一大堆批改、備課，以及需要回覆的訊息。等到這些事情都處理完，工作其實早就已經延伸到放學以後很久了。在這種情況下，人很容易會覺得，薪金並沒有把整個故事說完整。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a secondary-school teacher may spend the whole day
teaching, then go home with piles of marking, lesson planning, and messages to
reply to. By the time all that is done, the work has already spilled well beyond
school hours. In that kind of situation, it is easy to feel that the pay does not tell
the whole story.
[例如，一位中學老師可能白天整天都在上課，回到家之後還要面對一大堆批改、備課，以及
需要回覆的訊息。等到這些事情都處理完，工作其實早就已經延伸到放學以後很久了。在這
種情況下，人很容易會覺得，薪金並沒有把整個故事說完整。]

components.english: So, overall, I would lean towards saying that many teachers are not paid enough for the full weight of the job they carry.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我會傾向認為，很多老師的薪酬並不足以真正反映他們肩上那份工作的重量。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, I would lean towards saying that many teachers are
not paid enough for the full weight of the job they carry.
[所以，整體來說，我會傾向認為，很多老師的薪酬並不足以真正反映他們肩上那份工作的
重量。]

components.english: That said, I do not think the picture is completely black and white, because some teachers do receive stable pay and relatively decent benefits compared with other professions.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為事情完全是非黑即白，因為有些老師的薪酬確實算穩定，而且和其他一些職業相比，福利也相對不錯。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think the picture is completely black and white,
because some teachers do receive stable pay and relatively decent benefits
compared with other professions.
[不過，我也不認為事情完全是非黑即白，因為有些老師的薪酬確實算穩定，而且和其他一
些職業相比，福利也相對不錯。]

components.english: To be honest, in some cases, especially in more established schools or in the public system, teaching can still offer a level of job security that many other people would value. So the issue is not always that teachers are badly paid in absolute terms. Rather, it is that their pay often falls short when measured against their workload, emotional labour, and the importance of their role in society.
components.chinese: 老實說，在某些情況下，特別是在一些較成熟的學校，或者公營體系之中，教學工作仍然可以提供一種很多其他職業未必有的穩定感。所以，問題不一定是老師的薪酬在絕對意義上非常差。更準確地說，是當人們把它和工作量、情緒勞動，以及他們在社會中的重要角色相比時，這份薪酬很多時仍然顯得不太相稱。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, in some cases, especially in more established
schools or in the public system, teaching can still offer a level of job security that
many other people would value. So the issue is not always that teachers are badly
paid in absolute terms. Rather, it is that their pay often falls short when
measured against their workload, emotional labour, and the importance of their
role in society.
[老實說，在某些情況下，特別是在一些較成熟的學校，或者公營體系之中，教學工作仍然可
以提供一種很多其他職業未必有的穩定感。所以，問題不一定是老師的薪酬在絕對意義上
非常差。更準確地說，是當人們把它和工作量、情緒勞動，以及他們在社會中的重要角色相
比時，這份薪酬很多時仍然顯得不太相稱。]

components.english: For instance, a teacher may earn a steady income and have a structured career path, which is certainly valuable. However, if that same teacher is constantly stretched thin by teaching duties, paperwork, and parents’ expectations, then even a decent salary can start to feel less generous than it first appears.
components.chinese: 例如，一位老師也許擁有穩定收入，以及清晰的職業發展路線，而這些當然都很有價值。不過，如果同一位老師長期被教學工作、文書行政，以及家長期望拉得很緊，那麼即使薪金本身看起來不錯，也可能慢慢讓人覺得沒有表面上那麼寬裕。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a teacher may earn a steady income and have a
structured career path, which is certainly valuable. However, if that same
teacher is constantly stretched thin by teaching duties, paperwork, and parents’
expectations, then even a decent salary can start to feel less generous than it first
appears.
[例如，一位老師也許擁有穩定收入，以及清晰的職業發展路線，而這些當然都很有價值。
不過，如果同一位老師長期被教學工作、文書行政，以及家長期望拉得很緊，那麼即使薪金
本身看起來不錯，也可能慢慢讓人覺得沒有表面上那麼寬裕。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say teachers may be paid reasonably in some cases, but many are still not paid enough for what they truly give. In the end, the issue is often not just the amount itself, but whether society is really using both pay and respect to reflect the value that teachers carry.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，有些老師的薪酬在某程度上算合理，但很多人仍然沒有因為自己真正付出的東西而得到足夠回報。說到底，問題很多時不只是金額本身，而是社會是否真的用薪酬和尊重，去回應老師所承擔的價值。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say teachers may be paid reasonably in some cases,
but many are still not paid enough for what they truly give. In the end, the issue
is often not just the amount itself, but whether society is really using both pay
and respect to reflect the value that teachers carry.
[總結來說，我會說，有些老師的薪酬在某程度上算合理，但很多人仍然沒有因為自己真正
付出的東西而得到足夠回報。說到底，問題很多時不只是金額本身，而是社會是否真的用薪
酬和尊重，去回應老師所承擔的價值。]

question.english: What role should the teacher have in the classroom?
question.chinese: 老師在課堂上應該扮演甚麼角色？

source_lines.text: Topic: What role should the teacher have in the classroom?
老師在課堂上應該扮演甚麼角色？

components.english: Personally, I think a teacher should be a guide, a leader, and a source of support, rather than just someone who stands at the front and talks for the whole lesson.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為老師在課堂上應該是引導者、領導者，以及支持學生的人，而不只是站在前面把整堂課講完的人。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think a teacher should be a guide, a leader, and a source of
support, rather than just someone who stands at the front and talks for the
whole lesson.
[就我個人而言，我認為老師在課堂上應該是引導者、領導者，以及支持學生的人，而不只
是站在前面把整堂課講完的人。]

components.english: The main reason is that good teaching is not a one-way street. A teacher should explain ideas clearly, keep the class focused, encourage students to think for themselves, and create an atmosphere where people feel comfortable asking questions. In that sense, a good teacher is a bit like a compass: they do not walk the whole journey for the students, but they help them move in the right direction.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，好的教學並不是單向輸出的過程。老師應該清楚解釋概念、讓課堂保持專注、鼓勵學生自己思考，並營造一種讓人敢發問的氣氛。從這個角度來說，一位好老師有點像指南針：他不會替學生走完整段路，但他會幫助學生朝正確方向前進。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that good teaching is not a one-way street. A
teacher should explain ideas clearly, keep the class focused, encourage students
to think for themselves, and create an atmosphere where people feel comfortable
asking questions. In that sense, a good teacher is a bit like a compass: they do
not walk the whole journey for the students, but they help them move in the
right direction.
[主要原因是，好的教學並不是單向輸出的過程。老師應該清楚解釋概念、讓課堂保持專
注、鼓勵學生自己思考，並營造一種讓人敢發問的氣氛。從這個角度來說，一位好老師有點
像指南針：他不會替學生走完整段路，但他會幫助學生朝正確方向前進。]

components.english: For example, in a discussion-based lesson, a good teacher would not simply spoon-feed students all the answers. Instead, they might ask probing questions, give helpful hints, and guide the discussion so that students can arrive at the answer with their own minds switched on. That kind of teaching usually sticks much better.
components.chinese: 例如，在一堂以討論為主的課堂裡，一位好老師不會只是把所有答案直接餵給學生。相反，他可能會提出一些有啟發性的問題、給出有幫助的提示，並引導討論，讓學生在真正動腦的情況下自己走到答案面前。這樣的教學方式，通常也更容易讓人記得住。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in a discussion-based lesson, a good teacher would not
simply spoon-feed students all the answers. Instead, they might ask probing
questions, give helpful hints, and guide the discussion so that students can
arrive at the answer with their own minds switched on. That kind of teaching
usually sticks much better.
[例如，在一堂以討論為主的課堂裡，一位好老師不會只是把所有答案直接餵給學生。相反
，他可能會提出一些有啟發性的問題、給出有幫助的提示，並引導討論，讓學生在真正動腦
的情況下自己走到答案面前。這樣的教學方式，通常也更容易讓人記得住。]

components.english: So, if you ask me, the teacher’s role should be to lead learning without dominating it, and to help students become active learners rather than passive listeners.
components.chinese: 所以，如果你問我，我會說老師的角色應該是在不壓過學生的情況下帶領學習，並幫助學生成為主動的學習者，而不是被動的聆聽者。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if you ask me, the teacher’s role should be to lead learning
without dominating it, and to help students become active learners rather than
passive listeners.
[所以，如果你問我，我會說老師的角色應該是在不壓過學生的情況下帶領學習，並幫助學
生成為主動的學習者，而不是被動的聆聽者。]

components.english: That said, I also think a teacher needs to have real authority in the classroom, because support without structure can quickly fall apart.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為老師在課堂上必須有真正的權威，因為只有支持而沒有結構，事情很快就會散掉。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I also think a teacher needs to have real authority in the
classroom, because support without structure can quickly fall apart.
[不過，我也認為老師在課堂上必須有真正的權威，因為只有支持而沒有結構，事情很快就
會散掉。]

components.english: A classroom cannot run well if the teacher is only a friendly presence and never sets standards. Students need encouragement, but they also need boundaries, discipline, and honest feedback. In other words, a teacher should not be a dictator, but nor should they fade into the background. They need to hold the room together and keep learning on track.
components.chinese: 如果老師只是很友善地陪伴，卻從來不設下標準，那課堂其實很難運作得好。學生需要鼓勵，但同時也需要界線、紀律，以及誠實的回饋。換句話說，老師不應該是個獨裁者，但也不應該退到背景裡變得毫無存在感。他必須把整個課堂穩住，並讓學習保持在正軌上。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: A classroom cannot run well if the teacher is only a friendly
presence and never sets standards. Students need encouragement, but they also
need boundaries, discipline, and honest feedback. In other words, a teacher
should not be a dictator, but nor should they fade into the background. They
need to hold the room together and keep learning on track.
[如果老師只是很友善地陪伴，卻從來不設下標準，那課堂其實很難運作得好。學生需要鼓
勵，但同時也需要界線、紀律，以及誠實的回饋。換句話說，老師不應該是個獨裁者，但也不
應該退到背景裡變得毫無存在感。他必須把整個課堂穩住，並讓學習保持在正軌上。]

components.english: For instance, if students are doing group work and the discussion starts drifting off-topic, a good teacher should step in, refocus the class, and remind them of the goal. Likewise, if deadlines are missed or behaviour becomes disruptive, the teacher has to deal with it firmly but fairly.
components.chinese: 例如，如果學生在做小組活動時，討論開始偏離主題，一位好老師就應該適時介入，把大家重新拉回重點，並提醒他們這堂課的目標。同樣地，如果有人錯過截止日期，或者課堂秩序開始受干擾，老師也必須堅定但公平地處理。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if students are doing group work and the discussion
starts drifting off-topic, a good teacher should step in, refocus the class, and
remind them of the goal. Likewise, if deadlines are missed or behaviour becomes
disruptive, the teacher has to deal with it firmly but fairly.
[例如，如果學生在做小組活動時，討論開始偏離主題，一位好老師就應該適時介入，把大
家重新拉回重點，並提醒他們這堂課的目標。同樣地，如果有人錯過截止日期，或者課堂秩
序開始受干擾，老師也必須堅定但公平地處理。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say a teacher should be a guide, an organiser, and a steady presence in the classroom. In the end, the best teachers do not simply fill students’ heads with information; they bring out the best in them while still keeping the class grounded, focused, and moving forward.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說老師在課堂上應該是引導者、組織者，以及一種穩定的存在。說到底，最好的老師不只是把資訊塞進學生腦袋裡；他們更會在讓學生發揮最好一面的同時，仍然把整個課堂穩穩地放在正軌上，專注地向前推進。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say a teacher should be a guide, an organiser, and a
steady presence in the classroom. In the end, the best teachers do not simply fill
students’ heads with information; they bring out the best in them while still
keeping the class grounded, focused, and moving forward.
[總結來說，我會說老師在課堂上應該是引導者、組織者，以及一種穩定的存在。說到底，最
好的老師不只是把資訊塞進學生腦袋裡；他們更會在讓學生發揮最好一面的同時，仍然把
整個課堂穩穩地放在正軌上，專注地向前推進。]

question.english: In your opinion, what are the most important qualities for a good teacher to have?
question.chinese: 在你看來，一位好老師最重要應具備哪些特質？

source_lines.text: Topic: In your opinion, what are the most important qualities for a good
teacher to have?
在你看來，一位好老師最重要應具備哪些特質？

components.english: In my view, the two most important qualities are the ability to explain things clearly and the patience to guide different kinds of students.
components.chinese: 在我看來，最重要的兩個特質，是把事情講清楚的能力，以及有耐性去引導不同類型學生的能力。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In my view, the two most important qualities are the ability to explain
things clearly and the patience to guide different kinds of students.
[在我看來，最重要的兩個特質，是把事情講清楚的能力，以及有耐性去引導不同類型學生
的能力。]

components.english: A teacher may know their subject inside out, but if they cannot explain it in a simple and engaging way, that knowledge stays locked up instead of reaching students. A really good teacher knows how to break difficult ideas into smaller pieces, use examples that make sense, and adjust their language to the level of the class. In that sense, teaching is not just about knowing; it is about making other people able to know.
components.chinese: 一位老師也許對自己的科目非常熟悉，但如果他不能用簡單而吸引人的方式講出來，那些知識很多時就像被鎖住一樣，進不到學生心裡。真正好的老師懂得把困難的概念拆細、用學生聽得明的例子去解釋，並且因應班上程度去調整自己的表達。從這個角度來看，教學不只是「自己懂」，而是「讓別人也能懂」。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: A teacher may know their subject inside out, but if they cannot
explain it in a simple and engaging way, that knowledge stays locked up instead
of reaching students. A really good teacher knows how to break difficult ideas
into smaller pieces, use examples that make sense, and adjust their language to
the level of the class. In that sense, teaching is not just about knowing; it is
about making other people able to know.
[一位老師也許對自己的科目非常熟悉，但如果他不能用簡單而吸引人的方式講出來，那些
知識很多時就像被鎖住一樣，進不到學生心裡。真正好的老師懂得把困難的概念拆細、用學
生聽得明的例子去解釋，並且因應班上程度去調整自己的表達。從這個角度來看，教學不只
是「自己懂」，而是「讓別人也能懂」。]

components.english: For example, a good maths teacher does not just repeat the formula louder and louder. They may draw it, compare it to real life, or explain it in a second or third way until the penny finally drops. When that happens, the student does not just learn the answer; they feel that learning is possible.
components.chinese: 例如，一位好的數學老師不會只是把公式愈講愈大聲地重複。他可能會畫圖、拿現實生活來比喻，或者換第二種、第三種方法去解釋，直到學生突然明白為止。當那一刻真的出現時，學生不只是學會了答案，同時也會感受到：原來自己是學得到的。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a good maths teacher does not just repeat the formula
louder and louder. They may draw it, compare it to real life, or explain it in a
second or third way until the penny finally drops. When that happens, the
student does not just learn the answer; they feel that learning is possible.
[例如，一位好的數學老師不會只是把公式愈講愈大聲地重複。他可能會畫圖、拿現實生活
來比喻，或者換第二種、第三種方法去解釋，直到學生突然明白為止。當那一刻真的出現時
，學生不只是學會了答案，同時也會感受到：原來自己是學得到的。]

components.english: So, for me, clarity matters enormously, because a teacher who can explain well can turn confusion into confidence.
components.chinese: 所以，對我來說，清晰的講解能力非常重要，因為一位能把內容講得好的人，真的可以把迷惘變成自信。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, for me, clarity matters enormously, because a teacher who can
explain well can turn confusion into confidence.
[所以，對我來說，清晰的講解能力非常重要，因為一位能把內容講得好的人，真的可以把
迷惘變成自信。]

components.english: That said, clear explanation alone is not enough; a good teacher also needs patience, empathy, and a fair amount of emotional intelligence.
components.chinese: 不過，單靠講得清楚仍然不夠；一位好老師同時也需要耐性、同理心，以及相當程度的情緒智慧。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea: That said, clear explanation alone is not enough; a good teacher also
needs patience, empathy, and a fair amount of emotional intelligence.
[不過，單靠講得清楚仍然不夠；一位好老師同時也需要耐性、同理心，以及相當程度的情
緒智慧。]

components.english: Students do not all learn at the same speed, and they certainly do not all struggle in the same way. Some need encouragement, some need structure, and some simply need a little more time to find their footing. A good teacher does not make students feel small for not understanding. Instead, they stay calm, keep standards high, and give students room to grow. In other words, they are kind without being soft, and firm without being cold.
components.chinese: 學生的學習速度並不一樣，而他們遇到困難的方式也絕對不會完全相同。有些人需要鼓勵，有些人需要規範，而有些人其實只是需要多一點時間去站穩腳步。一位好老師不會因為學生一時不明白，就讓他們覺得自己很差。相反，他會保持冷靜、維持要求，同時也給學生留下成長的空間。換句話說，他是有善意而不放縱，有原則而不冷漠。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Students do not all learn at the same speed, and they certainly
do not all struggle in the same way. Some need encouragement, some need
structure, and some simply need a little more time to find their footing. A good
teacher does not make students feel small for not understanding. Instead, they
stay calm, keep standards high, and give students room to grow. In other words,
they are kind without being soft, and firm without being cold.
[學生的學習速度並不一樣，而他們遇到困難的方式也絕對不會完全相同。有些人需要鼓勵
，有些人需要規範，而有些人其實只是需要多一點時間去站穩腳步。一位好老師不會因為學
生一時不明白，就讓他們覺得自己很差。相反，他會保持冷靜、維持要求，同時也給學生留
下成長的空間。換句話說，他是有善意而不放縱，有原則而不冷漠。]

components.english: For instance, if a student keeps making mistakes in class, a poor teacher may embarrass them and shut them down. A good teacher, by contrast, will correct the mistake, encourage the effort, and help the student try again without losing heart. That kind of response can shape a student’s attitude to learning for years.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個學生在課堂上一再犯錯，差的老師也許會令他難堪，甚至直接把他的信心關掉。相比之下，一位好老師會糾正錯誤、肯定努力，並且幫助學生在不灰心的情況下再試一次。這種回應方式，很多時候會長遠地影響一個學生對學習的態度。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a student keeps making mistakes in class, a poor
teacher may embarrass them and shut them down. A good teacher, by contrast,
will correct the mistake, encourage the effort, and help the student try again
without losing heart. That kind of response can shape a student’s attitude to
learning for years.
[例如，如果一個學生在課堂上一再犯錯，差的老師也許會令他難堪，甚至直接把他的信心
關掉。相比之下，一位好老師會糾正錯誤、肯定努力，並且幫助學生在不灰心的情況下再試
一次。這種回應方式，很多時候會長遠地影響一個學生對學習的態度。]

components.english: Overall, I would say a good teacher should be clear, patient, and genuinely invested in students’ growth. Subject knowledge is important, of course, but what really sets a great teacher apart is the ability to reach students’ minds without losing their hearts.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說一位好老師應該講解清楚、有耐性，而且真心關心學生的成長。學科知識當然重要，但真正把好老師和普通老師分開的，往往是那種既能走進學生腦袋，也不會失去學生內心的能力。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I would say a good teacher should be clear, patient, and
genuinely invested in students’ growth. Subject knowledge is important, of
course, but what really sets a great teacher apart is the ability to reach students’
minds without losing their hearts.
[總結來說，我會說一位好老師應該講解清楚、有耐性，而且真心關心學生的成長。學科知
識當然重要，但真正把好老師和普通老師分開的，往往是那種既能走進學生腦袋，也不會失
去學生內心的能力。]

question.english: In your opinion, how can a teacher make lessons for children more interesting?
question.chinese: 你認為老師可以怎樣令孩子的課堂變得更有趣？

source_lines.text: In your opinion, how can a teacher make lessons for children more interesting?
你認為老師可以怎樣令孩子的課堂變得更有趣？

components.english: I think the most effective way is to make lessons more interactive and more connected to children’s real lives, rather than just talking at them from the front of the classroom.
components.chinese: 我認為，最有效的方法就是令課堂更有互動性，並且和孩子的真實生活連結起來，而不是老師單方面站在課室前面不停講。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the most effective way is to make lessons more interactive and
more connected to children’s real lives, rather than just talking at them from the
front of the classroom.
[我認為，最有效的方法就是令課堂更有互動性，並且和孩子的真實生活連結起來，而不是
老師單方面站在課室前面不停講。]

components.english: Children usually learn best when they are involved, not when they are treated like empty cups waiting to be filled. If a lesson only consists of listening and copying, their attention can drift very quickly. By contrast, if they are invited to ask questions, share ideas, move around, solve problems, or try things out for themselves, the class comes alive. In that sense, interest often begins the moment learning stops feeling like a monologue and starts feeling like an experience.
components.chinese: 孩子很多時候在真正參與的時候，學得最好，而不是像一個空杯子那樣被動地等人倒進內容。如果一堂課只是聽、抄、記，他們的注意力往往很快就會飄走。相反，如果老師邀請他們發問、分享想法、走動、解難，或者親自試一試，整個課堂就會活起來。從這個角度來看，興趣很多時候就是從學習不再像獨白，而開始像一種體驗的那一刻開始的。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Children usually learn best when they are involved, not when
they are treated like empty cups waiting to be filled. If a lesson only consists of
listening and copying, their attention can drift very quickly. By contrast, if they
are invited to ask questions, share ideas, move around, solve problems, or try
things out for themselves, the class comes alive. In that sense, interest often
begins the moment learning stops feeling like a monologue and starts feeling
like an experience.
[孩子很多時候在真正參與的時候，學得最好，而不是像一個空杯子那樣被動地等人倒進內
容。如果一堂課只是聽、抄、記，他們的注意力往往很快就會飄走。相反，如果老師邀請他們
發問、分享想法、走動、解難，或者親自試一試，整個課堂就會活起來。從這個角度來看，興
趣很多時候就是從學習不再像獨白，而開始像一種體驗的那一刻開始的。]

components.english: For example, in a science lesson, instead of only explaining a concept on the board, a teacher could let children do a simple experiment themselves. In a language class, the teacher could use role-play, games, or short group activities rather than just drilling vocabulary. Once children can see, touch, or use what they are learning, the idea usually sticks much better.
components.chinese: 例如，在科學課裡，老師與其只是在黑板上解釋概念，不如讓孩子親手做一個簡單實驗。在語文課裡，老師也可以用角色扮演、遊戲，或者短小的小組活動，而不只是反覆操練詞彙。當孩子可以看見、摸到，或者真正用到自己學的東西時，那個概念通常就會記得更牢。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in a science lesson, instead of only explaining a concept
on the board, a teacher could let children do a simple experiment themselves. In
a language class, the teacher could use role-play, games, or short group activities
rather than just drilling vocabulary. Once children can see, touch, or use what
they are learning, the idea usually sticks much better.
[例如，在科學課裡，老師與其只是在黑板上解釋概念，不如讓孩子親手做一個簡單實驗。
在語文課裡，老師也可以用角色扮演、遊戲，或者短小的小組活動，而不只是反覆操練詞
彙。當孩子可以看見、摸到，或者真正用到自己學的東西時，那個概念通常就會記得更牢。]

components.english: So, to me, one key to making lessons interesting is to let children take part in the learning instead of just sitting there while learning happens around them.
components.chinese: 所以，對我來說，令課堂有趣的一個關鍵，就是讓孩子真正參與學習，而不是只是坐在那裡，看著學習在自己身邊發生。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to me, one key to making lessons interesting is to let children
take part in the learning instead of just sitting there while learning happens
around them.
[所以，對我來說，令課堂有趣的一個關鍵，就是讓孩子真正參與學習，而不是只是坐在那
裡，看著學習在自己身邊發生。]

components.english: On top of that, I think teachers need variety and a sense of energy, because even a good subject can go flat if it is taught in the same way every time.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為，老師需要有變化，也需要有一種帶動氣氛的能量，因為即使是一個本來很好的科目，如果每一次都用同一種方法去教，也很容易變得平淡。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think teachers need variety and a sense of energy,
because even a good subject can go flat if it is taught in the same way every time.
[除此之外，我也認為，老師需要有變化，也需要有一種帶動氣氛的能量，因為即使是一個
本來很好的科目，如果每一次都用同一種方法去教，也很容易變得平淡。]

components.english: Children get bored when everything starts to feel predictable. If every lesson follows the same script, even strong content can lose its spark. That is why it helps when teachers mix things up with stories, pictures, humour, group work, competitions, or real-life examples. Just as importantly, children are usually more engaged when they feel safe, noticed, and encouraged. A warm teacher with genuine enthusiasm can light up a room faster than any worksheet can.
components.chinese: 當所有事情都變得太可預測時，孩子就很容易感到悶。如果每一堂課都照著同一個劇本走，再好的內容也可能慢慢失去火花。所以，老師若能加入故事、圖片、幽默、小組活動、比賽，或者生活化例子，往往就很有幫助。同樣重要的是，當孩子感到安全、被看見、被鼓勵時，他們通常也會投入得多。一位真正有熱情、也有溫度的老師，很多時比任何一張工作紙都更快令整間課室亮起來。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Children get bored when everything starts to feel predictable. If
every lesson follows the same script, even strong content can lose its spark. That
is why it helps when teachers mix things up with stories, pictures, humour,
group work, competitions, or real-life examples. Just as importantly, children
are usually more engaged when they feel safe, noticed, and encouraged. A warm
teacher with genuine enthusiasm can light up a room faster than any worksheet
can.
[當所有事情都變得太可預測時，孩子就很容易感到悶。如果每一堂課都照著同一個劇本走
，再好的內容也可能慢慢失去火花。所以，老師若能加入故事、圖片、幽默、小組活動、比賽
，或者生活化例子，往往就很有幫助。同樣重要的是，當孩子感到安全、被看見、被鼓勵時，
他們通常也會投入得多。一位真正有熱情、也有溫度的老師，很多時比任何一張工作紙都更
快令整間課室亮起來。]

components.english: For instance, a history teacher could turn a lesson into a story or even a small mystery for students to solve, while a maths teacher could turn numbers into a shopping game or a team challenge.
components.chinese: 例如，一位歷史老師可以把課堂變成一個故事，甚至一個讓學生一起破解的小謎團；而一位數學老師則可以把數字變成購物遊戲，或者小組挑戰。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a history teacher could turn a lesson into a story or
even a small mystery for students to solve, while a maths teacher could turn
numbers into a shopping game or a team challenge. In both cases, the subject
stops feeling dry and starts feeling like something children can step into.
[例如，一位歷史老師可以把課堂變成一個故事，甚至一個讓學生一起破解的小謎團；而一

components.english: In both cases, the subject stops feeling dry and starts feeling like something children can step into.
components.chinese: 在這兩種情況下，科目就不再只是乾巴巴的內容，而開始變成孩子可以走進去的東西。

source_lines.text: 位數學老師則可以把數字變成購物遊戲，或者小組挑戰。在這兩種情況下，科目就不再只是
乾巴巴的內容，而開始變成孩子可以走進去的東西。]

question.english: Do you think computers will one day replace teachers in the classroom?
question.chinese: 你認為將來電腦有一天會在課堂上取代老師嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think computers will one day replace teachers in the classroom?
[你認為將來電腦有一天會在課堂上取代老師嗎？]

components.english: Personally, no, I do not think computers will fully replace teachers in the classroom, although they may take over some parts of teaching.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，不，我不認為電腦會在課堂上完全取代老師，雖然它們的確可能會接手教學中的某些部分。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, no, I do not think computers will fully replace teachers in
the classroom, although they may take over some parts of teaching.
[就我個人而言，不，我不認為電腦會在課堂上完全取代老師，雖然它們的確可能會接手教
學中的某些部分。]

components.english: The main reason is that teaching is not just about passing on information. A good teacher reads the room, notices confusion, senses boredom, encourages shy students, and changes their approach when something is not working. A computer can deliver content, but a teacher gives that content direction, warmth, and a human face. In that sense, a teacher is not just a source of knowledge, but a guide through it.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，教學並不只是把資訊傳遞出去而已。一位好的老師會看懂整個課堂的氣氛、留意到學生的困惑、察覺到沉悶感、鼓勵害羞的學生，並且在某種方法行不通時即時改變做法。電腦可以傳送內容，但老師會替那些內容加上方向、溫度，以及人的面貌。從這個角度來說，老師不只是知識的來源，更是帶領學生走過知識的人。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that teaching is not just about passing on
information. A good teacher reads the room, notices confusion, senses boredom,
encourages shy students, and changes their approach when something is not
working. A computer can deliver content, but a teacher gives that content
direction, warmth, and a human face. In that sense, a teacher is not just a source
of knowledge, but a guide through it.
[主要原因是，教學並不只是把資訊傳遞出去而已。一位好的老師會看懂整個課堂的氣氛、
留意到學生的困惑、察覺到沉悶感、鼓勵害羞的學生，並且在某種方法行不通時即時改變做
法。電腦可以傳送內容，但老師會替那些內容加上方向、溫度，以及人的面貌。從這個角度
來說，老師不只是知識的來源，更是帶領學生走過知識的人。]

components.english: For example, if a student looks lost during a lesson, a teacher may pause, explain the idea in simpler words, or use a completely different example on the spot. If a child is anxious, distracted, or lacking confidence, a teacher may notice that too. A machine may give the right answer, but it cannot always reach the student behind the question.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個學生在課堂上看起來一臉茫然，老師可能會立刻停下來，用更簡單的方式重新解釋，或者當場換一個完全不同的例子。如果一個孩子焦慮、分心，或者缺乏自信，老師也可能察覺得到。機器也許能給出正確答案，但它未必總能觸及那個提問背後真正的學生。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a student looks lost during a lesson, a teacher may
pause, explain the idea in simpler words, or use a completely different example
on the spot. If a child is anxious, distracted, or lacking confidence, a teacher may
notice that too. A machine may give the right answer, but it cannot always reach
the student behind the question.
[例如，如果一個學生在課堂上看起來一臉茫然，老師可能會立刻停下來，用更簡單的方式
重新解釋，或者當場換一個完全不同的例子。如果一個孩子焦慮、分心，或者缺乏自信，老
師也可能察覺得到。機器也許能給出正確答案，但它未必總能觸及那個提問背後真正的學

components.english: So, overall, I would say computers may become excellent teaching tools, but they are unlikely to replace teachers completely.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說電腦也許會成為非常出色的教學工具，但它們不太可能完全取代老師。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, I would say computers may become excellent teaching
tools, but they are unlikely to replace teachers completely.
[總結來說，我會說電腦也許會成為非常出色的教學工具，但它們不太可能完全取代老師。]

components.english: That said, I do think computers will play a much bigger role in the classroom in the future, especially in areas like practice, revision, and personalised learning.
components.chinese: 不過，我的確認為，未來電腦在課堂上的角色會大得多，尤其是在操練、溫習，以及個人化學習這些方面。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do think computers will play a much bigger role in the
classroom in the future, especially in areas like practice, revision, and
personalised learning.
[不過，我的確認為，未來電腦在課堂上的角色會大得多，尤其是在操練、溫習，以及個人化
學習這些方面。]

components.english: Computers are very good at speed, repetition, and adaptation. They can give instant feedback, provide extra exercises, track progress, and adjust the level to suit different students. In that sense, they can take a lot of weight off teachers’ shoulders and make learning more efficient. So the future may not be teacher or computer, but teacher and computer working hand in hand.
components.chinese: 電腦在速度、重複操練，以及調整內容方面其實非常強。它們可以即時提供回饋、給予額外練習、追蹤進度，並且按不同學生的程度調整難度。從這個角度來說，它們的確可以替老師分擔不少負擔，也讓學習變得更有效率。所以，未來與其說是老師或電腦二選一，不如說更像是老師和電腦並肩合作。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Computers are very good at speed, repetition, and adaptation.
They can give instant feedback, provide extra exercises, track progress, and
adjust the level to suit different students. In that sense, they can take a lot of
weight off teachers’ shoulders and make learning more efficient. So the future
may not be teacher or computer, but teacher and computer working hand in
hand.
[電腦在速度、重複操練，以及調整內容方面其實非常強。它們可以即時提供回饋、給予額
外練習、追蹤進度，並且按不同學生的程度調整難度。從這個角度來說，它們的確可以替老
師分擔不少負擔，也讓學習變得更有效率。所以，未來與其說是老師或電腦二選一，不如說
更像是老師和電腦並肩合作。]

components.english: For instance, computers can be very useful for language drills, maths practice, pronunciation exercises, or reviewing material at home. A student may learn the basic content through software, but then rely on the teacher to ask deeper questions, clear up misunderstandings, and connect learning to real life.
components.chinese: 例如，電腦在語言操練、數學練習、發音訓練，或者在家重溫課堂內容方面，都可以很有幫助。學生也許可以先透過軟件學會基本內容，但之後仍然要依靠老師去提出更深入的問題、澄清誤解，並把學習和真實生活連結起來。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, computers can be very useful for language drills,
maths practice, pronunciation exercises, or reviewing material at home. A
student may learn the basic content through software, but then rely on the
teacher to ask deeper questions, clear up misunderstandings, and connect
learning to real life.
[例如，電腦在語言操練、數學練習、發音訓練，或者在家重溫課堂內容方面，都可以很有幫
助。學生也許可以先透過軟件學會基本內容，但之後仍然要依靠老師去提出更深入的問題、
澄清誤解，並把學習和真實生活連結起來。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say computers will almost certainly transform the classroom, but not replace the teacher at its heart. The best education is likely to come not from cold efficiency alone, but from technology guided by human judgment.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說電腦幾乎肯定會改變課堂的樣貌，但不會取代站在課堂核心位置的老師。最好的教育，很可能不會只來自冷冰冰的效率，而是來自由人的判斷所引導的科技。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say computers will almost certainly transform the
classroom, but not replace the teacher at its heart. The best education is likely to
come not from cold efficiency alone, but from technology guided by human
judgment.
[總結來說，我會說電腦幾乎肯定會改變課堂的樣貌，但不會取代站在課堂核心位置的老
師。最好的教育，很可能不會只來自冷冰冰的效率，而是來自由人的判斷所引導的科技。]

question.english: What’s the best invention in the past hundred years?
question.chinese: 在過去一百年裡，最好的發明是甚麼？

source_lines.text: Topic: What’s the best invention in the past hundred years?
在過去一百年裡，最好的發明是甚麼？

components.english: Personally, I’d say the internet is probably the best invention in the past hundred years.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我會說，過去一百年裡最好的發明大概就是互聯網。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I’d say the internet is probably the best invention in the past
hundred years.
[就我個人而言，我會說，過去一百年裡最好的發明大概就是互聯網。]

components.english: The main reason is that it has changed almost every part of modern life. It has transformed the way people communicate, learn, work, shop, and even think about distance. In the past, information moved at a much slower pace, and staying in touch with people far away took real effort. Now, the world feels much smaller, almost as if whole continents have been brought within arm’s reach. To me, that kind of impact is hard to beat.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，它幾乎改變了現代生活的每一個部分。它徹底改變了人們溝通、學習、工作、購物，甚至理解距離的方式。以前，資訊流動得慢得多，而要和遠方的人保持聯絡，也往往要花上不少力氣。現在，世界感覺小了很多，幾乎像是整個地球都被拉到伸手可及的範圍之內。對我來說，這種影響力真的很難被超越。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that it has changed almost every part of
modern life. It has transformed the way people communicate, learn, work, shop,
and even think about distance. In the past, information moved at a much slower
pace, and staying in touch with people far away took real effort. Now, the world
feels much smaller, almost as if whole continents have been brought within
arm’s reach. To me, that kind of impact is hard to beat.
[主要原因是，它幾乎改變了現代生活的每一個部分。它徹底改變了人們溝通、學習、工作、
購物，甚至理解距離的方式。以前，資訊流動得慢得多，而要和遠方的人保持聯絡，也往往
要花上不少力氣。現在，世界感覺小了很多，幾乎像是整個地球都被拉到伸手可及的範圍之
內。對我來說，這種影響力真的很難被超越。]

components.english: For example, with the internet, a student can learn from online courses, a worker can attend meetings from home, and family members living in different countries can still talk face to face on a screen. It has opened doors to knowledge and connection in a way that would have sounded almost magical not that long ago.
components.chinese: 例如，有了互聯網，學生可以透過網上課程學習，上班族可以在家開會，而住在不同國家的家人也仍然可以透過螢幕面對面聊天。它為知識和人與人之間的連結打開了大門，而這種事放在不算太久以前，幾乎聽起來都像魔法一樣。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, with the internet, a student can learn from online
courses, a worker can attend meetings from home, and family members living in
different countries can still talk face to face on a screen. It has opened doors to
knowledge and connection in a way that would have sounded almost magical
not that long ago.
[例如，有了互聯網，學生可以透過網上課程學習，上班族可以在家開會，而住在不同國家
的家人也仍然可以透過螢幕面對面聊天。它為知識和人與人之間的連結打開了大門，而這
種事放在不算太久以前，幾乎聽起來都像魔法一樣。]

components.english: So, if you ask me for one invention that has had the widest impact, I would definitely go with the internet.
components.chinese: 所以，如果你問我哪一項發明帶來了最廣泛的影響，我一定會選互聯網。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if you ask me for one invention that has had the widest
impact, I would definitely go with the internet.
[所以，如果你問我哪一項發明帶來了最廣泛的影響，我一定會選互聯網。]

components.english: That said, I can also see why some people would argue that medical inventions deserve that title even more.
components.chinese: 不過，我也明白，為甚麼有些人會認為醫學上的發明其實更配得上這個位置。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I can also see why some people would argue that medical
inventions deserve that title even more.
[不過，我也明白，為甚麼有些人會認為醫學上的發明其實更配得上這個位置。]

components.english: If you judge “the best” by how many lives have been protected or improved, then things like vaccines, antibiotics, or medical imaging make an incredibly strong case. The internet has changed the rhythm of life, but medical inventions have changed the odds of life itself. In other words, one has made life more connected, while the other has made life more survivable.
components.chinese: 如果你是以「拯救了多少生命」或者「改善了多少人的健康」來判斷「最好」這件事，那麼像疫苗、抗生素，或者醫學影像這些發明，其實都非常有說服力。互聯網改變了生活的節奏，但醫學發明改變的，卻是生命本身的機會。換句話說，前者讓生活更有連結，後者則讓生命更有可能延續。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: If you judge “the best” by how many lives have been protected
or improved, then things like vaccines, antibiotics, or medical imaging make an
incredibly strong case. The internet has changed the rhythm of life, but medical
inventions have changed the odds of life itself. In other words, one has made life
more connected, while the other has made life more survivable.
[如果你是以「拯救了多少生命」或者「改善了多少人的健康」來判斷「最好」這件事，那麼像
疫苗、抗生素，或者醫學影像這些發明，其實都非常有說服力。互聯網改變了生活的節奏，
但醫學發明改變的，卻是生命本身的機會。換句話說，前者讓生活更有連結，後者則讓生命
更有可能延續。]

components.english: For instance, someone can live without social media or online shopping, but they may not live without proper medicine or treatment. That is why I think the answer depends a little on what standard you use.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人沒有社交媒體、沒有網上購物，仍然可以生活；但如果沒有合適的藥物或治療，他卻未必能活得下去。正因如此，我覺得答案其實多少也取決於你用甚麼標準去衡量。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone can live without social media or online
shopping, but they may not live without proper medicine or treatment. That is
why I think the answer depends a little on what standard you use.
[例如，一個人沒有社交媒體、沒有網上購物，仍然可以生活；但如果沒有合適的藥物或治
療，他卻未必能活得下去。正因如此，我覺得答案其實多少也取決於你用甚麼標準去衡量。]

components.english: Overall, I’d still say the internet is the best invention of the past hundred years because of how deeply it has reshaped everyday life. But if the question is really about saving lives, then medical inventions may well take the crown.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我仍然會說互聯網是過去一百年裡最好的發明，因為它對日常生活的重塑實在太深了。不過，如果這道問題更重點在於拯救生命，那麼醫學發明也很可能才是真正的冠軍。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d still say the internet is the best invention of the past
hundred years because of how deeply it has reshaped everyday life. But if the
question is really about saving lives, then medical inventions may well take the
crown.
[總結來說，我仍然會說互聯網是過去一百年裡最好的發明，因為它對日常生活的重塑實在
太深了。不過，如果這道問題更重點在於拯救生命，那麼醫學發明也很可能才是真正的冠

question.english: What’s the influence of science on human life?
question.chinese: 科學對人類生活有甚麼影響？

source_lines.text: Topic: What’s the influence of science on human life?
科學對人類生活有甚麼影響？

components.english: I think the influence of science on human life has been enormous, and overall it has changed life for the better.
components.chinese: 我認為，科學對人類生活的影響是非常巨大的，而且整體來說，它的確令生活變得更好。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the influence of science on human life has been enormous, and
overall it has changed life for the better.
[我認為，科學對人類生活的影響是非常巨大的，而且整體來說，它的確令生活變得更好。]

components.english: The main reason is that science has transformed the way people live, work, travel, communicate, and survive. It has made life longer through medicine, easier through technology, and more comfortable through modern systems such as clean water, electricity, and transport. In a way, science has changed the texture of everyday life so completely that people often stop noticing it. It is not just a subject in a laboratory; it is part of the backbone of modern life.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，科學徹底改變了人們生活、工作、出行、溝通，甚至生存的方式。它透過醫學延長了壽命，透過科技令生活更方便，也透過食水、電力和交通等現代系統令生活更舒適。某程度上，科學已經把日常生活的質感改變得如此徹底，以致人們很多時候反而不再察覺它的存在。它不只是實驗室裡的一門學科，而是現代生活的骨幹之一。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that science has transformed the way people
live, work, travel, communicate, and survive. It has made life longer through
medicine, easier through technology, and more comfortable through modern
systems such as clean water, electricity, and transport. In a way, science has
changed the texture of everyday life so completely that people often stop
noticing it. It is not just a subject in a laboratory; it is part of the backbone of
modern life.
[主要原因是，科學徹底改變了人們生活、工作、出行、溝通，甚至生存的方式。它透過醫學
延長了壽命，透過科技令生活更方便，也透過食水、電力和交通等現代系統令生活更舒適。
某程度上，科學已經把日常生活的質感改變得如此徹底，以致人們很多時候反而不再察覺
它的存在。它不只是實驗室裡的一門學科，而是現代生活的骨幹之一。]

components.english: For example, vaccines, antibiotics, smartphones, and even simple household appliances have all made an enormous difference. In the past, people had to live with risks, delays, and discomfort that many of us now barely think about. Science has, quite literally, brought light into homes and added years to lives.
components.chinese: 例如，疫苗、抗生素、智能手機，甚至一些簡單的家電，都對人類生活帶來了極大的改變。在過去，人們必須面對很多今天的我們幾乎不會再特別去想的風險、延誤和不便。科學可以說是真真正正把光帶進了家庭，也把更多歲月加進了人生。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, vaccines, antibiotics, smartphones, and even simple
household appliances have all made an enormous difference. In the past, people
had to live with risks, delays, and discomfort that many of us now barely think
about. Science has, quite literally, brought light into homes and added years to
lives.
[例如，疫苗、抗生素、智能手機，甚至一些簡單的家電，都對人類生活帶來了極大的改變。
在過去，人們必須面對很多今天的我們幾乎不會再特別去想的風險、延誤和不便。科學可以
說是真真正正把光帶進了家庭，也把更多歲月加進了人生。]

components.english: So, if I had to sum it up, I’d say science has made human life safer, longer, and more connected.
components.chinese: 所以，如果要我總結，我會說科學令人類生活變得更安全、更長壽，也更緊密相連。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to sum it up, I’d say science has made human life safer,
longer, and more connected.
[所以，如果要我總結，我會說科學令人類生活變得更安全、更長壽，也更緊密相連。]

components.english: That said, I do not think the influence of science is purely positive, because it also gives people more power, and power can be used badly.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為科學的影響是完全正面的，因為它同時也給了人更多力量，而力量本身是可以被錯誤使用的。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think the influence of science is purely positive,
because it also gives people more power, and power can be used badly.
[不過，我也不認為科學的影響是完全正面的，因為它同時也給了人更多力量，而力量本身
是可以被錯誤使用的。]

components.english: To be honest, science is a tool, not a conscience. It can cure disease, but it can also produce weapons. It can connect people, but it can also make them overdependent on screens and systems. In other words, science can open doors, but it cannot decide on its own which doors should be opened. That part still depends on human judgment, ethics, and restraint.
components.chinese: 老實說，科學是一種工具，而不是良知本身。它可以治病，但也可以製造武器；它可以令人與人之間更有連結，但也可能令人過度依賴螢幕和系統。換句話說，科學可以打開很多門，但它自己並不能決定哪些門應該被打開。那部分最終仍然取決於人的判斷、倫理，以及克制。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, science is a tool, not a conscience. It can cure
disease, but it can also produce weapons. It can connect people, but it can also
make them overdependent on screens and systems. In other words, science can
open doors, but it cannot decide on its own which doors should be opened. That
part still depends on human judgment, ethics, and restraint.
[老實說，科學是一種工具，而不是良知本身。它可以治病，但也可以製造武器；它可以令人
與人之間更有連結，但也可能令人過度依賴螢幕和系統。換句話說，科學可以打開很多門，
但它自己並不能決定哪些門應該被打開。那部分最終仍然取決於人的判斷、倫理，以及克

components.english: For instance, medical science can save countless lives, which is clearly wonderful. But scientific progress has also contributed to problems such as pollution, surveillance, and destructive military technology. So the real issue is not science itself, but whether human wisdom can keep pace with human invention.
components.chinese: 例如，醫學科學可以拯救無數生命，這當然是很美好的事。不過，科學進步同時也和污染、監控，以及具破壞性的軍事科技等問題有關。所以，真正的問題不在於科學本身，而在於人類的智慧能否跟得上人類的發明。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, medical science can save countless lives, which is clearly
wonderful. But scientific progress has also contributed to problems such as
pollution, surveillance, and destructive military technology. So the real issue is
not science itself, but whether human wisdom can keep pace with human
invention.
[例如，醫學科學可以拯救無數生命，這當然是很美好的事。不過，科學進步同時也和污染、
監控，以及具破壞性的軍事科技等問題有關。所以，真正的問題不在於科學本身，而在於人
類的智慧能否跟得上人類的發明。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say science has had a profound and mostly positive influence on human life, but it is a double-edged sword. It has given humanity stronger hands; the real question is whether we also have a steady enough mind to use them well.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說科學對人類生活帶來了深遠而且大致正面的影響，但它始終是一把雙刃劍。它給了人類更有力的雙手；真正的問題是，我們是否同時也有足夠穩定而清醒的頭腦去好好使用它。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say science has had a profound and mostly positive
influence on human life, but it is a double-edged sword. It has given humanity
stronger hands; the real question is whether we also have a steady enough mind
to use them well.
[總結來說，我會說科學對人類生活帶來了深遠而且大致正面的影響，但它始終是一把雙刃
劍。它給了人類更有力的雙手；真正的問題是，我們是否同時也有足夠穩定而清醒的頭腦去
好好使用它。]

question.english: What can individuals do for scientific research?
question.chinese: 個人可以為科學研究做些甚麼？

source_lines.text: Topic: What can individuals do for scientific research?
個人可以為科學研究做些甚麼？

components.english: I think individuals can help scientific research in more ways than people often realise, even if they are not scientists themselves.
components.chinese: 我認為，個人其實能為科學研究做的事情，比很多人想像中更多，即使他們本身不是科學家也一樣。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think individuals can help scientific research in more ways than people
often realise, even if they are not scientists themselves.
[我認為，個人其實能為科學研究做的事情，比很多人想像中更多，即使他們本身不是科學
家也一樣。]

components.english: The main reason is that research does not move forward on lab coats alone; it also depends on public participation, support, and trust. Ordinary people can take part in surveys, medical studies, experiments, or citizen-science projects, and that kind of involvement can provide researchers with useful data and real-world insight. In that sense, individuals are not just standing on the sidelines; they can become part of the engine that keeps discovery moving.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，研究的推進並不只是靠實驗室裡穿白袍的人；它同時也依賴公眾的參與、支持，以及信任。一般人可以參加問卷調查、醫學研究、實驗，或者公民科學計劃，而這類參與能為研究人員提供有用的數據，以及來自現實世界的觀察。從這個角度來看，個人並不是只能站在場邊；他們其實也可以成為推動發現向前走的那部引擎裡的一部分。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that research does not move forward on lab
coats alone; it also depends on public participation, support, and trust.
Ordinary people can take part in surveys, medical studies, experiments, or
citizen-science projects, and that kind of involvement can provide researchers
with useful data and real-world insight. In that sense, individuals are not just
standing on the sidelines; they can become part of the engine that keeps
discovery moving.
[主要原因是，研究的推進並不只是靠實驗室裡穿白袍的人；它同時也依賴公眾的參與、支
持，以及信任。一般人可以參加問卷調查、醫學研究、實驗，或者公民科學計劃，而這類參
與能為研究人員提供有用的數據，以及來自現實世界的觀察。從這個角度來看，個人並不是
只能站在場邊；他們其實也可以成為推動發現向前走的那部引擎裡的一部分。]

components.english: For example, someone might join a university study, allow their health data to be used responsibly for research, or take part in a project that tracks birds, weather patterns, or air quality. One person’s contribution may seem small by itself, but when many people do the same, the value quickly adds up.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可以參加大學研究、在負責任和合規的情況下讓自己的健康數據被用作研究，或者參與一些追蹤鳥類、天氣模式，或空氣質素的計劃。單靠一個人的貢獻看起來也許不算大，但當很多人都一起這樣做時，它的價值很快就會累積起來。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone might join a university study, allow their
health data to be used responsibly for research, or take part in a project that
tracks birds, weather patterns, or air quality. One person’s contribution may
seem small by itself, but when many people do the same, the value quickly adds
up.
[例如，一個人可以參加大學研究、在負責任和合規的情況下讓自己的健康數據被用作研究
，或者參與一些追蹤鳥類、天氣模式，或空氣質素的計劃。單靠一個人的貢獻看起來也許不
算大，但當很多人都一起這樣做時，它的價值很快就會累積起來。]

components.english: So, one clear way individuals can help is by participating directly, because research often needs real people, real observations, and real cooperation.
components.chinese: 所以，個人其中一個很明顯能幫上忙的方法，就是直接參與，因為研究很多時都需要真實的人、真實的觀察，以及真實的合作。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear way individuals can help is by participating directly,
because research often needs real people, real observations, and real cooperation.
[所以，個人其中一個很明顯能幫上忙的方法，就是直接參與，因為研究很多時都需要真實
的人、真實的觀察，以及真實的合作。]

components.english: On top of that, I think individuals can also help scientific research indirectly by respecting evidence, supporting science education, and creating a culture that values curiosity and truth.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為，個人還可以透過尊重證據、支持科學教育，以及建立一種重視好奇心和真相的文化，去間接幫助科學研究。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think individuals can also help scientific research
indirectly by respecting evidence, supporting science education, and creating a
culture that values curiosity and truth.
[除此之外，我也認為，個人還可以透過尊重證據、支持科學教育，以及建立一種重視好奇心
和真相的文化，去間接幫助科學研究。]

components.english: To be honest, science does not flourish in a vacuum. It grows better in a society where people are willing to think critically, listen to evidence, and support long-term research even when the results are not immediate. Individuals can do that by reading responsibly, not spreading nonsense online, encouraging children to stay curious, donating to research causes, or backing institutions that do serious work. In other words, even if someone never steps into a lab, they can still help create the soil in which science grows.
components.chinese: 老實說，科學並不是在真空中自然繁盛的。它會在一個願意批判思考、願意聆聽證據，並且即使短期看不到成果也願意支持長線研究的社會裡，發展得更好。個人可以透過負責任地閱讀資訊、不在網上散播荒謬內容、鼓勵孩子保持好奇、捐助研究計劃，或者支持那些認真做事的機構，來做到這一點。換句話說，即使一個人從未走進實驗室，他仍然可以幫忙打造讓科學生長的土壤。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, science does not flourish in a vacuum. It grows
better in a society where people are willing to think critically, listen to evidence,
and support long-term research even when the results are not immediate.
Individuals can do that by reading responsibly, not spreading nonsense online,
encouraging children to stay curious, donating to research causes, or backing
institutions that do serious work. In other words, even if someone never steps
into a lab, they can still help create the soil in which science grows.
[老實說，科學並不是在真空中自然繁盛的。它會在一個願意批判思考、願意聆聽證據，並且
即使短期看不到成果也願意支持長線研究的社會裡，發展得更好。個人可以透過負責任地
閱讀資訊、不在網上散播荒謬內容、鼓勵孩子保持好奇、捐助研究計劃，或者支持那些認真
做事的機構，來做到這一點。換句話說，即使一個人從未走進實驗室，他仍然可以幫忙打造
讓科學生長的土壤。]

components.english: For instance, a parent who encourages a child to ask questions, a citizen who supports public health research, or an ordinary reader who checks facts before sharing an article is already doing something meaningful. These actions may look quiet, but they help science breathe in the real world.
components.chinese: 例如，一位鼓勵孩子多提問的家長、一位支持公共健康研究的市民，或者一位在轉發文章之前先查證內容的普通讀者，其實都已經在做有意義的事情。這些行動看起來也許很安靜，但它們其實都在幫助科學在真實世界裡好好呼吸。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a parent who encourages a child to ask questions, a
citizen who supports public health research, or an ordinary reader who checks
facts before sharing an article is already doing something meaningful. These
actions may look quiet, but they help science breathe in the real world.
[例如，一位鼓勵孩子多提問的家長、一位支持公共健康研究的市民，或者一位在轉發文章
之前先查證內容的普通讀者，其實都已經在做有意義的事情。這些行動看起來也許很安靜，
但它們其實都在幫助科學在真實世界裡好好呼吸。]

components.english: Overall, I think individuals can contribute to scientific research both directly and indirectly. They can take part, give support, stay curious, and respect evidence. In the end, science may be driven by experts, but it moves further when ordinary people are willing to walk alongside it.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我認為個人既可以直接，也可以間接地為科學研究作出貢獻。他們可以參與、支持、保持好奇心，並且尊重證據。說到底，科學也許是由專家推動的，但當普通人願意和它並肩前行時，它往往能走得更遠。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I think individuals can contribute to scientific research
both directly and indirectly. They can take part, give support, stay curious, and
respect evidence. In the end, science may be driven by experts, but it moves
further when ordinary people are willing to walk alongside it.
[總結來說，我認為個人既可以直接，也可以間接地為科學研究作出貢獻。他們可以參與、支
持、保持好奇心，並且尊重證據。說到底，科學也許是由專家推動的，但當普通人願意和它
並肩前行時，它往往能走得更遠。]

question.english: What influence can international cooperation in science bring about?
question.chinese: 國際科學合作可以帶來甚麼影響？

source_lines.text: Topic: What influence can international cooperation in science bring about?
國際科學合作可以帶來甚麼影響？

components.english: I think international cooperation in science can have a hugely positive influence, mainly because it allows knowledge, talent, and resources to be pooled rather than kept in separate corners.
components.chinese: 我認為，國際科學合作可以帶來非常正面的影響，主要因為它能讓知識、人才和資源被匯聚起來，而不是分散地各自留在不同角落。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think international cooperation in science can have a hugely positive
influence, mainly because it allows knowledge, talent, and resources to be pooled
rather than kept in separate corners.
[我認為，國際科學合作可以帶來非常正面的影響，主要因為它能讓知識、人才和資源被匯
聚起來，而不是分散地各自留在不同角落。]

components.english: The main reason is that science moves faster when minds meet. No single country has all the answers, and many scientific problems are simply too large for one nation to tackle alone. When researchers share data, compare findings, and build on each other’s work, progress can gather real momentum. In that sense, international cooperation does not just add knowledge; it multiplies it.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，當不同的頭腦走在一起時，科學就會走得更快。沒有任何一個國家擁有所有答案，而很多科學問題本身也大到不是單靠一個國家就能解決的。當研究人員共享數據、比較研究結果，並在彼此的工作基礎上繼續推進時，進步就能真正形成動能。從這個角度來說，國際合作不只是增加知識，而是在放大知識的力量。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that science moves faster when minds meet.
No single country has all the answers, and many scientific problems are simply
too large for one nation to tackle alone. When researchers share data, compare
findings, and build on each other’s work, progress can gather real momentum.
In that sense, international cooperation does not just add knowledge; it
multiplies it.
[主要原因是，當不同的頭腦走在一起時，科學就會走得更快。沒有任何一個國家擁有所有
答案，而很多科學問題本身也大到不是單靠一個國家就能解決的。當研究人員共享數據、比
較研究結果，並在彼此的工作基礎上繼續推進時，進步就能真正形成動能。從這個角度來說
，國際合作不只是增加知識，而是在放大知識的力量。]

components.english: For example, if scientists from different countries work together on issues like disease, climate change, or clean energy, they can combine different strengths and move more quickly towards practical solutions. One team may have the technology, another may have funding, and another may have unique field experience. When those pieces click into place, the whole picture becomes much stronger.
components.chinese: 例如，如果不同國家的科學家在疾病、氣候變化，或者潔淨能源這些議題上合作，他們就能把各自不同的長處結合起來，更快走向實際的解決方法。一個團隊可能擁有技術，另一個可能有資金，還有另一個可能具備獨特的實地經驗。當這些拼圖拼在一起時，整體力量就會強得多。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if scientists from different countries work together on
issues like disease, climate change, or clean energy, they can combine different
strengths and move more quickly towards practical solutions. One team may
have the technology, another may have funding, and another may have unique
field experience. When those pieces click into place, the whole picture becomes
much stronger.
[例如，如果不同國家的科學家在疾病、氣候變化，或者潔淨能源這些議題上合作，他們就
能把各自不同的長處結合起來，更快走向實際的解決方法。一個團隊可能擁有技術，另一個
可能有資金，還有另一個可能具備獨特的實地經驗。當這些拼圖拼在一起時，整體力量就會
強得多。]

components.english: So, one major influence of international scientific cooperation is that it can accelerate discovery and make it easier to deal with problems that cross borders.
components.chinese: 所以，國際科學合作其中一個很大的影響，就是它能加快發現的速度，並讓人更容易處理那些本來就跨越國界的問題。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major influence of international scientific cooperation is
that it can accelerate discovery and make it easier to deal with problems that
cross borders.
[所以，國際科學合作其中一個很大的影響，就是它能加快發現的速度，並讓人更容易處理
那些本來就跨越國界的問題。]

components.english: On top of that, I think scientific cooperation can also bring countries closer together by building trust, shared standards, and a stronger sense of common purpose.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為科學合作能把不同國家拉得更近，因為它能建立信任、共同標準，以及更強烈的共同目標感。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think scientific cooperation can also bring countries
closer together by building trust, shared standards, and a stronger sense of
common purpose.
[除此之外，我也認為科學合作能把不同國家拉得更近，因為它能建立信任、共同標準
，以及更強烈的共同目標感。]

components.english: When people from different countries work side by side in the name of evidence and discovery, they are not just exchanging information; they are also learning how to listen, respect differences, and work towards something bigger than politics alone. In that sense, science can act like a bridge. It does not erase disagreement, of course, but it can remind people that some goals are shared by humanity as a whole.
components.chinese: 當來自不同國家的人為了證據和發現而並肩合作時，他們不只是在交換資訊；他們同時也在學習如何聆聽、如何尊重差異，以及如何朝著比單純政治更大的目標努力。從這個角度來說，科學可以像一座橋。它當然不能抹去所有分歧，但它能提醒人們：有些目標其實是整個人類共同擁有的。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: When people from different countries work side by side in the
name of evidence and discovery, they are not just exchanging information; they
are also learning how to listen, respect differences, and work towards something
bigger than politics alone. In that sense, science can act like a bridge. It does not
erase disagreement, of course, but it can remind people that some goals are
shared by humanity as a whole.
[當來自不同國家的人為了證據和發現而並肩合作時，他們不只是在交換資訊；他們同
時也在學習如何聆聽、如何尊重差異，以及如何朝著比單純政治更大的目標努力。從
這個角度來說，科學可以像一座橋。它當然不能抹去所有分歧，但它能提醒人們：有些
目標其實是整個人類共同擁有的。]

components.english: For instance, if young scientists study abroad, join international research teams, or attend global conferences, they often come away with more than technical knowledge. They also gain a wider perspective and a better understanding of how other societies think and work. That kind of connection can plant the seeds of long-term cooperation far beyond the laboratory.
components.chinese: 例如，如果年輕科學家到海外進修、加入國際研究團隊，或者參加全球性的學術會議，他們最後帶走的往往不只是技術知識。他們同時也會獲得更開闊的視野，並更理解其他社會是怎樣思考和運作的。這種連結很多時不只停留在實驗室裡，還能為長遠合作種下種子。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if young scientists study abroad, join international
research teams, or attend global conferences, they often come away with more
than technical knowledge. They also gain a wider perspective and a better
understanding of how other societies think and work. That kind of connection
can plant the seeds of long-term cooperation far beyond the laboratory.
[例如，如果年輕科學家到海外進修、加入國際研究團隊，或者參加全球性的學術會議
，他們最後帶走的往往不只是技術知識。他們同時也會獲得更開闊的視野，並更理解
其他社會是怎樣思考和運作的。這種連結很多時不只停留在實驗室裡，還能為長遠合
作種下種子。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say international cooperation in science can influence the world in both practical and human ways: it can speed up progress, improve lives, and build bridges between countries. In the end, when science is shared rather than guarded, it has a far better chance of serving the world rather than just serving one corner of it.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說國際科學合作能在實際層面和人文層面同時影響世界：它能加快進步、改善生活，也能在國家之間搭起橋樑。說到底，當科學是被分享而不是被死守時，它就更有機會服務整個世界，而不只是世界中的某一個角落。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say international cooperation in science can
influence the world in both practical and human ways: it can speed up progress,
improve lives, and build bridges between countries. In the end, when science is
shared rather than guarded, it has a far better chance of serving the world
rather than just serving one corner of it.
[總結來說，我會說國際科學合作能在實際層面和人文層面同時影響世界：它能加快進
步、改善生活，也能在國家之間搭起橋樑。說到底，當科學是被分享而不是被死守時，
它就更有機會服務整個世界，而不只是世界中的某一個角落。]

question.english: Do you think it is mandatory to teach science in schools?
question.chinese: 你認為學校是否必須教授科學？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think it is mandatory to teach science in schools?
你認為學校是否必須教授科學？

components.english: Yes, I do think science should be mandatory in schools, at least to a basic level.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為學校應該必須教授科學，至少在基礎層面上應該是這樣。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do think science should be mandatory in schools, at least to a basic
level.
[是的，我的確認為學校應該必須教授科學，至少在基礎層面上應該是這樣。]

components.english: The main reason is that science is not just another school subject; it is a way of understanding the world. It helps students see how things work, ask better questions, and separate fact from fiction. In modern life, people are constantly surrounded by issues related to health, technology, energy, and the environment, so without some scientific understanding, they are almost walking through fog.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，科學不只是一門普通學科；它其實是一種理解世界的方法。它能幫助學生明白事物如何運作、提出更好的問題，並且分清事實和想像。在現代生活裡，人們不斷面對和健康、科技、能源，以及環境有關的議題，所以如果沒有某種基本的科學理解，其實就很像在迷霧中行走。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that science is not just another school
subject; it is a way of understanding the world. It helps students see how things
work, ask better questions, and separate fact from fiction. In modern life, people
are constantly surrounded by issues related to health, technology, energy, and
the environment, so without some scientific understanding, they are almost
walking through fog.
[主要原因是，科學不只是一門普通學科；它其實是一種理解世界的方法。它能幫助學生明
白事物如何運作、提出更好的問題，並且分清事實和想像。在現代生活裡，人們不斷面對和
健康、科技、能源，以及環境有關的議題，所以如果沒有某種基本的科學理解，其實就很像
在迷霧中行走。]

components.english: For example, if students learn basic science, they are in a much better position to understand things like vaccines, nutrition, pollution, or even simple everyday technology. That knowledge may begin in the classroom, but it quickly spills into real life.
components.chinese: 例如，如果學生學過基本科學，他們就會更有能力理解疫苗、營養、污染，甚至一些日常科技產品這類事情。這些知識也許是從課室開始的，但很快就會流到真實生活裡。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if students learn basic science, they are in a much
better position to understand things like vaccines, nutrition, pollution, or even
simple everyday technology. That knowledge may begin in the classroom, but it
quickly spills into real life.
[例如，如果學生學過基本科學，他們就會更有能力理解疫苗、營養、污染，甚至一些日常科
技產品這類事情。這些知識也許是從課室開始的，但很快就會流到真實生活裡。]

components.english: So yes, I would say science should definitely be a compulsory part of education, because it gives students a more informed and rational way to live in the modern world.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說科學絕對應該是教育裡的必修部分，因為它能給學生一種更有根據、也更理性的方式去面對現代世界。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say science should definitely be a compulsory part
of education, because it gives students a more informed and rational way to live
in the modern world.
[所以，是的，我會說科學絕對應該是教育裡的必修部分，因為它能給學生一種更有根據、
也更理性的方式去面對現代世界。]

components.english: That said, I do not think every student needs to study science in a highly advanced or specialised way.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為每一個學生都需要以非常高深或專門化的方式去學科學。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think every student needs to study science in a
highly advanced or specialised way.
[不過，我並不認為每一個學生都需要以非常高深或專門化的方式去學科學。]

components.english: To me, there is a difference between making science compulsory and forcing every student down the same academic path. Basic scientific literacy is essential, but beyond that, students should have some freedom to follow their strengths and interests. A future artist, writer, or musician still needs enough science to think clearly, but they do not necessarily need to be pushed deep into complex formulas if that is not where their gifts lie. In other words, the foundation should be shared, but the staircase does not have to be identical for everyone.
components.chinese: 對我來說，「科學必修」和「逼每一個學生走同一條學術路」其實是兩回事。基本的科學素養是必要的，但再往上走之後，學生應該有一些空間去跟隨自己的長處和興趣。未來的藝術家、作家，或者音樂家，當然仍然需要足夠的科學知識去清晰思考，但如果那不是他們天賦所在，也不一定要被一路推進複雜公式的深水區。換句話說，地基應該大家都有，但樓梯不必每個人都走得一模一樣。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To me, there is a difference between making science
compulsory and forcing every student down the same academic path. Basic
scientific literacy is essential, but beyond that, students should have some
freedom to follow their strengths and interests. A future artist, writer, or
musician still needs enough science to think clearly, but they do not necessarily
need to be pushed deep into complex formulas if that is not where their gifts lie.
In other words, the foundation should be shared, but the staircase does not have
to be identical for everyone.
[對我來說，「科學必修」和「逼每一個學生走同一條學術路」其實是兩回事。基本的科學素養
是必要的，但再往上走之後，學生應該有一些空間去跟隨自己的長處和興趣。未來的藝術
家、作家，或者音樂家，當然仍然需要足夠的科學知識去清晰思考，但如果那不是他們天賦
所在，也不一定要被一路推進複雜公式的深水區。換句話說，地基應該大家都有，但樓梯不
必每個人都走得一模一樣。]

components.english: For instance, I think all students should understand the basics of biology, physics, and scientific reasoning, but not all of them need to study advanced chemistry or higher-level physics in the same depth. That would be a bit like using one key for every lock.
components.chinese: 例如，我認為所有學生都應該理解生物、物理，以及科學思維的基本概念，但並不是每個人都需要用同樣深的程度去學高階化學或高階物理。那樣做其實有點像拿同一把鑰匙去開所有鎖。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, I think all students should understand the basics of
biology, physics, and scientific reasoning, but not all of them need to study
advanced chemistry or higher-level physics in the same depth. That would be a
bit like using one key for every lock.
[例如，我認為所有學生都應該理解生物、物理，以及科學思維的基本概念，但並不是每個
人都需要用同樣深的程度去學高階化學或高階物理。那樣做其實有點像拿同一把鑰匙去開
所有鎖。]

components.english: Overall, I do think science should be mandatory in schools, because every student needs a basic understanding of the world they live in. However, compulsory science should mean giving everyone a strong foundation, not forcing everyone into the same mould.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我的確認為學校應該必修科學，因為每一個學生都需要對自己所生活的世界有基本理解。不過，科學必修的意思應該是讓每個人都有穩固的基礎，而不是把每個人都硬塞進同一個模子裡。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I do think science should be mandatory in schools,
because every student needs a basic understanding of the world they live in.
However, compulsory science should mean giving everyone a strong foundation,
not forcing everyone into the same mould.
[總結來說，我的確認為學校應該必修科學，因為每一個學生都需要對自己所生活的世界有
基本理解。不過，科學必修的意思應該是讓每個人都有穩固的基礎，而不是把每個人都硬塞
進同一個模子裡。]

question.english: Why do some people dislike using text messages?
question.chinese: 為甚麼有些人不喜歡用文字訊息溝通？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do some people dislike using text messages?
為甚麼有些人不喜歡用文字訊息溝通？

components.english: I think one major reason is that text messages can feel a bit cold and limited, especially when people want real human connection.
components.chinese: 我認為，其中一個主要原因是，文字訊息很多時會讓人覺得有點冷，也有點受限制，特別是當人們真正想要的是有溫度的人際連結。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one major reason is that text messages can feel a bit cold and
limited, especially when people want real human connection.
[我認為，其中一個主要原因是，文字訊息很多時會讓人覺得有點冷，也有點受限制，特別
是當人們真正想要的是有溫度的人際連結。]

components.english: When people text, they lose a lot of the things that make communication feel natural, such as tone of voice, facial expression, and immediate reaction. As a result, even a simple message can come across the wrong way. A joke may sound rude, a short reply may sound impatient, and a serious conversation may feel flatter than it should. In that sense, text messages can sometimes feel like trying to paint with only one colour.
components.chinese: 當人們用文字傳訊時，他們會失去很多讓溝通變得自然的元素，例如語氣、表情，以及即時反應。結果就是，即使一條很簡單的訊息，也可能會被理解錯。玩笑可能看起來像無禮，簡短回覆可能像不耐煩，而嚴肅的對話也可能變得比原本應該有的樣子更平板。從這個角度來看，文字訊息有時就像只用一種顏色去畫畫。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When people text, they lose a lot of the things that make
communication feel natural, such as tone of voice, facial expression, and
immediate reaction. As a result, even a simple message can come across the
wrong way. A joke may sound rude, a short reply may sound impatient, and a
serious conversation may feel flatter than it should. In that sense, text messages
can sometimes feel like trying to paint with only one colour.
[當人們用文字傳訊時，他們會失去很多讓溝通變得自然的元素，例如語氣、表情，以及即
時反應。結果就是，即使一條很簡單的訊息，也可能會被理解錯。玩笑可能看起來像無禮，
簡短回覆可能像不耐煩，而嚴肅的對話也可能變得比原本應該有的樣子更平板。從這個角
度來看，文字訊息有時就像只用一種顏色去畫畫。]

components.english: For example, if someone writes “Fine.” in a message, the reader may start wondering whether that means genuinely fine, slightly annoyed, or quietly upset. In face-to-face conversation, or even on a phone call, that kind of confusion is much less likely.
components.chinese: 例如，如果有人在訊息裡只寫一句「Fine.」，讀的人可能就會開始猜，到底那是真的沒事、是有點不高興，還是其實心裡有情緒。在面對面交談，甚至只是講電話時，這種誤會通常都比較不容易出現。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone writes “Fine.” in a message, the reader may
start wondering whether that means genuinely fine, slightly annoyed, or quietly
upset. In face-to-face conversation, or even on a phone call, that kind of
confusion is much less likely.
[例如，如果有人在訊息裡只寫一句「Fine.」，讀的人可能就會開始猜，到底那是真的沒事、
是有點不高興，還是其實心裡有情緒。在面對面交談，甚至只是講電話時，這種誤會通常都
比較不容易出現。]

components.english: So, one clear reason some people dislike text messages is that they can make communication feel thinner, colder, and more easily misunderstood.
components.chinese: 所以，有些人不喜歡文字訊息，其中一個很明顯的原因，就是它會令溝通變得較單薄、較冷，也更容易被誤解。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear reason some people dislike text messages is that they
can make communication feel thinner, colder, and more easily misunderstood.
[所以，有些人不喜歡文字訊息，其中一個很明顯的原因，就是它會令溝通變得較單薄、較
冷，也更容易被誤解。]

components.english: On top of that, some people simply find texting inconvenient, tiring, or less satisfying than speaking directly.
components.chinese: 除此之外，有些人也只是單純覺得打字很麻煩、很累，或者遠遠沒有直接說話那麼令人滿足。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, some people simply find texting inconvenient, tiring,
or less satisfying than speaking directly.
[除此之外，有些人也只是單純覺得打字很麻煩、很累，或者遠遠沒有直接說話那麼令人滿

components.english: Not everyone enjoys typing out long thoughts on a small screen. Some people speak much more naturally than they write, and others may not like staring at their phone all day. There is also a generational side to it sometimes. Older people, in particular, may prefer phone calls because they feel quicker, clearer, and more personal. To them, texting can feel like too much tapping for too little warmth.
components.chinese: 不是每一個人都喜歡在小小的螢幕上慢慢打出一大段想法。有些人本來就比起寫字更擅長說話，而也有人不喜歡整天盯著手機看。很多時候，這裡面也會帶點世代差異。特別是一些年長人士，可能會更喜歡打電話，因為那樣更快、更清楚，也更有人情味。對他們來說，傳訊息有時會像是按了半天，卻換不來多少溫度。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Not everyone enjoys typing out long thoughts on a small screen.
Some people speak much more naturally than they write, and others may not
like staring at their phone all day. There is also a generational side to it
sometimes. Older people, in particular, may prefer phone calls because they feel
quicker, clearer, and more personal. To them, texting can feel like too much
tapping for too little warmth.
[不是每一個人都喜歡在小小的螢幕上慢慢打出一大段想法。有些人本來就比起寫字更擅
長說話，而也有人不喜歡整天盯著手機看。很多時候，這裡面也會帶點世代差異。特別是一
些年長人士，可能會更喜歡打電話，因為那樣更快、更清楚，也更有人情味。對他們來說，傳
訊息有時會像是按了半天，卻換不來多少溫度。]

components.english: For instance, if someone needs to explain a complicated problem, they may feel it is much easier to call a friend than to send ten separate messages and still worry that the meaning has not landed properly. In that case, texting starts to feel less like convenience and more like hard work.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人需要解釋一個很複雜的問題，他可能會覺得，與其分十條訊息慢慢傳，還不如直接打個電話給朋友，而且打完之後還不用擔心對方到底有沒有完全明白。在這種情況下，傳訊息給人的感覺就不再是方便，反而更像是一種費力。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if someone needs to explain a complicated problem,
they may feel it is much easier to call a friend than to send ten separate messages
and still worry that the meaning has not landed properly. In that case, texting
starts to feel less like convenience and more like hard work.
[例如，如果一個人需要解釋一個很複雜的問題，他可能會覺得，與其分十條訊息慢慢傳，
還不如直接打個電話給朋友，而且打完之後還不用擔心對方到底有沒有完全明白。在這種
情況下，傳訊息給人的感覺就不再是方便，反而更像是一種費力。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say some people dislike text messages because they can feel impersonal, easy to misread, and sometimes more tiring than talking. For those people, a real voice still carries something a screen cannot quite hold.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，有些人不喜歡文字訊息，是因為它會讓人覺得不夠有人情味、容易被誤解，而且有時甚至比直接說話更累。對這些人來說，真實的人聲始終帶著一些螢幕無法真正承載的東西。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say some people dislike text messages because they
can feel impersonal, easy to misread, and sometimes more tiring than talking.
For those people, a real voice still carries something a screen cannot quite hold.
[總結來說，我會說，有些人不喜歡文字訊息，是因為它會讓人覺得不夠有人情味、容易被
誤解，而且有時甚至比直接說話更累。對這些人來說，真實的人聲始終帶著一些螢幕無法真
正承載的東西。]

question.english: Have the ways that people communicated with each other changed much in the last few decades?
question.chinese: 在過去幾十年裡，人們彼此溝通的方式有很大改變嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Have the ways that people communicated with each other changed much
in the last few decades?
[在過去幾十年裡，人們彼此溝通的方式有很大改變嗎？]

components.english: Yes, absolutely. I think the way people communicate has changed enormously in the last few decades.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對有。我認為，在過去幾十年裡，人們溝通的方式出現了非常大的改變。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, absolutely. I think the way people communicate has changed
enormously in the last few decades.
[是的，絕對有。我認為，在過去幾十年裡，人們溝通的方式出現了非常大的改變。]

components.english: The biggest difference is speed. In the past, communication was often slower, more deliberate, and sometimes more formal. People relied much more on face-to-face conversations, landline calls, letters, or perhaps email later on. Now, communication is instant. A message can be sent in seconds, a video call can connect people across the world, and social media has turned communication into something constant rather than occasional. In that sense, we have moved from waiting for replies to almost living inside them.
components.chinese: 最大的分別在於速度。以前的溝通方式通常較慢、較經過思考，而且有時也較正式。人們較多依靠面對面交談、家居電話、書信，之後才慢慢有電郵。現在，溝通幾乎是即時的。一個訊息幾秒鐘就能送出，一個視像通話就能把相隔世界兩端的人連起來，而社交媒體更把溝通變成一件持續進行的事，而不是偶爾發生的事。從這個角度來說，我們已經從「等回覆」的年代，走到了幾乎像活在回覆之中的年代。
