their own point of view, but they are willing to admit when their behaviour has
crossed a line.
[例如，如果一個情緒較成熟的人在會議中打斷了別人，或者在爭論中說得太重，他往往較
容易在事後察覺到，並且主動道歉，而不用等別人逼他。他也許仍然有自己的立場，但他願
意承認自己的行為有越界的地方。]

components.english: So, in my view, the people most likely to apologise are those who have enough humility and emotional intelligence to value the relationship more than their pride.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，最容易道歉的人，通常是那些有足夠謙遜和情商的人，因為他們更重視關係本身，而不是一味死守自己的面子。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in my view, the people most likely to apologise are those who
have enough humility and emotional intelligence to value the relationship more
than their pride.
[所以，在我看來，最容易道歉的人，通常是那些有足夠謙遜和情商的人，因為他們更重視
關係本身，而不是一味死守自己的面子。]

components.english: That said, people who are very polite, conflict-averse, or slightly insecure may also say sorry very often, sometimes even more than necessary.
components.chinese: 不過，那些特別有禮貌、怕衝突，或者有點缺乏安全感的人，也常常會很容易說對不起，有時甚至比真正需要的還多。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, people who are very polite, conflict-averse, or slightly
insecure may also say sorry very often, sometimes even more than necessary.
[不過，那些特別有禮貌、怕衝突，或者有點缺乏安全感的人，也常常會很容易說對不起，有
時甚至比真正需要的還多。]

components.english: In those cases, the apology does not always come from deep reflection. Sometimes it comes from a desire to keep the peace, avoid tension, or stop other people from being unhappy. That is why some people say sorry almost as a reflex. They apologise for being late, for taking up space, for asking questions, and sometimes even for things that were not really their fault. In other words, some apologies come from strength, while others come from nervousness.
components.chinese: 在這些情況下，道歉不一定是出於很深的反思。有時候，它只是來自想維持和平、避免尷尬，或者不想令別人不高興的心態。所以，有些人會把說對不起變成一種反射動作。他們會為遲到道歉、為佔了位置道歉、為提問道歉，有時甚至連明明不是自己責任的事情也會先道歉。換句話說，有些道歉來自力量，但也有些道歉來自不安。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In those cases, the apology does not always come from deep
reflection. Sometimes it comes from a desire to keep the peace, avoid tension, or
stop other people from being unhappy. That is why some people say sorry
almost as a reflex. They apologise for being late, for taking up space, for asking
questions, and sometimes even for things that were not really their fault. In
other words, some apologies come from strength, while others come from
nervousness.
[在這些情況下，道歉不一定是出於很深的反思。有時候，它只是來自想維持和平、避免尷
尬，或者不想令別人不高興的心態。所以，有些人會把說對不起變成一種反射動作。他們會
為遲到道歉、為佔了位置道歉、為提問道歉，有時甚至連明明不是自己責任的事情也會先道
歉。換句話說，有些道歉來自力量，但也有些道歉來自不安。]

components.english: For instance, a customer service worker may apologise to angry customers for a delay they did not personally cause, simply because that is the quickest way to calm the situation. In the same way, a shy person might say “sorry” before asking for help, even though they have done nothing wrong at all.
components.chinese: 例如，一位客服人員也許會為一個其實不是自己造成的延誤向生氣的客人道歉，只因為那是最快令場面冷卻下來的方法。同樣地，一個害羞的人可能會在開口求助之前先說一句「不好意思」或「對不起」，即使他根本沒有做錯任何事。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a customer service worker may apologise to angry
customers for a delay they did not personally cause, simply because that is the
quickest way to calm the situation. In the same way, a shy person might say
“sorry” before asking for help, even though they have done nothing wrong at
all.
[例如，一位客服人員也許會為一個其實不是自己造成的延誤向生氣的客人道歉，只因為那
是最快令場面冷卻下來的方法。同樣地，一個害羞的人可能會在開口求助之前先說一句「不
好意思」或「對不起」，即使他根本沒有做錯任何事。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the people most likely to say sorry are either emotionally mature enough to take responsibility, or gentle enough to want harmony more than confrontation. The healthiest kind of apology, though, is the one that comes from honesty rather than fear.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，最容易說對不起的人，不是那些成熟到願意承擔責任的人，就是那些溫和到比起衝突更想維持和諧的人。不過，最健康的道歉，始終還是那種出於真誠，而不是出於害怕的道歉。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the people most likely to say sorry are either
emotionally mature enough to take responsibility, or gentle enough to want
harmony more than confrontation. The healthiest kind of apology, though, is
the one that comes from honesty rather than fear.
[總結來說，我會說，最容易說對不起的人，不是那些成熟到願意承擔責任的人，就是那些
溫和到比起衝突更想維持和諧的人。不過，最健康的道歉，始終還是那種出於真誠，而不是
出於害怕的道歉。]

question.english: In what situations do you think people need to, or should, apologise?
question.chinese: 你認為人在甚麼情況下需要，或者應該道歉？

source_lines.text: Topic: In what situations do you think people need to, or should, apologise?
[你認為人在甚麼情況下需要，或者應該道歉？]

components.english: I think people should apologise whenever their words or actions have hurt someone, caused inconvenience, or crossed a line, whether they meant to or not.
components.chinese: 我認為，只要一個人的說話或行為傷害了別人、帶來了不便，或者越了界，他就應該道歉，不論那是否出於本意。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think people should apologise whenever their words or actions have
hurt someone, caused inconvenience, or crossed a line, whether they meant to or
not.
[我認為，只要一個人的說話或行為傷害了別人、帶來了不便，或者越了界，他就應該道歉，
不論那是否出於本意。]

components.english: To me, an apology is not really about declaring yourself a terrible person; it is about acknowledging impact. People sometimes focus too much on intention and say, “I didn’t mean it,” as if that settles everything. But in real life, impact often matters just as much. If someone feels hurt, embarrassed, ignored, or let down because of what you did, a sincere apology can lower the temperature and begin to mend the damage. In that sense, an apology is less a sign of weakness than a sign of emotional maturity.
components.chinese: 對我來說，道歉其實不是在宣告自己是個很差的人，而是在承認事情帶來了甚麼影響。很多人太着重自己的本意，常常會說：「我又不是故意的」，好像這樣事情就算完了。但在現實生活裡，結果很多時候和動機一樣重要。如果別人因為你的行為而感到受傷、難堪、被忽視，或者失望，那麼一個真誠的道歉就能幫助事情降溫，並開始修補傷害。從這個角度來說，道歉與其說是軟弱，不如說是一種情感上的成熟。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: To me, an apology is not really about declaring yourself a
terrible person; it is about acknowledging impact. People sometimes focus too
much on intention and say, “I didn’t mean it,” as if that settles everything. But
in real life, impact often matters just as much. If someone feels hurt,
embarrassed, ignored, or let down because of what you did, a sincere apology
can lower the temperature and begin to mend the damage. In that sense, an
apology is less a sign of weakness than a sign of emotional maturity.
[對我來說，道歉其實不是在宣告自己是個很差的人，而是在承認事情帶來了甚麼影響。很
多人太着重自己的本意，常常會說：「我又不是故意的」，好像這樣事情就算完了。但在現實
生活裡，結果很多時候和動機一樣重要。如果別人因為你的行為而感到受傷、難堪、被忽視
，或者失望，那麼一個真誠的道歉就能幫助事情降溫，並開始修補傷害。從這個角度來說，
道歉與其說是軟弱，不如說是一種情感上的成熟。]

components.english: For example, if someone speaks too harshly in an argument, shows up very late and ruins other people’s plans, or makes a joke that lands badly, they should apologise. A small apology at the right moment can stop a small bruise from turning into a deeper crack in the relationship.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人在爭吵中說話太重、遲到很久而打亂了別人的安排，或者開了一個讓人不舒服的玩笑，那他都應該道歉。一句及時的小小道歉，很多時候就能避免一個小擦傷慢慢變成關係裡更深的裂縫。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone speaks too harshly in an argument, shows
up very late and ruins other people’s plans, or makes a joke that lands badly,
they should apologise. A small apology at the right moment can stop a small
bruise from turning into a deeper crack in the relationship.
[例如，如果一個人在爭吵中說話太重、遲到很久而打亂了別人的安排，或者開了一個讓人
不舒服的玩笑，那他都應該道歉。一句及時的小小道歉，很多時候就能避免一個小擦傷慢慢
變成關係裡更深的裂縫。]

components.english: So, generally speaking, people should apologise when they have caused real harm or discomfort, because saying sorry is often the first step towards putting things right.
components.chinese: 所以，一般來說，只要一個人帶來了真正的傷害或不舒服，他就應該道歉，因為說一句對不起，往往正是把事情拉回正軌的第一步。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, generally speaking, people should apologise when they have
caused real harm or discomfort, because saying sorry is often the first step
towards putting things right.
[所以，一般來說，只要一個人帶來了真正的傷害或不舒服，他就應該道歉，因為說一句對
不起，往往正是把事情拉回正軌的第一步。]

components.english: That said, I also think people should apologise in situations where trust or respect matters more than pride, even if the mistake seems small.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為，在一些信任和尊重比面子更重要的情況下，即使錯誤看起來不大，人也應該道歉。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I also think people should apologise in situations where
trust or respect matters more than pride, even if the mistake seems small.
[不過，我也認為，在一些信任和尊重比面子更重要的情況下，即使錯誤看起來不大，人也
應該道歉。]

components.english: Sometimes the issue is not dramatic, but it still leaves a mark. For instance, forgetting an important promise, interrupting someone repeatedly, or dismissing another person’s feelings may not look like a huge offence on the surface, but over time, those things can wear a relationship thin. That is why apologies matter not only after big mistakes, but also after small acts of carelessness. A relationship is rarely broken by one earthquake alone; sometimes it is worn down by tiny cracks no one bothers to repair.
components.chinese: 有時候，問題本身未必很戲劇化，但它仍然會留下痕跡。例如，忘記一個重要承諾、一次又一次打斷別人說話，或者輕視別人的感受，表面上看來也許不是甚麼大罪，但長久下來，這些事情卻可能把一段關係慢慢磨薄。這也是為甚麼道歉不只在大錯之後才重要，在那些出於粗心或疏忽的小錯之後也同樣重要。一段關係很少只是被一場大地震毀掉；很多時候，它是被那些沒有人願意修補的小裂縫慢慢耗損的。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Sometimes the issue is not dramatic, but it still leaves a mark.
For instance, forgetting an important promise, interrupting someone
repeatedly, or dismissing another person’s feelings may not look like a huge
offence on the surface, but over time, those things can wear a relationship thin.
That is why apologies matter not only after big mistakes, but also after small
acts of carelessness. A relationship is rarely broken by one earthquake alone;
sometimes it is worn down by tiny cracks no one bothers to repair.
[有時候，問題本身未必很戲劇化，但它仍然會留下痕跡。例如，忘記一個重要承諾、一次又
一次打斷別人說話，或者輕視別人的感受，表面上看來也許不是甚麼大罪，但長久下來，這
些事情卻可能把一段關係慢慢磨薄。這也是為甚麼道歉不只在大錯之後才重要，在那些出
於粗心或疏忽的小錯之後也同樣重要。一段關係很少只是被一場大地震毀掉；很多時候，它
是被那些沒有人願意修補的小裂縫慢慢耗損的。]

components.english: For instance, if a parent forgets something they promised their child, or a friend keeps cancelling plans at the last minute, an apology matters even if nobody is shouting or crying. In moments like that, saying sorry tells the other person, “I see what I did, and I’m not brushing it aside.”
components.chinese: 例如，如果一位父母忘記了自己答應孩子的事情，或者一位朋友總是在最後一刻取消約定，那麼即使當下沒有人大吵大鬧，也同樣需要一句道歉。在這些時候，說一句對不起其實是在向對方表達：「我看見了自己的問題，而且我沒有把它輕輕帶過。」

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a parent forgets something they promised their
child, or a friend keeps cancelling plans at the last minute, an apology matters
even if nobody is shouting or crying. In moments like that, saying sorry tells the
other person, “I see what I did, and I’m not brushing it aside.”
[例如，如果一位父母忘記了自己答應孩子的事情，或者一位朋友總是在最後一刻取消約定
，那麼即使當下沒有人大吵大鬧，也同樣需要一句道歉。在這些時候，說一句對不起其實是
在向對方表達：「我看見了自己的問題，而且我沒有把它輕輕帶過。」]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people should apologise whenever they have damaged someone’s feelings, trust, or peace of mind, whether in a big way or a small one. Essentially，a good apology does not make a person smaller; very often, it makes them better.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，只要一個人傷害了別人的感受、信任，或者內心的安穩，不論那是大是小，他都應該道歉。總結來說，一個好的道歉不會令一個人顯得渺小；很多時候，它反而會讓那個人變得更好。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say people should apologise whenever they have
damaged someone’s feelings, trust, or peace of mind, whether in a big way or a
small one. Essentially，a good apology does not make a person smaller; very
often, it makes them better.
[總結來說，我會說，只要一個人傷害了別人的感受、信任，或者內心的安穩，不論那是大是
小，他都應該道歉。總結來說，一個好的道歉不會令一個人顯得渺小；很多時候，它反而會
讓那個人變得更好。]

question.english: What do you think of those people who don’t apologise very often?
question.chinese: 你怎樣看那些不太常道歉的人？

source_lines.text: Topic: What do you think of those people who don’t apologise very often?
你怎樣看那些不太常道歉的人？

components.english: To be honest, I generally don’t have a very positive impression of people who hardly ever apologise, especially when they are clearly in the wrong.
components.chinese: 老實說，我通常對那些幾乎不怎樣道歉的人印象不算太好，尤其是當他們明明做錯了的時候。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: To be honest, I generally don’t have a very positive impression of people
who hardly ever apologise, especially when they are clearly in the wrong.
[老實說，我通常對那些幾乎不怎樣道歉的人印象不算太好，尤其是當他們明明做錯了的時

components.english: The main reason is that an apology is not just a word; it is a sign of self-awareness, humility, and respect for other people’s feelings. If someone never apologises, they can come across as proud, stubborn, or emotionally immature. It may feel as though they would rather dig their heels in than simply admit, “Yes, that was my fault.”
components.chinese: 主要原因是，道歉不只是一句說話而已；它其實是一種自覺、謙遜，以及尊重別人感受的表現。如果一個人從來不道歉，他往往會給人一種很驕傲、很固執，或者情感上不夠成熟的感覺。那種感覺就像是：他們寧願死撐到底，也不願意簡單地承認一句「是的，這是我的錯」。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that an apology is not just a word; it is a sign
of self-awareness, humility, and respect for other people’s feelings. If someone
never apologises, they can come across as proud, stubborn, or emotionally
immature. It may feel as though they would rather dig their heels in than
simply admit, “Yes, that was my fault.”
[主要原因是，道歉不只是一句說話而已；它其實是一種自覺、謙遜，以及尊重別人感受的
表現。如果一個人從來不道歉，他往往會給人一種很驕傲、很固執，或者情感上不夠成熟的
感覺。那種感覺就像是：他們寧願死撐到底，也不願意簡單地承認一句「是的，這是我的

components.english: For example, if someone says something hurtful in an argument and then just carries on as if nothing happened, that can leave a bad taste in the mouth. Even a small apology can clear the air, but silence can leave the tension hanging there like a cloud that never quite moves on.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人在爭執中說了很傷人的話，之後卻若無其事地繼續過日子，那真的很容易令人心裡不是味兒。其實一句小小的道歉，很多時就能化解氣氛；但如果甚麼都不說，那份尷尬和不快就會一直吊在那裡，像一片怎樣都散不掉的烏雲。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone says something hurtful in an argument and
then just carries on as if nothing happened, that can leave a bad taste in the
mouth. Even a small apology can clear the air, but silence can leave the tension
hanging there like a cloud that never quite moves on.
[例如，如果一個人在爭執中說了很傷人的話，之後卻若無其事地繼續過日子，那真的很容
易令人心裡不是味兒。其實一句小小的道歉，很多時就能化解氣氛；但如果甚麼都不說，那
份尷尬和不快就會一直吊在那裡，像一片怎樣都散不掉的烏雲。]

components.english: So, in general, I’d say people who rarely apologise can be quite difficult to deal with, because they make repair and reconciliation much harder than they need to be.
components.chinese: 所以，一般來說，我會覺得那些很少道歉的人相處起來會比較困難，因為他們會令修補關係、重建和解這件事變得比本來更難。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in general, I’d say people who rarely apologise can be quite
difficult to deal with, because they make repair and reconciliation much harder
than they need to be.
[所以，一般來說，我會覺得那些很少道歉的人相處起來會比較困難，因為他們會令修補關
係、重建和解這件事變得比本來更難。]

components.english: That said, I don’t think every person who apologises rarely is necessarily a bad person.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為每一個很少道歉的人就一定是壞人。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I don’t think every person who apologises rarely is
necessarily a bad person.
[不過，我也不認為每一個很少道歉的人就一定是壞人。]

components.english: Sometimes people grow up in families or cultures where apologising is seen as losing face or showing weakness. Others may find it genuinely hard to express emotion, even when they do feel remorse. In some cases, they may prefer to show regret through actions rather than words. So I think the real issue is not how often someone says “sorry,” but whether they are able to recognise the hurt they caused and make it right.
components.chinese: 有時候，一個人成長的家庭或文化背景，會把道歉看成丟面子，或者看成軟弱的表現。也有些人其實是真的不擅長表達情感，即使他心裡的確有後悔。在某些情況下，他們甚至更傾向用行動去表達歉意，而不是用說話。所以我覺得，真正的重點不只是他有多常說「對不起」，而是他能不能看見自己造成了甚麼傷害，並且願意補救。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Sometimes people grow up in families or cultures where
apologising is seen as losing face or showing weakness. Others may find it
genuinely hard to express emotion, even when they do feel remorse. In some
cases, they may prefer to show regret through actions rather than words. So I
think the real issue is not how often someone says “sorry,” but whether they are
able to recognise the hurt they caused and make it right.
[有時候，一個人成長的家庭或文化背景，會把道歉看成丟面子，或者看成軟弱的表現。也
有些人其實是真的不擅長表達情感，即使他心裡的確有後悔。在某些情況下，他們甚至更傾
向用行動去表達歉意，而不是用說話。所以我覺得，真正的重點不只是他有多常說「對不起」
，而是他能不能看見自己造成了甚麼傷害，並且願意補救。]

components.english: For instance, a father may never say “I’m sorry” very easily, but if he realises he was too harsh, he may change his behaviour, speak more gently next time, or quietly try to make things better. That doesn’t make the lack of apology ideal, of course, but it does show that actions can sometimes speak louder than words.
components.chinese: 例如，一位父親也許不太容易直接說出「對不起」，但如果他意識到自己之前太嚴厲了，他可能會改變自己的做法、下次說話更溫和，或者安靜地用行動把事情修補回來。那當然不代表不道歉就是理想的做法，但它的確說明了：有時候，行動也可以比說話更有力量。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a father may never say “I’m sorry” very easily, but if he
realises he was too harsh, he may change his behaviour, speak more gently next
time, or quietly try to make things better. That doesn’t make the lack of apology
ideal, of course, but it does show that actions can sometimes speak louder than
words.
[例如，一位父親也許不太容易直接說出「對不起」，但如果他意識到自己之前太嚴厲了，他
可能會改變自己的做法、下次說話更溫和，或者安靜地用行動把事情修補回來。那當然不代
表不道歉就是理想的做法，但它的確說明了：有時候，行動也可以比說話更有力量。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people who don’t apologise very often can be frustrating, and in many cases they really should learn to swallow their pride a little more. Still, I’d judge them not only by whether they say the words, but also by whether they reflect, take responsibility, and try to put things right.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說那些不太常道歉的人確實可能令人很挫折，而在很多情況下，他們也的確應該學會多放下面子一點。不過，我最終不會只看他們有沒有把那句話說出口；我也會看他們有沒有反思、有沒有承擔責任，以及有沒有真心把事情修補好。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say people who don’t apologise very often can be
frustrating, and in many cases they really should learn to swallow their pride a
little more. Still, I’d judge them not only by whether they say the words, but
also by whether they reflect, take responsibility, and try to put things right.
[總結來說，我會說那些不太常道歉的人確實可能令人很挫折，而在很多情況下，他們也的
確應該學會多放下面子一點。不過，我最終不會只看他們有沒有把那句話說出口；我也會看
他們有沒有反思、有沒有承擔責任，以及有沒有真心把事情修補好。]

question.english: What different types of traditional products are produced in your country?
question.chinese: 你國家會生產哪些不同類型的傳統產品？

source_lines.text: What different types of traditional products are produced in your country?
你國家會生產哪些不同類型的傳統產品？

components.english: In Hong Kong, I’d say traditional products mainly fall into two broad groups: traditional foods and handmade cultural products.
components.chinese: 在香港，我會說傳統產品主要可以分成兩大類：傳統食品，以及手工製作的文化產品。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, I’d say traditional products mainly fall into two broad
groups: traditional foods and handmade cultural products.
[在香港，我會說傳統產品主要可以分成兩大類：傳統食品，以及手工製作的文化產品。]

components.english: The first group is probably the easiest to notice, because food is where tradition often stays most alive. Hong Kong is known for a lot of traditional local products, especially things like wife cakes, egg rolls, herbal tea, dried seafood, and other old-style snacks or ingredients that are closely tied to Cantonese food culture. These products are not just things people consume; they also carry memory, family habits, and a sense of place. In that sense, traditional food is a bit like edible history.
components.chinese: 第一類大概是最容易看見的，因為傳統很多時候最容易留在食物裡。香港有不少傳統本地產品，尤其像老婆餅、蛋卷、涼茶、海味，以及其他一些和粵式飲食文化緊緊相連的舊式小食或材料。這些產品不只是人們吃的東西；它們同時也承載著回憶、家庭習慣，以及一種地方感。從這個角度來看，傳統食物有點像可以吃下去的歷史。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The first group is probably the easiest to notice, because food is
where tradition often stays most alive. Hong Kong is known for a lot of
traditional local products, especially things like wife cakes, egg rolls, herbal tea,
dried seafood, and other old-style snacks or ingredients that are closely tied to
Cantonese food culture. These products are not just things people consume;
they also carry memory, family habits, and a sense of place. In that sense,
traditional food is a bit like edible history.
[第一類大概是最容易看見的，因為傳統很多時候最容易留在食物裡。香港有不少傳統本地
產品，尤其像老婆餅、蛋卷、涼茶、海味，以及其他一些和粵式飲食文化緊緊相連的舊式小
食或材料。這些產品不只是人們吃的東西；它們同時也承載著回憶、家庭習慣，以及一種地
方感。從這個角度來看，傳統食物有點像可以吃下去的歷史。]

components.english: For example, people often buy traditional pastries or festive food during celebrations, and many families still use dried seafood, preserved ingredients, or herbal drinks in everyday life. Even if modern food is everywhere, these older products still hold their ground because they taste of home.
components.chinese: 例如，人們在節日時常會購買傳統糕餅或應節食品，而很多家庭在日常生活中也仍然會用海味、保存食材，或者喝一些草本飲品。即使現代食品到處都是，這些較傳統的產品仍然站得住腳，因為它們有一種家的味道。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, people often buy traditional pastries or festive food
during celebrations, and many families still use dried seafood, preserved
ingredients, or herbal drinks in everyday life. Even if modern food is
everywhere, these older products still hold their ground because they taste of
home.
[例如，人們在節日時常會購買傳統糕餅或應節食品，而很多家庭在日常生活中也仍然會用
海味、保存食材，或者喝一些草本飲品。即使現代食品到處都是，這些較傳統的產品仍然站
得住腳，因為它們有一種家的味道。]

components.english: So, one major type of traditional product in Hong Kong is food, because food keeps culture alive in a very direct and everyday way.
components.chinese: 所以，香港其中一大類傳統產品就是食品，因為食物能以一種很直接、也很日常的方式把文化留住。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major type of traditional product in Hong Kong is food,
because food keeps culture alive in a very direct and everyday way.
[所以，香港其中一大類傳統產品就是食品，因為食物能以一種很直接、也很日常的方式把
文化留住。]

components.english: On top of that, Hong Kong also produces traditional handmade items, especially those connected with clothing, craftsmanship, and old cultural practices.
components.chinese: 除此之外，香港也會生產一些傳統手工產品，特別是那些和服飾、工藝，以及舊有文化習俗有關的東西。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, Hong Kong also produces traditional handmade items,
especially those connected with clothing, craftsmanship, and old cultural
practices.
[除此之外，香港也會生產一些傳統手工產品，特別是那些和服飾、工藝，以及舊有文化習
俗有關的東西。]

components.english: To be honest, these products may be less visible than food nowadays, but they are still an important part of traditional culture. I’m thinking of things like handmade cheongsam, traditional seals, paper crafts, incense-related items, and products linked to festivals or Cantonese opera. These are the kinds of things that show the touch of the hand rather than the speed of the machine. In other words, they are not only products; they are pieces of heritage made tangible.
components.chinese: 老實說，這些產品今天也許沒有食物那麼容易被看見，但它們仍然是傳統文化裡很重要的一部分。我想到的像是手工旗袍、傳統印章、紙藝、香品，以及一些和節慶或粵劇有關的用品。這些東西展現的，是手工的溫度，而不是機器的速度。換句話說，它們不只是產品，而是把文化遺產真正做成了可以摸得到的東西。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, these products may be less visible than food
nowadays, but they are still an important part of traditional culture. I’m
thinking of things like handmade cheongsam, traditional seals, paper crafts,
incense-related items, and products linked to festivals or Cantonese opera.
These are the kinds of things that show the touch of the hand rather than the
speed of the machine. In other words, they are not only products; they are pieces
of heritage made tangible.
[老實說，這些產品今天也許沒有食物那麼容易被看見，但它們仍然是傳統文化裡很重要的
一部分。我想到的像是手工旗袍、傳統印章、紙藝、香品，以及一些和節慶或粵劇有關的用
品。這些東西展現的，是手工的溫度，而不是機器的速度。換句話說，它們不只是產品，而是
把文化遺產真正做成了可以摸得到的東西。]

components.english: For instance, a handmade cheongsam is not just a dress; it reflects tailoring skill, aesthetic tradition, and a certain elegance that fast fashion cannot easily copy. In the same way, traditional festival crafts may look simple on the surface, but they often carry layers of symbolism and custom behind them.
components.chinese: 例如，一件手工旗袍不只是一條裙子；它同時反映了裁縫技藝、審美傳統，以及一種快時尚很難真正複製的雅致。同樣地，一些傳統節慶工藝品表面上也許看起來很簡單，但背後往往承載著一層又一層的象徵意義和習俗。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a handmade cheongsam is not just a dress; it reflects
tailoring skill, aesthetic tradition, and a certain elegance that fast fashion
cannot easily copy. In the same way, traditional festival crafts may look simple
on the surface, but they often carry layers of symbolism and custom behind
them.
[例如，一件手工旗袍不只是一條裙子；它同時反映了裁縫技藝、審美傳統，以及一種快時
尚很難真正複製的雅致。同樣地，一些傳統節慶工藝品表面上也許看起來很簡單，但背後往
往承載著一層又一層的象徵意義和習俗。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the main traditional products in Hong Kong are traditional foods and handmade cultural goods. One feeds the body, while the other feeds the memory; together, they help keep local identity from fading into the background.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港主要的傳統產品包括傳統食品，以及手工文化用品。一類滋養身體，另一類滋養記憶；兩者加在一起，幫助本地身份認同不至於慢慢退到背景裡。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the main traditional products in Hong Kong
are traditional foods and handmade cultural goods. One feeds the body, while
the other feeds the memory; together, they help keep local identity from fading
into the background.
[總結來說，我會說香港主要的傳統產品包括傳統食品，以及手工文化用品。一類滋養身體
，另一類滋養記憶；兩者加在一起，幫助本地身份認同不至於慢慢退到背景裡。]

question.english: In your opinion, why do traditional products attract tourists?
question.chinese: 依你看，為甚麼傳統產品會吸引遊客？

source_lines.text: Topic: In your opinion, why do traditional products attract tourists?
依你看，為甚麼傳統產品會吸引遊客？

components.english: I think traditional products attract tourists mainly because they feel authentic and give people a real connection to the local culture.
components.chinese: 我認為，傳統產品之所以會吸引遊客，主要是因為它們帶有真實感，並且能讓人和當地文化產生真正的連結。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think traditional products attract tourists mainly because they feel
authentic and give people a real connection to the local culture.
[我認為，傳統產品之所以會吸引遊客，主要是因為它們帶有真實感，並且能讓人和當地文
化產生真正的連結。]

components.english: The main reason is that tourists usually do not travel just to see things they could easily find at home. They want something rooted in the place they are visiting — something with local colour, history, and character. Traditional products often carry the fingerprints of a culture, so to speak. They reflect local skills, materials, customs, and ways of life, which makes them feel much more meaningful than ordinary mass-produced goods.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，遊客通常不會只是為了看一些在自己家附近也輕易能找到的東西而旅行。他們想看到的是一些真正扎根於當地的東西——一些帶有地方色彩、歷史和個性的事物。某程度上，傳統產品就像帶著一種文化的指紋。它們反映出當地的手藝、材料、習俗，以及生活方式，所以和一般大量生產的商品相比，往往更有意義。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that tourists usually do not travel just to see
things they could easily find at home. They want something rooted in the place
they are visiting — something with local colour, history, and character.
Traditional products often carry the fingerprints of a culture, so to speak. They
reflect local skills, materials, customs, and ways of life, which makes them feel
much more meaningful than ordinary mass-produced goods.
[主要原因是，遊客通常不會只是為了看一些在自己家附近也輕易能找到的東西而旅行。他
們想看到的是一些真正扎根於當地的東西——一些帶有地方色彩、歷史和個性的事物。某
程度上，傳統產品就像帶著一種文化的指紋。它們反映出當地的手藝、材料、習俗，以及生
活方式，所以和一般大量生產的商品相比，往往更有意義。]

components.english: For example, if visitors come to a place like Hong Kong, they may be drawn to traditional snacks, handmade crafts, old-style tea sets, or products linked to Cantonese culture. Those items do not just look nice; they tell a story about where they come from.
components.chinese: 例如，如果遊客來到像香港這樣的地方，他們可能會被一些傳統小食、手工藝品、舊式茶具，或者和粵語文化有關的產品吸引。這些東西不只是看起來漂亮；它們本身就在講述自己從哪裡來的故事。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if visitors come to a place like Hong Kong, they may be
drawn to traditional snacks, handmade crafts, old-style tea sets, or products
linked to Cantonese culture. Those items do not just look nice; they tell a story
about where they come from.
[例如，如果遊客來到像香港這樣的地方，他們可能會被一些傳統小食、手工藝品、舊式茶具
，或者和粵語文化有關的產品吸引。這些東西不只是看起來漂亮；它們本身就在講述自己從
哪裡來的故事。]

components.english: So, in my view, traditional products attract tourists because they offer something genuine, memorable, and closely tied to the identity of a place.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，傳統產品之所以吸引遊客，是因為它們提供了一些真實、難忘，而且和一個地方身份緊密相連的東西。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in my view, traditional products attract tourists because they
offer something genuine, memorable, and closely tied to the identity of a place.
[所以，在我看來，傳統產品之所以吸引遊客，是因為它們提供了一些真實、難忘，而且和一
個地方身份緊密相連的東西。]

components.english: On top of that, I think traditional products appeal to tourists because they make better souvenirs and gifts than ordinary modern products.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為，傳統產品之所以吸引遊客，是因為和一般現代商品相比，它們往往更適合作為紀念品或禮物。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think traditional products appeal to tourists because
they make better souvenirs and gifts than ordinary modern products.
[除此之外，我也認為，傳統產品之所以吸引遊客，是因為和一般現代商品相比，它們往往更
適合作為紀念品或禮物。]

components.english: To be honest, many tourists want to take a little piece of the place home with them. A traditional product does that much better than something generic. It feels special, distinctive, and hard to replace. In other words, it is not just an object; it is a memory you can hold in your hands. That emotional value is a big part of the attraction.
components.chinese: 老實說，很多遊客都希望把那個地方的一小部分帶回家。而傳統產品往往比一般普通商品更能做到這一點。它會讓人覺得特別、有辨識度，而且不容易被取代。換句話說，它不只是一件物品；它更像是一段可以握在手中的回憶。這種情感價值，本身就是吸引力的重要來源。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, many tourists want to take a little piece of the
place home with them. A traditional product does that much better than
something generic. It feels special, distinctive, and hard to replace. In other
words, it is not just an object; it is a memory you can hold in your hands. That
emotional value is a big part of the attraction.
[老實說，很多遊客都希望把那個地方的一小部分帶回家。而傳統產品往往比一般普通商品
更能做到這一點。它會讓人覺得特別、有辨識度，而且不容易被取代。換句話說，它不只是
一件物品；它更像是一段可以握在手中的回憶。這種情感價值，本身就是吸引力的重要來

components.english: For instance, a tourist may buy a handmade item, a box of traditional pastries, or a piece of local artwork because it feels much more personal than buying something from an international chain store. When they give it to someone else, the gift already comes with a story attached.
components.chinese: 例如，一位遊客可能會買一件手工製品、一盒傳統糕點，或者一件本地藝術作品，因為這些東西比起從國際連鎖店買來的商品，會顯得更有個人味道。當他把它送給別人時，那份禮物本身就已經帶著一個故事。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a tourist may buy a handmade item, a box of
traditional pastries, or a piece of local artwork because it feels much more
personal than buying something from an international chain store. When they
give it to someone else, the gift already comes with a story attached.
[例如，一位遊客可能會買一件手工製品、一盒傳統糕點，或者一件本地藝術作品，因為這些
東西比起從國際連鎖店買來的商品，會顯得更有個人味道。當他把它送給別人時，那份禮物
本身就已經帶著一個故事。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say traditional products attract tourists because they combine cultural meaning, uniqueness, and emotional value. They do not just help people remember a trip; they let people carry a small piece of that place away with them.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說傳統產品之所以吸引遊客，是因為它們把文化意義、獨特性，以及情感價值結合在一起。它們不只是幫助人記住一趟旅程；它們更讓人能把那個地方的一小部分真正帶走。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say traditional products attract tourists because
they combine cultural meaning, uniqueness, and emotional value. They do not
just help people remember a trip; they let people carry a small piece of that place
away with them.
[總結來說，我會說傳統產品之所以吸引遊客，是因為它們把文化意義、獨特性，以及情感價
值結合在一起。它們不只是幫助人記住一趟旅程；它們更讓人能把那個地方的一小部分真
正帶走。]

question.english: Do you think there are benefits of traditional products to locals?
question.chinese: 你認為傳統產品對本地人有好處嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think there are benefits of traditional products to locals?
你認為傳統產品對本地人有好處嗎？

components.english: Yes, definitely. I think traditional products can bring real benefits to locals, both economically and culturally.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對有。我認為傳統產品確實能為本地人帶來真正的好處，無論在經濟上還是文化上都是如此。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, definitely. I think traditional products can bring real benefits to
locals, both economically and culturally.
[是的，絕對有。我認為傳統產品確實能為本地人帶來真正的好處，無論在經濟上還是文化
上都是如此。]

components.english: The main reason is that traditional products are not just objects for sale; they often carry local skills, local stories, and local identity. When people buy traditional food, crafts, clothing, or handmade items, they are not only buying a product; they are also supporting local workers and keeping older knowledge alive. In that sense, traditional products are a bit like cultural fingerprints: they remind people where they come from and what makes their community distinctive.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，傳統產品不只是拿來販賣的物件；它們很多時也承載著本地技藝、本地故事，以及本地身份認同。當人們購買傳統食品、手工藝品、服飾，或者手製用品時，他們不只是買了一件商品，同時也在支持本地工作者，並讓較舊的知識和技藝繼續活下去。從這個角度來說，傳統產品有點像文化上的指紋：它提醒人們自己來自哪裡，也提醒他們這個社區有甚麼與眾不同。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that traditional products are not just objects
for sale; they often carry local skills, local stories, and local identity. When
people buy traditional food, crafts, clothing, or handmade items, they are not
only buying a product; they are also supporting local workers and keeping older
knowledge alive. In that sense, traditional products are a bit like cultural
fingerprints: they remind people where they come from and what makes their
community distinctive.
[主要原因是，傳統產品不只是拿來販賣的物件；它們很多時也承載著本地技藝、本地故事
，以及本地身份認同。當人們購買傳統食品、手工藝品、服飾，或者手製用品時，他們不只是
買了一件商品，同時也在支持本地工作者，並讓較舊的知識和技藝繼續活下去。從這個角度
來說，傳統產品有點像文化上的指紋：它提醒人們自己來自哪裡，也提醒他們這個社區有甚
麼與眾不同。]

components.english: For example, if a town is known for traditional snacks, pottery, embroidery, or herbal products, local residents can earn income from producing and selling them. At the same time, younger people may become more interested in learning those skills instead of letting them fade into the background.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個地方以傳統小食、陶器、刺繡，或者草藥產品聞名，本地居民就可以透過製作和售賣這些東西去賺取收入。與此同時，年輕人也可能會因此對學習這些技藝更感興趣，而不是讓它們慢慢退到背景裡。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a town is known for traditional snacks, pottery,
embroidery, or herbal products, local residents can earn income from producing
and selling them. At the same time, younger people may become more interested
in learning those skills instead of letting them fade into the background.
[例如，如果一個地方以傳統小食、陶器、刺繡，或者草藥產品聞名，本地居民就可以透過製
作和售賣這些東西去賺取收入。與此同時，年輕人也可能會因此對學習這些技藝更感興趣，
而不是讓它們慢慢退到背景裡。]

components.english: So yes, I would say traditional products can benefit locals in a very practical way, because they can support livelihoods while also preserving a sense of cultural continuity.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說傳統產品能以一種很實際的方式讓本地人受益，因為它們既可以支持生計，也可以保存文化延續感。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say traditional products can benefit locals in a
very practical way, because they can support livelihoods while also preserving a
sense of cultural continuity.
[所以，是的，我會說傳統產品能以一種很實際的方式讓本地人受益，因為它們既可以支持
生計，也可以保存文化延續感。]

components.english: That said, I do not think traditional products are automatically beneficial in every case.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為傳統產品在每一種情況下都一定自動帶來好處。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think traditional products are automatically
beneficial in every case.
[不過，我並不認為傳統產品在每一種情況下都一定自動帶來好處。]

components.english: If they are treated only as tourist gimmicks, they can lose their soul. Sometimes traditions are packaged so heavily for sale that they become more show than substance. On top of that, if the products are overpriced or no longer useful in daily life, local people themselves may stop buying them. So the real benefit depends on whether the tradition still feels meaningful, authentic, and connected to the community.
components.chinese: 如果它們只是被當成吸引遊客的噱頭，那麼它們就可能慢慢失去靈魂。有時一些傳統會被過度包裝成商品，最後變得重表演、多於重實質。除此之外，如果這些產品價格太高，或者在日常生活中已經沒甚麼實際用途，本地人自己也可能不再願意購買。所以，真正的好處其實取決於這些傳統是否仍然有意義、夠真實，而且和社區本身保持連結。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: If they are treated only as tourist gimmicks, they can lose their
soul. Sometimes traditions are packaged so heavily for sale that they become
more show than substance. On top of that, if the products are overpriced or no
longer useful in daily life, local people themselves may stop buying them. So the
real benefit depends on whether the tradition still feels meaningful, authentic,
and connected to the community.
[如果它們只是被當成吸引遊客的噱頭，那麼它們就可能慢慢失去靈魂。有時一些傳統會被
過度包裝成商品，最後變得重表演、多於重實質。除此之外，如果這些產品價格太高，或者
在日常生活中已經沒甚麼實際用途，本地人自己也可能不再願意購買。所以，真正的好處其
實取決於這些傳統是否仍然有意義、夠真實，而且和社區本身保持連結。]

components.english: For instance, a handmade traditional item can be valuable if local families still use it, appreciate it, and pass it on. But if it is only produced for display and no one in the community truly cares about it anymore, then it may become little more than a souvenir with a pretty label.
components.chinese: 例如，一件手工製作的傳統用品，如果本地家庭仍然會使用它、珍惜它，甚至把它傳承下去，那它就很有價值。但如果它只是為了展示而被生產出來，而社區裡其實已經沒有人真正關心它，那它最後可能就只剩下一個包裝漂亮的紀念品而已。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a handmade traditional item can be valuable if local
families still use it, appreciate it, and pass it on. But if it is only produced for
display and no one in the community truly cares about it anymore, then it may
become little more than a souvenir with a pretty label.
[例如，一件手工製作的傳統用品，如果本地家庭仍然會使用它、珍惜它，甚至把它傳承下
去，那它就很有價值。但如果它只是為了展示而被生產出來，而社區裡其實已經沒有人真
正關心它，那它最後可能就只剩下一個包裝漂亮的紀念品而已。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say traditional products can absolutely benefit locals, especially by supporting jobs, pride, and cultural memory. In the end, truly living traditional products are not just things a place can sell; they are things that help a community keep its roots while still moving towards the future.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說傳統產品絕對可以讓本地人受益，尤其是在支持就業、建立自豪感，以及保存文化記憶方面。說到底，真正有生命力的傳統產品，不只是可以拿來賣的東西，而是能讓一個地方既保住根，也走向未來的東西。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say traditional products can absolutely benefit
locals, especially by supporting jobs, pride, and cultural memory. In the end,
truly living traditional products are not just things a place can sell; they are
things that help a community keep its roots while still moving towards the
future.
[總結來說，我會說傳統產品絕對可以讓本地人受益，尤其是在支持就業、建立自豪感，以及
保存文化記憶方面。說到底，真正有生命力的傳統產品，不只是可以拿來賣的東西，而是能
讓一個地方既保住根，也走向未來的東西。]

question.english: Do you think the government should help in the promotion of traditional products?
question.chinese: 你認為政府應該幫助推廣傳統產品嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think the government should help in the promotion of
traditional products?
你認為政府應該幫助推廣傳統產品嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do think the government should help to promote traditional products.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為政府應該幫助推廣傳統產品。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do think the government should help to promote traditional
products.
[是的，我的確認為政府應該幫助推廣傳統產品。]

components.english: The main reason is that traditional products are not just things people buy and sell; they also carry culture, history, and identity. A handmade craft, a traditional snack, or a local textile is often more than a product on a shelf. It is culture you can hold in your hands. If the government does nothing, many of these products can easily be pushed aside by cheap mass-produced goods, and once they disappear, a piece of local heritage disappears with them.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，傳統產品不只是買賣的商品而已；它們同時也承載著文化、歷史，以及身份認同。一件手工藝品、一種傳統小吃，或者一款本地布藝，很多時都不只是架上的貨品，而是你可以拿在手上的文化。如果政府甚麼都不做，很多這類產品就很容易被廉價的大量生產商品擠走，而一旦它們消失，隨之消失的往往也是一部分本地文化。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that traditional products are not just things
people buy and sell; they also carry culture, history, and identity. A handmade
craft, a traditional snack, or a local textile is often more than a product on a
shelf. It is culture you can hold in your hands. If the government does nothing,
many of these products can easily be pushed aside by cheap mass-produced
goods, and once they disappear, a piece of local heritage disappears with them.
[主要原因是，傳統產品不只是買賣的商品而已；它們同時也承載著文化、歷史，以及身份
認同。一件手工藝品、一種傳統小吃，或者一款本地布藝，很多時都不只是架上的貨品，而
是你可以拿在手上的文化。如果政府甚麼都不做，很多這類產品就很容易被廉價的大量生
產商品擠走，而一旦它們消失，隨之消失的往往也是一部分本地文化。]

components.english: For example, the government could organise cultural fairs, support local artisans, include traditional products in tourism campaigns, or help small producers reach a wider market. That kind of support can give old traditions a new lease of life instead of letting them gather dust in the corner.
components.chinese: 例如，政府可以舉辦文化市集、支持本地工藝師、把傳統產品納入旅遊宣傳，或者幫助小型生產者接觸更大的市場。這類支持能讓舊傳統重新活起來，而不是任由它們在角落裡慢慢積塵。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, the government could organise cultural fairs, support
local artisans, include traditional products in tourism campaigns, or help small
producers reach a wider market. That kind of support can give old traditions a
new lease of life instead of letting them gather dust in the corner.
[例如，政府可以舉辦文化市集、支持本地工藝師、把傳統產品納入旅遊宣傳，或者幫助小
型生產者接觸更大的市場。這類支持能讓舊傳統重新活起來，而不是任由它們在角落裡慢
慢積塵。]

components.english: So yes, I would say government support is important, because it can help traditional products survive in a fast-moving modern market.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說政府的支持是重要的，因為它能幫助傳統產品在節奏很快的現代市場裡生存下去。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say government support is important, because it
can help traditional products survive in a fast-moving modern market.
[所以，是的，我會說政府的支持是重要的，因為它能幫助傳統產品在節奏很快的現代市場
裡生存下去。]

components.english: That said, I do not think the government should promote traditional products blindly or endlessly.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為政府應該盲目地、無止境地推廣所有傳統產品。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think the government should promote traditional
products blindly or endlessly.
[不過，我並不認為政府應該盲目地、無止境地推廣所有傳統產品。]

components.english: Support should be smart, not sentimental. In my view, the government should help with branding, training, online promotion, design, and business opportunities, rather than simply pouring money into things without a future. Otherwise, a traditional product may stay alive on paper but die in real life. The goal should be to help it stand on its own two feet, not keep it on life support forever.
components.chinese: 支持應該是聰明的，而不只是感情用事。在我看來，政府應該協助品牌建立、培訓、網上推廣、設計，以及商業機會，而不是單純把錢不斷投進一些根本沒有未來的東西。否則，一件傳統產品也許在紙面上還活著，但在真實生活裡其實已經死了。真正的目標應該是幫助它自己站穩，而不是永遠替它吊著一口氣。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Support should be smart, not sentimental. In my view, the
government should help with branding, training, online promotion, design, and
business opportunities, rather than simply pouring money into things without
a future. Otherwise, a traditional product may stay alive on paper but die in real
life. The goal should be to help it stand on its own two feet, not keep it on life
support forever.
[支持應該是聰明的，而不只是感情用事。在我看來，政府應該協助品牌建立、培訓、網上推
廣、設計，以及商業機會，而不是單純把錢不斷投進一些根本沒有未來的東西。否則，一件
傳統產品也許在紙面上還活著，但在真實生活裡其實已經死了。真正的目標應該是幫助它
自己站穩，而不是永遠替它吊著一口氣。]

components.english: For instance, instead of giving endless subsidies to a traditional workshop, the government could help it modernise its packaging, tell its story better, and connect with younger customers. That way, the product remains traditional at heart, but not stuck in the past.
components.chinese: 例如，政府與其無止境地補貼一間傳統工坊，不如幫助它把包裝做得更現代、把自己的故事講得更好，並且和年輕消費者建立連結。這樣一來，那件產品在核心上仍然是傳統的，但又不至於被困在過去。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, instead of giving endless subsidies to a traditional
workshop, the government could help it modernise its packaging, tell its story
better, and connect with younger customers. That way, the product remains
traditional at heart, but not stuck in the past.
[例如，政府與其無止境地補貼一間傳統工坊，不如幫助它把包裝做得更現代、把自己的故
事講得更好，並且和年輕消費者建立連結。這樣一來，那件產品在核心上仍然是傳統的，但
又不至於被困在過去。]

components.english: Overall, I do think the government should help promote traditional products, because they preserve culture and support local livelihoods. However, the support should be practical and forward-looking. Tradition should be protected, yes, but it should also be given the chance to breathe, adapt, and remain relevant in the modern world.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我的確認為政府應該幫助推廣傳統產品，因為它們既能保存文化，也能支持本地生計。不過，這種支持應該是實際而且有前瞻性的。傳統固然需要被保護，但同時也應該被給予機會去呼吸、去適應，並在現代世界裡繼續有存在感。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I do think the government should help promote
traditional products, because they preserve culture and support local
livelihoods. However, the support should be practical and forward-looking.
Tradition should be protected, yes, but it should also be given the chance to
breathe, adapt, and remain relevant in the modern world.
[總結來說，我的確認為政府應該幫助推廣傳統產品，因為它們既能保存文化，也能支持本
地生計。不過，這種支持應該是實際而且有前瞻性的。傳統固然需要被保護，但同時也應該
被給予機會去呼吸、去適應，並在現代世界裡繼續有存在感。]

question.english: Do you think because of globalisation countries are adopting each other’s traditions?
question.chinese: 你認為因為全球化，各國都在互相吸收彼此的傳統嗎？

source_lines.text: Do you think because of globalisation countries are adopting each other’s traditions?
你認為因為全球化，各國都在互相吸收彼此的傳統嗎？

components.english: Yes, definitely. I do think globalisation has made countries adopt more and more of one another’s traditions.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對有。我確實認為，全球化令各國愈來愈多地吸收彼此的傳統。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, definitely. I do think globalisation has made countries adopt more
and more of one another’s traditions.
[是的，絕對有。我確實認為，全球化令各國愈來愈多地吸收彼此的傳統。]

components.english: The main reason is that people, media, and ideas now move across borders much more easily than they used to. Through travel, migration, films, social media, and international business, traditions no longer stay neatly in one corner of the world. Once people are exposed to another culture often enough, some customs naturally begin to travel, settle in, and take root. In that sense, traditions can spread a bit like seeds in the wind.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，現在人、媒體，以及想法跨越國界的速度和範圍，都比以前大得多。透過旅遊、移民、電影、社交媒體，以及國際商業往來，傳統已經不再安安靜靜地留在世界某一個角落。當人們頻繁接觸另一種文化時，一些習俗就很自然會開始流動、落地，甚至慢慢扎根。從這個角度來看，傳統有點像風中的種子一樣，會飄到別的地方生長。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that people, media, and ideas now move
across borders much more easily than they used to. Through travel, migration,
films, social media, and international business, traditions no longer stay neatly
in one corner of the world. Once people are exposed to another culture often
enough, some customs naturally begin to travel, settle in, and take root. In that
sense, traditions can spread a bit like seeds in the wind.
[主要原因是，現在人、媒體，以及想法跨越國界的速度和範圍，都比以前大得多。透過旅
遊、移民、電影、社交媒體，以及國際商業往來，傳統已經不再安安靜靜地留在世界某一個
角落。當人們頻繁接觸另一種文化時，一些習俗就很自然會開始流動、落地，甚至慢慢扎
根。從這個角度來看，傳統有點像風中的種子一樣，會飄到別的地方生長。]

components.english: For example, in Hong Kong, people celebrate things like Christmas and Halloween very enthusiastically, even though they are not originally local traditions. At the same time, in many places outside Asia, people have also become more familiar with Lunar New Year customs, such as red packets, festive food, or dragon and lion dances.
components.chinese: 例如，在香港，人們很熱烈地慶祝聖誕節和萬聖節，儘管它們本來並不是本地傳統。與此同時，在亞洲以外的很多地方，人們也愈來愈熟悉農曆新年的習俗，例如利是、節慶食品，以及舞龍舞獅。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in Hong Kong, people celebrate things like Christmas
and Halloween very enthusiastically, even though they are not originally local
traditions. At the same time, in many places outside Asia, people have also
become more familiar with Lunar New Year customs, such as red packets, festive
food, or dragon and lion dances.
[例如，在香港，人們很熱烈地慶祝聖誕節和萬聖節，儘管它們本來並不是本地傳統。與此
同時，在亞洲以外的很多地方，人們也愈來愈熟悉農曆新年的習俗，例如利是、節慶食品，
以及舞龍舞獅。]

components.english: So yes, I would say globalisation has clearly made traditions cross borders much more easily, and countries are definitely borrowing from one another more than before.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說，全球化的確令傳統更容易跨越國界，而各國之間也明顯比以前更常互相借用彼此的習俗。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say globalisation has clearly made traditions cross
borders much more easily, and countries are definitely borrowing from one
another more than before.
[所以，是的，我會說，全球化的確令傳統更容易跨越國界，而各國之間也明顯比以前更常
互相借用彼此的習俗。]

components.english: That said, I do not think countries simply copy each other’s traditions in a pure form. More often, they adapt them to fit local culture.
components.chinese: 不過，我不認為各國只是原封不動地照搬彼此的傳統。更多時候，它們其實會按照本地文化去調整和改造。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think countries simply copy each other’s traditions
in a pure form. More often, they adapt them to fit local culture.
[不過，我不認為各國只是原封不動地照搬彼此的傳統。更多時候，它們其實會按照本地文
化去調整和改造。]

components.english: When a tradition enters a new society, it usually changes shape a little. Local people keep the parts they enjoy, while quietly reshaping the rest to match their own values, habits, and lifestyle. So globalisation does not just create copying; it creates mixing. In other words, traditions do not simply move — they evolve.
components.chinese: 當一項傳統進入一個新的社會時，它通常都會稍微改變形狀。當地人會保留自己喜歡的部分，同時悄悄把其他部分調整成更符合自己價值觀、生活習慣，以及日常節奏的樣子。所以，全球化帶來的並不只是複製，而更多是一種混合。換句話說，傳統不只是移動了，它也在演變。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: When a tradition enters a new society, it usually changes shape
a little. Local people keep the parts they enjoy, while quietly reshaping the rest
to match their own values, habits, and lifestyle. So globalisation does not just
create copying; it creates mixing. In other words, traditions do not simply move
— they evolve.
[當一項傳統進入一個新的社會時，它通常都會稍微改變形狀。當地人會保留自己喜歡的部
分，同時悄悄把其他部分調整成更符合自己價值觀、生活習慣，以及日常節奏的樣子。所以
，全球化帶來的並不只是複製，而更多是一種混合。換句話說，傳統不只是移動了，它也在
演變。]

components.english: For instance, Christmas in some places may be less about religion and more about decorations, shopping, and gathering with family. Likewise, a festival borrowed from abroad may end up feeling half foreign and half local, almost like it has learned a new accent.
components.chinese: 例如，在某些地方，聖誕節也許已經不太著重宗教意義，反而更像是一個關於燈飾、購物，以及和家人相聚的節日。同樣地，一個從外地傳進來的節日，最後也可能會變得一半外來、一半本地，幾乎像是學會了一種新的口音。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, Christmas in some places may be less about religion
and more about decorations, shopping, and gathering with family. Likewise, a
festival borrowed from abroad may end up feeling half foreign and half local,
almost like it has learned a new accent.
[例如，在某些地方，聖誕節也許已經不太著重宗教意義，反而更像是一個關於燈飾、購物，
以及和家人相聚的節日。同樣地，一個從外地傳進來的節日，最後也可能會變得一半外來、
一半本地，幾乎像是學會了一種新的口音。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say yes, globalisation does encourage countries to adopt each other’s traditions, but what usually happens is not simple imitation. It is more like cultural blending. Traditions travel across borders, but they rarely arrive unchanged.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，是的，全球化的確會促使各國吸收彼此的傳統，但真正發生的通常不是簡單模仿，而更像是一種文化融合。傳統會跨越國界流動，但很少會以完全不變的樣子到達另一個地方。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say yes, globalisation does encourage countries to
adopt each other’s traditions, but what usually happens is not simple imitation.
It is more like cultural blending. Traditions travel across borders, but they
rarely arrive unchanged.
[總結來說，我會說，是的，全球化的確會促使各國吸收彼此的傳統，但真正發生的通常不
是簡單模仿，而更像是一種文化融合。傳統會跨越國界流動，但很少會以完全不變的樣子到
達另一個地方。]

question.english: Do you think traditional things from the past were of better quality than present-day things?
question.chinese: 你認為過去的傳統物品，品質比現代的東西更好嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think traditional things from the past were of better quality
than present-day things?
[你認為過去的傳統物品，品質比現代的東西更好嗎？]

components.english: In some cases, yes, I do think traditional things from the past were of better quality, especially when it comes to craftsmanship, durability, and attention to detail.
components.chinese: 在某些情況下，是的，我的確認為過去的傳統物品品質更好，尤其是在手工、耐用度，以及細節講究方面。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In some cases, yes, I do think traditional things from the past were of
better quality, especially when it comes to craftsmanship, durability, and
attention to detail.
[在某些情況下，是的，我的確認為過去的傳統物品品質更好，尤其是在手工、耐用度，以及
細節講究方面。]

components.english: A lot of older products were made more slowly and with more care, so they often feel sturdier and more solid. Whether it is furniture, clothing, or household items, many traditional things were built to last rather than built to sell fast. In that sense, they were not just products; they were almost made with a bit more soul in them. These days, some modern goods can feel a little too disposable, as if they are designed to be replaced before they are truly worn out.
components.chinese: 很多舊時代的產品都是慢慢做出來的，也更講究心思，所以它們往往給人一種更結實、更扎實的感覺。無論是家具、衣服，還是家居用品，很多傳統物品本來就是為了耐用而製作的，而不是為了快點賣出去。從這個角度來說，它們不只是商品，幾乎像是被放進了更多心血。相比之下，今天有些現代產品則會讓人覺得有點太「即棄」，好像還沒真正用舊，就已經準備被下一件取代了。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: A lot of older products were made more slowly and with more
care, so they often feel sturdier and more solid. Whether it is furniture,
clothing, or household items, many traditional things were built to last rather
than built to sell fast. In that sense, they were not just products; they were
almost made with a bit more soul in them. These days, some modern goods can
feel a little too disposable, as if they are designed to be replaced before they are
truly worn out.
[很多舊時代的產品都是慢慢做出來的，也更講究心思，所以它們往往給人一種更結實、更
扎實的感覺。無論是家具、衣服，還是家居用品，很多傳統物品本來就是為了耐用而製作的
，而不是為了快點賣出去。從這個角度來說，它們不只是商品，幾乎像是被放進了更多心
血。相比之下，今天有些現代產品則會讓人覺得有點太「即棄」，好像還沒真正用舊，就已經
準備被下一件取代了。]

components.english: For example, old wooden furniture or handmade leather goods often stay in good condition for decades if they are looked after properly. By contrast, some cheaper modern items may look attractive at first, but start falling apart far sooner than people expect.
components.chinese: 例如，一些舊式木製家具，或者手工皮具，只要保養得當，往往可以好好用上幾十年。相比之下，有些較便宜的現代產品一開始也許看起來不錯，但往往比人想像中更快出現損耗。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, old wooden furniture or handmade leather goods
often stay in good condition for decades if they are looked after properly. By
contrast, some cheaper modern items may look attractive at first, but start
falling apart far sooner than people expect.
[例如，一些舊式木製家具，或者手工皮具，只要保養得當，往往可以好好用上幾十年。相比
之下，有些較便宜的現代產品一開始也許看起來不錯，但往往比人想像中更快出現損耗。]

components.english: So yes, if we are talking about durability and craftsmanship, I would say many traditional things did have an edge.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，如果我們談的是耐用度和手工水準，我會說很多傳統物品的確更有優勢。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, if we are talking about durability and craftsmanship, I
would say many traditional things did have an edge.
[所以，是的，如果我們談的是耐用度和手工水準，我會說很多傳統物品的確更有優勢。]

components.english: That said, I would not say past things were better in every way, because modern products are often more convenient, safer, and more practical for everyday life.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不會說過去的東西在每一方面都比較好，因為現代產品很多時候更方便、更安全，也更適合日常生活。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I would not say past things were better in every way,
because modern products are often more convenient, safer, and more practical
for everyday life.
[不過，我也不會說過去的東西在每一方面都比較好，因為現代產品很多時候更方便、更安
全，也更適合日常生活。]

components.english: Modern products may not always have the same old-world charm, but they often do a better job in terms of technology, comfort, and efficiency. Today, people usually want things that are lighter, faster, easier to use, and more affordable. So while older things may win on character, modern things often win on function. In other words, the past may have built things like oak trees, but the present often builds things like racing bikes.
components.chinese: 現代產品也許未必總有那種舊時代的味道，但在科技、舒適度，以及效率方面，它們往往做得更好。今天的人通常都希望東西更輕、更快、更容易使用，而且更負擔得起。所以，過去的東西也許贏在個性和質感，而現代的東西則常常贏在功能性。換句話說，過去打造出來的東西像橡樹一樣厚實，而現在打造出來的東西則更像單車一樣輕快。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Modern products may not always have the same old-world
charm, but they often do a better job in terms of technology, comfort, and
efficiency. Today, people usually want things that are lighter, faster, easier to use,
and more affordable. So while older things may win on character, modern things
often win on function. In other words, the past may have built things like oak
trees, but the present often builds things like racing bikes.
[現代產品也許未必總有那種舊時代的味道，但在科技、舒適度，以及效率方面，它們往往
做得更好。今天的人通常都希望東西更輕、更快、更容易使用，而且更負擔得起。所以，過去
的東西也許贏在個性和質感，而現代的東西則常常贏在功能性。換句話說，過去打造出來的
東西像橡樹一樣厚實，而現在打造出來的東西則更像單車一樣輕快。]

components.english: For instance, an old appliance may have been built like a tank, but a modern one may save more energy, take up less space, and be much easier to operate. The same goes for clothing or tools: newer versions may not always last as long, but they can be more practical for the pace of modern life.
components.chinese: 例如，一件舊式電器也許做得像坦克一樣結實，但現代的款式可能更省電、更節省空間，而且操作起來容易得多。衣服和工具也是一樣：新的版本未必總是那麼耐用，但它們往往更適合現代生活的節奏。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, an old appliance may have been built like a tank, but a
modern one may save more energy, take up less space, and be much easier to
operate. The same goes for clothing or tools: newer versions may not always last
as long, but they can be more practical for the pace of modern life.
[例如，一件舊式電器也許做得像坦克一樣結實，但現代的款式可能更省電、更節省空間，
而且操作起來容易得多。衣服和工具也是一樣：新的版本未必總是那麼耐用，但它們往往更
適合現代生活的節奏。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say traditional things from the past were often better in terms of quality and longevity, but present-day things are usually better in terms of convenience and function.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說過去的傳統物品往往在品質和耐用度方面更勝一籌，但現代的東西通常在便利性和功能性方面表現更好。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say traditional things from the past were often better
in terms of quality and longevity, but present-day things are usually better in
terms of convenience and function.
[總結來說，我會說過去的傳統物品往往在品質和耐用度方面更勝一籌，但現代的東西通常
在便利性和功能性方面表現更好。]

question.english: Do you think it is necessary to protect traditions?
question.chinese: 你認為有必要保護傳統嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think it is necessary to protect traditions?
你認為有必要保護傳統嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do. I think it is necessary to protect traditions, because they help people stay connected to their culture, their history, and their sense of identity.
components.chinese: 是的，我認為有必要保護傳統，因為傳統能幫助人們和自己的文化、歷史，以及身份認同保持連結。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do. I think it is necessary to protect traditions, because they help
people stay connected to their culture, their history, and their sense of identity.
[是的，我認為有必要保護傳統，因為傳統能幫助人們和自己的文化、歷史，以及身份認同
保持連結。]

components.english: To me, traditions are a bit like roots. You may not look at them every day, but they help hold everything in place. Without them, a culture can start to feel thinner, flatter, and more forgetful. Traditions often carry values, stories, and ways of living that have been passed down over generations, so protecting them is not just about preserving old habits; it is also about protecting cultural memory.
components.chinese: 對我來說，傳統有點像樹根。你未必每天都會特別注意它們，但它們確實在幫整棵樹站穩。如果沒有傳統，一種文化就可能慢慢變得比較薄、比較淡，也比較容易忘記自己是誰。傳統往往承載著一代一代傳下來的價值觀、故事，以及生活方式，所以保護傳統不只是保存一些舊習慣；它同時也是在保存文化記憶。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: To me, traditions are a bit like roots. You may not look at them
every day, but they help hold everything in place. Without them, a culture can
start to feel thinner, flatter, and more forgetful. Traditions often carry values,
stories, and ways of living that have been passed down over generations, so
protecting them is not just about preserving old habits; it is also about
protecting cultural memory.
[對我來說，傳統有點像樹根。你未必每天都會特別注意它們，但它們確實在幫整棵樹站
穩。如果沒有傳統，一種文化就可能慢慢變得比較薄、比較淡，也比較容易忘記自己是誰。
傳統往往承載著一代一代傳下來的價值觀、故事，以及生活方式，所以保護傳統不只是保存
一些舊習慣；它同時也是在保存文化記憶。]

components.english: For example, traditional festivals, local food customs, and older art forms can give younger people a clearer sense of where they come from. If those things disappear completely, people may still move forward, of course, but they may do so with less of a link to the past.
components.chinese: 例如，傳統節日、地方飲食習俗，以及較古老的藝術形式，都能讓年輕一代更清楚知道自己從哪裡來。如果這些東西完全消失，人們當然仍然會繼續向前走，但他們和過去之間的連結可能就會少很多。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, traditional festivals, local food customs, and older art
forms can give younger people a clearer sense of where they come from. If those
things disappear completely, people may still move forward, of course, but they
may do so with less of a link to the past.
[例如，傳統節日、地方飲食習俗，以及較古老的藝術形式，都能讓年輕一代更清楚知道自
己從哪裡來。如果這些東西完全消失，人們當然仍然會繼續向前走，但他們和過去之間的連
結可能就會少很多。]

components.english: So yes, I would say protecting traditions is important, because they give a society continuity as well as character.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說保護傳統是重要的，因為傳統不只給一個社會延續性，也給它獨特的性格。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say protecting traditions is important, because
they give a society continuity as well as character.
[所以，是的，我會說保護傳統是重要的，因為傳統不只給一個社會延續性，也給它獨特的
性格。]

components.english: That said, I do not think every tradition should be protected blindly, just because it is old.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為每一種傳統都應該因為「它很古老」就被盲目保護。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think every tradition should be protected blindly,
just because it is old.
[不過，我也不認為每一種傳統都應該因為「它很古老」就被盲目保護。]

components.english: Some traditions are meaningful and worth keeping, but others may no longer fit modern life, or may even be unfair or harmful. Tradition should be a bridge, not a chain. In my view, the wiser approach is to protect the traditions that enrich people’s lives, while letting go of the ones that hold people back. After all, culture should breathe, not be locked in a glass box.
components.chinese: 有些傳統很有意義，也值得保留下來；但也有一些可能已經不再適合現代生活，甚至可能帶有不公平或有害的成分。傳統應該是一座橋，而不是一條鎖鏈。在我看來，更明智的做法是保護那些能豐富人們生活的傳統，同時放下那些會拖住人向前走的部分。畢竟，文化應該能呼吸，而不是被鎖進玻璃箱裡。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Some traditions are meaningful and worth keeping, but others
may no longer fit modern life, or may even be unfair or harmful. Tradition
should be a bridge, not a chain. In my view, the wiser approach is to protect the
traditions that enrich people’s lives, while letting go of the ones that hold people
back. After all, culture should breathe, not be locked in a glass box.
[有些傳統很有意義，也值得保留下來；但也有一些可能已經不再適合現代生活，甚至可能
帶有不公平或有害的成分。傳統應該是一座橋，而不是一條鎖鏈。在我看來，更明智的做法
是保護那些能豐富人們生活的傳統，同時放下那些會拖住人向前走的部分。畢竟，文化應該
能呼吸，而不是被鎖進玻璃箱裡。]

components.english: For instance, celebrating festivals, preserving traditional crafts, or keeping local languages alive can be very valuable. But if a custom clearly limits people’s freedom or dignity, then I do not think protecting it in the name of tradition makes much sense.
components.chinese: 例如，慶祝傳統節日、保存傳統工藝，或者讓地方語言繼續活下去，這些都很有價值。但如果某種習俗明顯限制了人的自由或尊嚴，那我就不覺得只因為它是傳統，就一定要把它保留下來。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, celebrating festivals, preserving traditional crafts, or
keeping local languages alive can be very valuable. But if a custom clearly limits
people’s freedom or dignity, then I do not think protecting it in the name of
tradition makes much sense.
[例如，慶祝傳統節日、保存傳統工藝，或者讓地方語言繼續活下去，這些都很有價值。但
如果某種習俗明顯限制了人的自由或尊嚴，那我就不覺得只因為它是傳統，就一定要把它
保留下來。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say yes, it is necessary to protect traditions, but with judgment rather than blind loyalty. The best traditions deserve to be carried forward, not because they are old, but because they still have life in them.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，是的，保護傳統是有必要的，但前提是要有判斷，而不是盲目忠於一切。最好的傳統值得被帶到未來，不是因為它們古老，而是因為它們到今天仍然有生命力。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say yes, it is necessary to protect traditions, but with
judgment rather than blind loyalty. The best traditions deserve to be carried
forward, not because they are old, but because they still have life in them.
[總結來說，我會說，是的，保護傳統是有必要的，但前提是要有判斷，而不是盲目忠於一
切。最好的傳統值得被帶到未來，不是因為它們古老，而是因為它們到今天仍然有生命力。]

question.english: How do most people travel long distances in your country?
question.chinese: 你國家裡，大多數人長途出行時通常會怎樣旅行？

source_lines.text: Topic: How do most people travel long distances in your country?
你國家裡，大多數人長途出行時通常會怎樣旅行？

components.english: In Hong Kong, I’d say most people usually travel long distances by plane, although trains and coaches also matter for places that are closer.
components.chinese: 在香港，我會說大多數人長途出行通常都是坐飛機，雖然如果目的地比較近，火車和長途巴士也同樣重要。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, I’d say most people usually travel long distances by
plane, although trains and coaches also matter for places that are closer.
[在香港，我會說大多數人長途出行通常都是坐飛機，雖然如果目的地比較近，火車和長途
巴士也同樣重要。]

components.english: The main reason is that Hong Kong is a small place, so if people are travelling a really long distance, they are often going overseas, and flying is simply the fastest and most practical option. When time matters, planes make the world feel much smaller. In that sense, air travel is not just convenient; it is almost the default choice for serious distance.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，香港地方不大，所以如果人們真的是去很遠的地方，很多時候其實就是去海外，而坐飛機自然是最快、也最實際的選擇。當時間很重要的時候，飛機會令人覺得世界好像一下子縮小了。從這個角度來看，航空交通不只是方便，而幾乎已經成了真正長途旅行的預設選項。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that Hong Kong is a small place, so if people
are travelling a really long distance, they are often going overseas, and flying is
simply the fastest and most practical option. When time matters, planes make
the world feel much smaller. In that sense, air travel is not just convenient; it is
almost the default choice for serious distance.
[主要原因是，香港地方不大，所以如果人們真的是去很遠的地方，很多時候其實就是去海
外，而坐飛機自然是最快、也最實際的選擇。當時間很重要的時候，飛機會令人覺得世界好
像一下子縮小了。從這個角度來看，航空交通不只是方便，而幾乎已經成了真正長途旅行的
預設選項。]

components.english: For example, if people are going to Japan, Thailand, Europe, or other places that are clearly far away, they would almost always choose to fly. Even for a relatively short holiday, most people would rather spend a few hours in the air than lose days on the road or at sea.
components.chinese: 例如，如果人們要去日本、泰國、歐洲，或者其他很明顯距離較遠的地方，他們幾乎都一定會選擇坐飛機。即使只是一個相對短的假期，大多數人也會寧願花幾個小時在空中，而不是把整整幾天耗在路上或海上。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if people are going to Japan, Thailand, Europe, or
other places that are clearly far away, they would almost always choose to fly.
Even for a relatively short holiday, most people would rather spend a few hours
in the air than lose days on the road or at sea.
[例如，如果人們要去日本、泰國、歐洲，或者其他很明顯距離較遠的地方，他們幾乎都一定
會選擇坐飛機。即使只是一個相對短的假期，大多數人也會寧願花幾個小時在空中，而不是
把整整幾天耗在路上或海上。]

components.english: So, generally speaking, planes are probably the main way people in Hong Kong travel long distances, simply because they save time and make faraway places feel reachable.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，飛機大概是香港人長途出行最主要的方式，原因很簡單，就是它節省時間，也讓遙遠的地方變得真正可以到達。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, generally speaking, planes are probably the main way people
in Hong Kong travel long distances, simply because they save time and make
faraway places feel reachable.
[所以，整體來說，飛機大概是香港人長途出行最主要的方式，原因很簡單，就是它節省時
間，也讓遙遠的地方變得真正可以到達。]

components.english: That said, if the distance is long but still within the region, many people would also use high-speed trains, cross-border coaches, or other public transport.
components.chinese: 不過，如果距離算長，但仍然是在區域之內，很多人也會坐高鐵、跨境巴士，或者其他公共交通工具。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, if the distance is long but still within the region, many
people would also use high-speed trains, cross-border coaches, or other public
transport.
[不過，如果距離算長，但仍然是在區域之內，很多人也會坐高鐵、跨境巴士，或者其他公共
交通工具。]

components.english: To be honest, not every long trip has to mean boarding a plane. If people are travelling to somewhere in mainland China or another nearby destination, trains and coaches can make more sense, especially when they are cheaper, easier, or less stressful. In other words, people usually choose whatever gets them there with the least fuss. Practicality often wins the day.
components.chinese: 老實說，並不是每一趟長途旅程都一定代表要上飛機。如果人們是去中國內地某些地方，或者一些比較近的目的地，火車和長途巴士有時反而更合理，特別是當它們更便宜、更方便，或者壓力更小的時候。換句話說，人們通常會選擇那種最省事的方法。很多時候，最後勝出的還是實際性。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, not every long trip has to mean boarding a plane.
If people are travelling to somewhere in mainland China or another nearby
destination, trains and coaches can make more sense, especially when they are
cheaper, easier, or less stressful. In other words, people usually choose whatever
gets them there with the least fuss. Practicality often wins the day.
[老實說，並不是每一趟長途旅程都一定代表要上飛機。如果人們是去中國內地某些地方，
或者一些比較近的目的地，火車和長途巴士有時反而更合理，特別是當它們更便宜、更方便
，或者壓力更小的時候。換句話說，人們通常會選擇那種最省事的方法。很多時候，最後勝
出的還是實際性。]

components.english: For instance, if someone is going to a nearby city for business or to visit relatives, they may choose rail instead of air because it feels more direct and less tiring overall. So even though flying dominates truly long-distance travel, other forms of transport still have their place.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人是去附近城市出差，或者探望親戚，他可能就會選擇坐鐵路而不是坐飛機，因為整體來說那樣會顯得更直接，也沒那麼累。所以，即使飛機在真正的長途旅行裡佔主導地位，其他交通方式仍然有它們自己的位置。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if someone is going to a nearby city for business or to
visit relatives, they may choose rail instead of air because it feels more direct and
less tiring overall. So even though flying dominates truly long-distance travel,
other forms of transport still have their place.
[例如，如果一個人是去附近城市出差，或者探望親戚，他可能就會選擇坐鐵路而不是坐飛
機，因為整體來說那樣會顯得更直接，也沒那麼累。所以，即使飛機在真正的長途旅行裡佔
主導地位，其他交通方式仍然有它們自己的位置。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people in Hong Kong mainly travel long distances by air, but the exact choice depends on where they are going, how much time they have, and how much convenience matters to them. For most people, the preferred route is usually the one that gets them far without making the journey feel longer than it has to be.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港人長途出行主要還是靠航空交通，但實際選擇仍然取決於他們要去哪裡、有多少時間，以及他們有多重視方便。對大多數人而言，最理想的方式通常就是那種能把人帶到遠方，卻又不會令路程顯得比必要更漫長的方法。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people in Hong Kong mainly travel long
distances by air, but the exact choice depends on where they are going, how
much time they have, and how much convenience matters to them. For most
people, the preferred route is usually the one that gets them far without making
the journey feel longer than it has to be.
[總結來說，我會說香港人長途出行主要還是靠航空交通，但實際選擇仍然取決於他們要去
哪裡、有多少時間，以及他們有多重視方便。對大多數人而言，最理想的方式通常就是那種
能把人帶到遠方，卻又不會令路程顯得比必要更漫長的方法。]

question.english: Have the types of transport people use changed much over the last few decades?
question.chinese: 在過去幾十年裡，人們使用的交通工具有很大改變嗎？

source_lines.text: Have the types of transport people use changed much over the last few decades?
在過去幾十年裡，人們使用的交通工具有很大改變嗎？

components.english: Yes, I think they have changed quite a lot over the last few decades, both in terms of the kinds of transport people use and the way they use them.
components.chinese: 是的，我認為在過去幾十年裡，交通工具的使用其實改變了不少，不只是人們用甚麼工具改變了，就連他們怎樣使用這些工具也變了。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I think they have changed quite a lot over the last few decades, both
in terms of the kinds of transport people use and the way they use them.
[是的，我認為在過去幾十年裡，交通工具的使用其實改變了不少，不只是人們用甚麼工具
改變了，就連他們怎樣使用這些工具也變了。]

components.english: The main reason is that modern life has become faster, more urban, and more connected, so transport has had to keep pace. In many places, people now have far more options than they used to, such as better public transport, cheaper flights, ride-hailing services, and various app-based travel tools. In that sense, transport has not just become faster; it has become smarter and more flexible as well.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，現代生活已經變得更快、更城市化，也更加互相連結，所以交通系統本身也必須跟上節奏。在很多地方，人們現在擁有的選擇比以前多得多，例如更完善的公共交通、更便宜的航班、網約車服務，以及各種以應用程式為基礎的出行工具。從這個角度來看，交通不只是變得更快了；它也同時變得更聰明、更有彈性。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that modern life has become faster, more
urban, and more connected, so transport has had to keep pace. In many places,
people now have far more options than they used to, such as better public
transport, cheaper flights, ride-hailing services, and various app-based travel
tools. In that sense, transport has not just become faster; it has become smarter
and more flexible as well.
[主要原因是，現代生活已經變得更快、更城市化，也更加互相連結，所以交通系統本身也必
須跟上節奏。在很多地方，人們現在擁有的選擇比以前多得多，例如更完善的公共交通、更
便宜的航班、網約車服務，以及各種以應用程式為基礎的出行工具。從這個角度來看，交通
不只是變得更快了；它也同時變得更聰明、更有彈性。]

components.english: For example, in a place like Hong Kong, people still rely heavily on buses, trains, and taxis, but the experience is very different from what it used to be. Nowadays, people can check arrival times on their phones, use digital payments, or switch easily between different forms of transport without too much hassle.
components.chinese: 例如，在像香港這樣的地方，人們當然仍然很依賴巴士、鐵路和的士，但整個使用體驗其實已經和以前很不一樣了。現在，人們可以直接用手機查看到站時間、使用電子支付，或者很方便地在不同交通工具之間切換，不需要像以前那樣麻煩。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in a place like Hong Kong, people still rely heavily on
buses, trains, and taxis, but the experience is very different from what it used to
be. Nowadays, people can check arrival times on their phones, use digital
payments, or switch easily between different forms of transport without too
much hassle.
[例如，在像香港這樣的地方，人們當然仍然很依賴巴士、鐵路和的士，但整個使用體驗其實
已經和以前很不一樣了。現在，人們可以直接用手機查看到站時間、使用電子支付，或者很
方便地在不同交通工具之間切換，不需要像以前那樣麻煩。]

components.english: So yes, I would say transport use has changed a great deal, mainly because people now travel in ways that are quicker, more convenient, and more integrated with technology.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說人們使用交通工具的方式的確改變了很多，主要是因為現在的出行方式更快、更方便，而且和科技結合得更緊密。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say transport use has changed a great deal, mainly
because people now travel in ways that are quicker, more convenient, and more
integrated with technology.
[所以，是的，我會說人們使用交通工具的方式的確改變了很多，主要是因為現在的出行方
式更快、更方便，而且和科技結合得更緊密。]

components.english: That said, I do not think the change is only about convenience; I also think people have become more aware of cost, traffic, and environmental issues.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為這些改變只是關乎方便而已；我同時覺得，人們對成本、交通擠塞，以及環境問題也變得更有意識了。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think the change is only about convenience; I also
think people have become more aware of cost, traffic, and environmental issues.
[不過，我也不認為這些改變只是關乎方便而已；我同時覺得，人們對成本、交通擠塞，以及
環境問題也變得更有意識了。]

components.english: To be honest, over the years, transport has become not just a practical issue, but also a social one. In some cases, people now choose public transport over private cars because it is cheaper, faster in crowded cities, or simply more environmentally responsible. On top of that, newer forms of transport, such as electric vehicles, shared mobility, and even cycling in some places, have started to gain more attention. In other words, the wheels are still turning, but the thinking behind them has shifted.
components.chinese: 老實說，這些年來，交通已經不只是單純的實際問題，也慢慢變成了一個社會議題。在某些情況下，人們現在會選擇公共交通而不是私家車，因為在擠迫的城市裡，前者更便宜、更快，或者單純就是對環境更負責任。除此之外，一些較新的交通方式，例如電動車、共享出行，甚至在某些地方的單車出行，也開始受到更多關注。換句話說，輪子仍然在轉，但背後的思維其實已經變了。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, over the years, transport has become not just a
practical issue, but also a social one. In some cases, people now choose public
transport over private cars because it is cheaper, faster in crowded cities, or
simply more environmentally responsible. On top of that, newer forms of
transport, such as electric vehicles, shared mobility, and even cycling in some
places, have started to gain more attention. In other words, the wheels are still
turning, but the thinking behind them has shifted.
[老實說，這些年來，交通已經不只是單純的實際問題，也慢慢變成了一個社會議題。在某些
情況下，人們現在會選擇公共交通而不是私家車，因為在擠迫的城市裡，前者更便宜、更快
，或者單純就是對環境更負責任。除此之外，一些較新的交通方式，例如電動車、共享出行，
甚至在某些地方的單車出行，也開始受到更多關注。換句話說，輪子仍然在轉，但背後的思
維其實已經變了。]

components.english: For instance, someone who might once have driven everywhere may now take the train for daily commuting, book a ride through an app when necessary, and only fly when they find a budget ticket. That kind of mixed travel habit was much less common in the past.
components.chinese: 例如，一個以前可能去哪裡都開車的人，現在也許會改為平日搭鐵路通勤、有需要時才用應用程式叫車，並且只在找到平價機票時才坐飛機。這種混合式的出行習慣，在以前其實沒有那麼普遍。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, someone who might once have driven everywhere
may now take the train for daily commuting, book a ride through an app when
necessary, and only fly when they find a budget ticket. That kind of mixed
travel habit was much less common in the past.
[例如，一個以前可能去哪裡都開車的人，現在也許會改為平日搭鐵路通勤、有需要時才用
應用程式叫車，並且只在找到平價機票時才坐飛機。這種混合式的出行習慣，在以前其實沒
有那麼普遍。]

components.english: Overall, I think the types of transport people use have changed a lot over the past few decades, not only because technology has moved forward, but also because people’s priorities have changed. Transport itself is certainly evolving, but in many ways, the bigger shift lies in how people think about getting from one place to another.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我認為在過去幾十年裡，人們使用的交通工具確實改變了很多，不只是因為科技進步了，也因為人們重視的東西改變了。交通工具本身當然在變，但更大的改變，很多時其實是人怎樣看待出行這件事。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I think the types of transport people use have changed
a lot over the past few decades, not only because technology has moved forward,
but also because people’s priorities have changed. Transport itself is certainly
evolving, but in many ways, the bigger shift lies in how people think about
getting from one place to another.
[總結來說，我認為在過去幾十年裡，人們使用的交通工具確實改變了很多，不只是因為科
技進步了，也因為人們重視的東西改變了。交通工具本身當然在變，但更大的改變，很多時
其實是人怎樣看待出行這件事。]

question.english: Do you think transport is likely to continue to improve in the future?
question.chinese: 你認為未來交通很可能會繼續改善嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think transport is likely to continue to improve in the future?
你認為未來交通很可能會繼續改善嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do think transport is likely to keep improving in the future.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為未來交通很可能會繼續改善。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do think transport is likely to keep improving in the future.
[是的，我的確認為未來交通很可能會繼續改善。]

components.english: The main reason is that modern life keeps putting pressure on transport systems to become faster, cleaner, and more efficient. Cities are getting busier, people are travelling more, and no one wants to waste half their life stuck in traffic or standing on overcrowded platforms. When a problem becomes big enough, innovation usually follows. In that sense, bad transport is often the mother of better transport.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，現代生活一直在迫使交通系統變得更快、更乾淨，也更有效率。城市愈來愈繁忙，人們移動得愈來愈多，而沒有人想把人生的一大半浪費在塞車裡，或者擠在擁擠的月台上。當一個問題變得夠大時，創新很多時候就會隨之而來。從這個角度來看，糟糕的交通往往正是更好交通的催生者。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that modern life keeps putting pressure on
transport systems to become faster, cleaner, and more efficient. Cities are getting
busier, people are travelling more, and no one wants to waste half their life
stuck in traffic or standing on overcrowded platforms. When a problem becomes
big enough, innovation usually follows. In that sense, bad transport is often the
mother of better transport.
[主要原因是，現代生活一直在迫使交通系統變得更快、更乾淨，也更有效率。城市愈來愈
繁忙，人們移動得愈來愈多，而沒有人想把人生的一大半浪費在塞車裡，或者擠在擁擠的月
台上。當一個問題變得夠大時，創新很多時候就會隨之而來。從這個角度來看，糟糕的交通
往往正是更好交通的催生者。]

components.english: For example, I think we are likely to see more electric vehicles, smarter traffic systems, better route-planning apps, and public transport that is more connected than it is now. Even small improvements, like more reliable arrival times or easier payment systems, can make daily travel feel much smoother.
components.chinese: 例如，我認為未來很可能會出現更多電動車、更聰明的交通管理系統、更好的路線規劃應用程式，以及比現在更互相連接的公共交通。即使只是一些看似細小的改善，例如到站時間更準確，或者付款系統更方便，也能令日常出行變得順暢得多。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, I think we are likely to see more electric vehicles,
smarter traffic systems, better route-planning apps, and public transport that is
more connected than it is now. Even small improvements, like more reliable
arrival times or easier payment systems, can make daily travel feel much
smoother.
[例如，我認為未來很可能會出現更多電動車、更聰明的交通管理系統、更好的路線規劃應
用程式，以及比現在更互相連接的公共交通。即使只是一些看似細小的改善，例如到站時間
更準確，或者付款系統更方便，也能令日常出行變得順暢得多。]

components.english: So yes, overall, I would say transport will probably continue to improve, because the demand for better mobility is not going away.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，整體來說，我會說交通大概會繼續改善，因為人們對更好流動方式的需求並不會消失。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, overall, I would say transport will probably continue to
improve, because the demand for better mobility is not going away.
[所以，是的，整體來說，我會說交通大概會繼續改善，因為人們對更好流動方式的需求並
不會消失。]

components.english: That said, I do not think improvement will always be smooth, equal, or automatic.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為這種改善一定會很順利、很平均，或者自然而然地發生。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think improvement will always be smooth, equal,
or automatic.
[不過，我並不認為這種改善一定會很順利、很平均，或者自然而然地發生。]

components.english: Better technology alone does not solve everything. Transport can become faster on paper but still remain expensive, crowded, or inconvenient in real life. Some places may move ahead quickly, while others are left behind. In other words, progress in transport is not just about speed; it is also about affordability, safety, comfort, and accessibility. If those things are ignored, a shiny new system can still leave people stranded in one way or another.
components.chinese: 單靠更好的科技並不能解決所有問題。交通也許在紙面上變快了，但在真實生活裡仍然可能很昂貴、很擠迫，或者不夠方便。有些地方可能進步得很快，但另一些地方卻會被拋在後面。換句話說，交通上的進步不只是關於速度，同時也關乎可負擔性、安全、舒適度，以及可達性。如果這些東西被忽略了，那麼一個看起來很先進的新系統，仍然可能以另一種方式把人困住。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Better technology alone does not solve everything. Transport
can become faster on paper but still remain expensive, crowded, or inconvenient
