in real life. Some places may move ahead quickly, while others are left behind. In
other words, progress in transport is not just about speed; it is also about
affordability, safety, comfort, and accessibility. If those things are ignored, a
shiny new system can still leave people stranded in one way or another.
[單靠更好的科技並不能解決所有問題。交通也許在紙面上變快了，但在真實生活裡仍然可
能很昂貴、很擠迫，或者不夠方便。有些地方可能進步得很快，但另一些地方卻會被拋在後
面。換句話說，交通上的進步不只是關於速度，同時也關乎可負擔性、安全、舒適度，以及可
達性。如果這些東西被忽略了，那麼一個看起來很先進的新系統，仍然可能以另一種方式把
人困住。]

components.english: For instance, a city might build a very modern railway line, but if it is too expensive, too crowded at rush hour, or badly linked to buses and neighbourhoods, many people will still feel that transport has not really improved in the way that matters.
components.chinese: 例如，一座城市也許建成了一條非常現代化的鐵路線，但如果它太貴、在繁忙時間太擠，或者和巴士及社區之間接駁得不好，那麼很多人仍然會覺得，交通其實並沒有在真正重要的地方改善。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a city might build a very modern railway line, but if it
is too expensive, too crowded at rush hour, or badly linked to buses and
neighbourhoods, many people will still feel that transport has not really
improved in the way that matters.
[例如，一座城市也許建成了一條非常現代化的鐵路線，但如果它太貴、在繁忙時間太擠，
或者和巴士及社區之間接駁得不好，那麼很多人仍然會覺得，交通其實並沒有在真正重要
的地方改善。]

components.english: Overall, I do think transport is likely to keep improving in the future, but the best kind of progress will not simply make travel faster; it will make it cleaner, safer, and easier for ordinary people to live with.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我的確認為未來交通很可能會繼續改善；不過，最理想的進步不應該只是讓出行變得更快，而是要讓它變得更乾淨、更安全，也更容易被普通人真正用得上。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I do think transport is likely to keep improving in the
future, but the best kind of progress will not simply make travel faster; it will
make it cleaner, safer, and easier for ordinary people to live with.
[總結來說，我的確認為未來交通很可能會繼續改善；不過，最理想的進步不應該只是讓出
行變得更快，而是要讓它變得更乾淨、更安全，也更容易被普通人真正用得上。]

question.english: Have the types of transportation people use changed much over the last few decades?
question.chinese: 在過去幾十年裡，人們使用的交通工具有很大改變嗎？

source_lines.text: Have the types of transportation people use changed much over the last few decades?
在過去幾十年裡，人們使用的交通工具有很大改變嗎？

components.english: Yes, I’d say they have changed quite a lot, although not always in a dramatic, overnight way.
components.chinese: 是的，我會說改變其實頗大，只不過很多時不是那種一夜之間翻天覆地的改變。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I’d say they have changed quite a lot, although not always in a
dramatic, overnight way.
[是的，我會說改變其實頗大，只不過很多時不是那種一夜之間翻天覆地的改變。]

components.english: In places like Hong Kong, people still rely heavily on buses, trains, taxis, and private cars, but the balance has shifted. Life is faster, cities are denser, and people now care much more about efficiency, cost, and convenience. On top of that, technology has changed the way people travel, because they can check routes instantly, compare options, and book transport with just a few taps. So the system may have the same bones, but it moves to a different rhythm now.
components.chinese: 在像香港這樣的地方，人們當然仍然非常依賴巴士、鐵路、的士，以及私家車，但整體比例和使用方式的確變了。生活節奏更快，城市也更密集，所以人們現在更重視效率、成本，以及方便程度。再加上科技改變了人們出行的方法，因為大家可以即時查路線、比較不同選擇，甚至只用幾下按鍵就預約交通工具。所以，整個系統雖然骨架還在，但它現在的運作節奏已經不一樣了。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In places like Hong Kong, people still rely heavily on buses,
trains, taxis, and private cars, but the balance has shifted. Life is faster, cities are
denser, and people now care much more about efficiency, cost, and convenience.
On top of that, technology has changed the way people travel, because they can
check routes instantly, compare options, and book transport with just a few
taps. So the system may have the same bones, but it moves to a different rhythm
now.
[在像香港這樣的地方，人們當然仍然非常依賴巴士、鐵路、的士，以及私家車，但整體比例
和使用方式的確變了。生活節奏更快，城市也更密集，所以人們現在更重視效率、成本，以
及方便程度。再加上科技改變了人們出行的方法，因為大家可以即時查路線、比較不同選擇
，甚至只用幾下按鍵就預約交通工具。所以，整個系統雖然骨架還在，但它現在的運作節奏
已經不一樣了。]

components.english: For example, a commuter who might once have taken one bus all the way may now combine the MTR, a short bus ride, and some walking, depending on traffic and time. In the same way, people are much more used to relying on phone apps to plan the quickest route instead of simply doing things out of habit.
components.chinese: 例如，一位上班族以前可能只是從頭到尾坐同一班巴士，但現在他可能會視乎交通和時間，把港鐵、短程巴士，以及步行結合起來。同樣地，現在人們也更習慣依靠手機應用程式去找最快路線，而不只是憑習慣照舊出門。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a commuter who might once have taken one bus all the
way may now combine the MTR, a short bus ride, and some walking, depending
on traffic and time. In the same way, people are much more used to relying on
phone apps to plan the quickest route instead of simply doing things out of
habit.
[例如，一位上班族以前可能只是從頭到尾坐同一班巴士，但現在他可能會視乎交通和時間
，把港鐵、短程巴士，以及步行結合起來。同樣地，現在人們也更習慣依靠手機應用程式去
找最快路線，而不只是憑習慣照舊出門。]

components.english: So yes, transport has changed quite a bit, especially in how flexible, connected, and time-sensitive it has become.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，交通方式的確改變了不少，特別是在它變得更靈活、更互相連接，也更講求時間效率這方面。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, transport has changed quite a bit, especially in how
flexible, connected, and time-sensitive it has become.
[所以，是的，交通方式的確改變了不少，特別是在它變得更靈活、更互相連接，也更講求時
間效率這方面。]

components.english: That said, I do not think the change is mainly about old forms of transport disappearing completely; it is more about new habits growing around them.
components.chinese: 不過，我不認為這種改變主要是因為舊有交通工具完全消失了；更準確地說，它其實是新的使用習慣慢慢圍繞著它們長出來。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think the change is mainly about old forms of
transport disappearing completely; it is more about new habits growing around
them.
[不過，我不認為這種改變主要是因為舊有交通工具完全消失了；更準確地說，它其實是新
的使用習慣慢慢圍繞著它們長出來。]

components.english: In other words, it is not as if buses vanished and everything suddenly turned futuristic. People still use the familiar options, but they use them in a smarter, more mixed way. A few newer forms, such as app-based ride services, have entered the picture, but the bigger shift is that journeys are now planned more like a puzzle than a straight line.
components.chinese: 換句話說，事情並不是那種巴士突然消失，然後一切都瞬間變得很科幻。人們仍然會用那些熟悉的交通工具，只不過現在用得更聰明，也更混合。一些較新的形式，例如透過應用程式預約的乘車服務，的確進入了日常生活，但更大的改變其實是：人們現在規劃行程時，更像是在拼圖，而不是走一條單一直線。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: In other words, it is not as if buses vanished and everything
suddenly turned futuristic. People still use the familiar options, but they use
them in a smarter, more mixed way. A few newer forms, such as app-based ride
services, have entered the picture, but the bigger shift is that journeys are now
planned more like a puzzle than a straight line.
[換句話說，事情並不是那種巴士突然消失，然後一切都瞬間變得很科幻。人們仍然會用那
些熟悉的交通工具，只不過現在用得更聰明，也更混合。一些較新的形式，例如透過應用程
式預約的乘車服務，的確進入了日常生活，但更大的改變其實是：人們現在規劃行程時，更
像是在拼圖，而不是走一條單一直線。]

components.english: For instance, when people travel longer distances now, they may be much more likely to fly than people were a few decades ago, while in daily life many city dwellers simply choose whichever option is fastest at that particular moment.
components.chinese: 例如，現在人們如果要走較長距離，選擇坐飛機的情況通常會比幾十年前普遍得多；而在日常生活裡，很多城市居民也只是直接選擇當下最快的那一種交通方式。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, when people travel longer distances now, they may be
much more likely to fly than people were a few decades ago, while in daily life
many city dwellers simply choose whichever option is fastest at that particular
moment.
[例如，現在人們如果要走較長距離，選擇坐飛機的情況通常會比幾十年前普遍得多；而在
日常生活裡，很多城市居民也只是直接選擇當下最快的那一種交通方式。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the types of transportation people use have changed a fair amount over the last few decades, but the bigger story is not just new vehicles; it is new behaviour. What has really changed is the way people piece their journeys together to match a faster, busier life.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，過去幾十年裡，人們使用的交通工具種類的確改變了不少，但更大的重點其實不只是出現了新工具，而是人們的出行習慣變了。真正改變的，是大家如何把一段段行程拼湊起來，以配合一種更快、更忙的生活節奏。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the types of transportation people use have
changed a fair amount over the last few decades, but the bigger story is not just
new vehicles; it is new behaviour. What has really changed is the way people
piece their journeys together to match a faster, busier life.
[總結來說，我會說，過去幾十年裡，人們使用的交通工具種類的確改變了不少，但更大的
重點其實不只是出現了新工具，而是人們的出行習慣變了。真正改變的，是大家如何把一段
段行程拼湊起來，以配合一種更快、更忙的生活節奏。]

question.english: What kinds of improvement have there been in transport in your country in recent years?
question.chinese: 近年來，你的國家的交通有了哪些改善？

source_lines.text: Topic: What kinds of improvement have there been in transport in your
country in recent years?
近年來，你的國家的交通有了哪些改善？

components.english: In Hong Kong, I’d say the biggest improvements have been better connections and smoother travel overall.
components.chinese: 在香港，我會說近年最大的交通改善，就是整體連接性更好了，而且出行也更順暢了。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, I’d say the biggest improvements have been better
connections and smoother travel overall.
[在香港，我會說近年最大的交通改善，就是整體連接性更好了，而且出行也更順暢了。]

components.english: The reason is that getting around the city feels less broken up than before. Some journeys that used to involve more awkward transfers or longer detours now feel more direct, and cross-border travel also seems easier and more natural than it did a few years ago. In that sense, the whole system feels a bit more joined-up.
components.chinese: 原因是，現在在城市裡移動，感覺上比以前少了一些零零碎碎的斷裂感。有些以前要轉很多次、或者要繞比較遠的路程，現在都變得更直接；而跨境出行也比前幾年顯得更方便、更自然。從這個角度來說，整個系統確實比以前更連成一體。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is that getting around the city feels less broken up
than before. Some journeys that used to involve more awkward transfers or
longer detours now feel more direct, and cross-border travel also seems easier
and more natural than it did a few years ago. In that sense, the whole system
feels a bit more joined-up.
[原因是，現在在城市裡移動，感覺上比以前少了一些零零碎碎的斷裂感。有些以前要轉很
多次、或者要繞比較遠的路程，現在都變得更直接；而跨境出行也比前幾年顯得更方便、更
自然。從這個角度來說，整個系統確實比以前更連成一體。]

components.english: For example, rail travel has become more convenient for some people, especially when going between the New Territories and Hong Kong Island, and people travelling to the Mainland also have more straightforward options now. Changes like that may not sound dramatic, but in everyday life they really do make a difference.
components.chinese: 例如，對某些人來說，鐵路出行現在更方便了，尤其是在新界和港島之間往返；而要去內地的人，現在也有更直接的選擇。這些改變聽起來也許不算非常戲劇化，但放在日常生活裡，的確很有分別。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, rail travel has become more convenient for some
people, especially when going between the New Territories and Hong Kong
Island, and people travelling to the Mainland also have more straightforward
options now. Changes like that may not sound dramatic, but in everyday life
they really do make a difference.
[例如，對某些人來說，鐵路出行現在更方便了，尤其是在新界和港島之間往返；而要去內
地的人，現在也有更直接的選擇。這些改變聽起來也許不算非常戲劇化，但放在日常生活裡
，的確很有分別。]

components.english: So, one clear improvement is that transport in Hong Kong has become more connected and more convenient in practical terms.
components.chinese: 所以，一個很明顯的改善就是，香港的交通在實際層面上變得更互相接通，也更方便了。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear improvement is that transport in Hong Kong has
become more connected and more convenient in practical terms.
[所以，一個很明顯的改善就是，香港的交通在實際層面上變得更互相接通，也更方便了。]

components.english: Another improvement, I think, is that transport has become smarter and less troublesome to use.
components.chinese: 我覺得另一個改善，就是交通系統變得更智能，也沒那麼麻煩了。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another improvement, I think, is that transport has become smarter
and less troublesome to use.
[我覺得另一個改善，就是交通系統變得更智能，也沒那麼麻煩了。]

components.english: It is not only about building roads or railways. It is also about making the whole experience more seamless. For instance, with newer tolling arrangements, drivers do not have to stop and fumble with payment in the old way. Small changes like that may seem unglamorous, but they help take the friction out of daily travel.
components.chinese: 這不只是關於興建道路或鐵路而已，同時也關乎怎樣令整個出行過程更流暢。舉例來說，隨著一些新的收費安排出現，司機已經不用再像以前那樣停下來慢慢處理付款。這些小改變表面上也許不算搶眼，但它們的確能把日常交通裡那些卡卡的地方磨平。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: It is not only about building roads or railways. It is also about
making the whole experience more seamless. For instance, with newer tolling
arrangements, drivers do not have to stop and fumble with payment in the old
way. Small changes like that may seem unglamorous, but they help take the
friction out of daily travel.
[這不只是關於興建道路或鐵路而已，同時也關乎怎樣令整個出行過程更流暢。舉例來說，
隨著一些新的收費安排出現，司機已經不用再像以前那樣停下來慢慢處理付款。這些小改
變表面上也許不算搶眼，但它們的確能把日常交通裡那些卡卡的地方磨平。]

components.english: For instance, if someone drives through tunnels regularly, that smoother payment system can save time and make the journey feel less annoying. Even for people who do not drive, they still benefit when the wider transport system becomes more efficient and less clogged up.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人經常駕車經過隧道，那種更順的付款方式就能省下一些時間，也讓整段路程沒那麼煩躁。即使是不開車的人，當整體交通系統變得更有效率、沒那麼容易堵塞時，他們其實也同樣會受惠。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if someone drives through tunnels regularly, that
smoother payment system can save time and make the journey feel less annoying.
Even for people who do not drive, they still benefit when the wider transport
system becomes more efficient and less clogged up.
[例如，如果一個人經常駕車經過隧道，那種更順的付款方式就能省下一些時間，也讓整段
路程沒那麼煩躁。即使是不開車的人，當整體交通系統變得更有效率、沒那麼容易堵塞時，
他們其實也同樣會受惠。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say transport in Hong Kong has improved by becoming more connected, more efficient, and more user-friendly. In the end, it feels less like a patchwork than it used to, and more like a system that is slowly learning how to move people with less fuss and more flow.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港的交通近年來之所以有改善，主要是因為它變得更互相接通、更有效率，也更方便使用。說到底，它已經沒有以前那麼像一塊一塊拼湊起來的東西，反而更像一個正慢慢學會如何用更少折騰、更多順暢去運送人們的系統。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say transport in Hong Kong has improved by
becoming more connected, more efficient, and more user-friendly. In the end, it
feels less like a patchwork than it used to, and more like a system that is slowly
learning how to move people with less fuss and more flow.
[總結來說，我會說香港的交通近年來之所以有改善，主要是因為它變得更互相接通、更有
效率，也更方便使用。說到底，它已經沒有以前那麼像一塊一塊拼湊起來的東西，反而更像
一個正慢慢學會如何用更少折騰、更多順暢去運送人們的系統。]

question.english: Do you think transportation is likely to continue to improve in the future?
question.chinese: 你認為未來交通很可能會繼續改善嗎？

source_lines.text: Do you think transportation is likely to continue to improve in the future?
你認為未來交通很可能會繼續改善嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do. Overall, I think transportation will probably continue to improve in the future.
components.chinese: 是的，我認為會。整體來說，我覺得未來交通大概會繼續改善。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do. Overall, I think transportation will probably continue to
improve in the future.
[是的，我認為會。整體來說，我覺得未來交通大概會繼續改善。]

components.english: The main reason is that modern life puts constant pressure on transport systems to become faster, cleaner, and more efficient. As cities grow and people become less willing to waste hours getting from A to B, governments and companies have every reason to keep upgrading the way people travel. In that sense, transportation is not standing still; it is always being pushed to move forward.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，現代生活一直在迫使交通系統變得更快、更環保，也更有效率。隨著城市擴大，而人們也愈來愈不願意把大量時間浪費在路途上，政府和企業自然都有很大動力去不斷改善人們出行的方式。從這個角度來看，交通並不是停在原地不動，而是一直被推著向前走。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that modern life puts constant pressure on
transport systems to become faster, cleaner, and more efficient. As cities grow
and people become less willing to waste hours getting from A to B, governments
and companies have every reason to keep upgrading the way people travel. In
that sense, transportation is not standing still; it is always being pushed to move
forward.
[主要原因是，現代生活一直在迫使交通系統變得更快、更環保，也更有效率。隨著城市擴
大，而人們也愈來愈不願意把大量時間浪費在路途上，政府和企業自然都有很大動力去不
斷改善人們出行的方式。從這個角度來看，交通並不是停在原地不動，而是一直被推著向前

components.english: For example, people can already see signs of that in everyday life: better public transport links, real-time travel updates, app-based route planning, and cleaner vehicles such as electric buses or trains. None of that feels like science fiction anymore; it already shows how travel can become smoother and smarter.
components.chinese: 例如，人們其實已經能在日常生活裡看見這種變化，例如更完善的公共交通接駁、即時交通資訊、用 app 規劃路線，以及像電動巴士或列車這類更清潔的交通工具。這些東西已經不再像科幻情節，而是真正顯示出出行怎樣能變得更順、更聰明。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, people can already see signs of that in everyday life:
better public transport links, real-time travel updates, app-based route
planning, and cleaner vehicles such as electric buses or trains. None of that feels
like science fiction anymore; it already shows how travel can become smoother
and smarter.
[例如，人們其實已經能在日常生活裡看見這種變化，例如更完善的公共交通接駁、即時交
通資訊、用 app 規劃路線，以及像電動巴士或列車這類更清潔的交通工具。這些東西已經不
再像科幻情節，而是真正顯示出出行怎樣能變得更順、更聰明。]

components.english: So yes, on the whole, I do think transportation is likely to keep improving, because the demand for better mobility is only getting stronger.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，整體來說，我的確覺得交通很可能會繼續改善，因為人們對更好出行方式的需求只會愈來愈強。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, on the whole, I do think transportation is likely to keep
improving, because the demand for better mobility is only getting stronger.
[所以，是的，整體來說，我的確覺得交通很可能會繼續改善，因為人們對更好出行方式的
需求只會愈來愈強。]

components.english: That said, I do not think improvement will happen evenly or automatically everywhere.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為這種改善會在所有地方都平均發生，更不會自動出現。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think improvement will happen evenly or
automatically everywhere.
[不過，我也不認為這種改善會在所有地方都平均發生，更不會自動出現。]

components.english: Transport does not improve by magic. It needs money, planning, space, and long-term thinking. If a city is overcrowded, poorly designed, or slow to invest, people may still face traffic jams, delays, and exhausting commutes. In other words, progress is likely, but it is not a straight line.
components.chinese: 交通改善並不是靠魔法自己出現的。它需要資金、規劃、空間，以及長遠思維。如果一座城市過度擠迫、設計欠佳，或者投資速度太慢，那麼人們仍然可能要面對擠塞、延誤，以及令人筋疲力盡的通勤。換句話說，進步很可能會發生，但它未必是一條筆直的路。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Transport does not improve by magic. It needs money,
planning, space, and long-term thinking. If a city is overcrowded, poorly
designed, or slow to invest, people may still face traffic jams, delays, and
exhausting commutes. In other words, progress is likely, but it is not a straight
line.
[交通改善並不是靠魔法自己出現的。它需要資金、規劃、空間，以及長遠思維。如果一座城
市過度擠迫、設計欠佳，或者投資速度太慢，那麼人們仍然可能要面對擠塞、延誤，以及令
人筋疲力盡的通勤。換句話說，進步很可能會發生，但它未必是一條筆直的路。]

components.english: For instance, a city may introduce better trains or buses, but if housing becomes more expensive and people are pushed farther and farther from where they work, the daily journey can still feel like a grind. So better technology alone does not guarantee a better experience.
components.chinese: 例如，一座城市也許會引入更好的列車或巴士，但如果住屋變得愈來愈昂貴，令大家被迫住得離工作地點愈來愈遠，那每天的通勤仍然可能讓人覺得十分折磨。所以，單靠更好的科技，並不一定保證更好的出行體驗。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a city may introduce better trains or buses, but if
housing becomes more expensive and people are pushed farther and farther
from where they work, the daily journey can still feel like a grind. So better
technology alone does not guarantee a better experience.
[例如，一座城市也許會引入更好的列車或巴士，但如果住屋變得愈來愈昂貴，令大家被迫
住得離工作地點愈來愈遠，那每天的通勤仍然可能讓人覺得十分折磨。所以，單靠更好的科
技，並不一定保證更好的出行體驗。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say yes, transportation will probably continue to improve in the future, but only if investment and planning keep pace with people’s needs. The future of transport may well be faster and cleaner, but the real test is whether it also becomes easier, fairer, and more human.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，是的，未來交通大概會繼續改善，但前提是投資和規劃真的能跟得上人們的需要。未來的交通也許會更快、更乾淨，但真正的考驗，是它能否同時變得更方便、更公平，也更有人味。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say yes, transportation will probably continue to
improve in the future, but only if investment and planning keep pace with
people’s needs. The future of transport may well be faster and cleaner, but the
real test is whether it also becomes easier, fairer, and more human.
[總結來說，我會說，是的，未來交通大概會繼續改善，但前提是投資和規劃真的能跟得上
人們的需要。未來的交通也許會更快、更乾淨，但真正的考驗，是它能否同時變得更方便、
更公平，也更有人味。]

question.english: What is your take on introducing transportation passes?
question.chinese: 你對推行交通月票或乘車通行證有甚麼看法？

source_lines.text: Topic: What is your take on introducing transportation passes?
你對推行交通月票或乘車通行證有甚麼看法？

components.english: Personally, I think introducing transportation passes is a very good idea overall, because they make daily travel simpler, cheaper, and less stressful for ordinary people.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為整體上推行交通月票或乘車通行證是一個很好的做法，因為它能令一般人的日常出行變得更簡單、更便宜，也更少壓力。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think introducing transportation passes is a very good idea
overall, because they make daily travel simpler, cheaper, and less stressful for
ordinary people.
[就我個人而言，我認為整體上推行交通月票或乘車通行證是一個很好的做法，因為它能令
一般人的日常出行變得更簡單、更便宜，也更少壓力。]

components.english: The main reason is that many people rely on public transport every single day, so even small savings and small conveniences can add up in a big way. Instead of paying for every journey one by one, people can travel with more peace of mind. In that sense, a transport pass does not just save money; it also takes some of the sting out of commuting.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，很多人每天都依賴公共交通工具，所以即使只是一些小小的節省和方便，累積起來也可以有很大分別。人們不用每一程都逐次付款，出行時也會更安心。從這個角度來看，交通通行證不只是省錢；它同時也能減輕通勤帶來的那種磨人感。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that many people rely on public transport
every single day, so even small savings and small conveniences can add up in a big
way. Instead of paying for every journey one by one, people can travel with more
peace of mind. In that sense, a transport pass does not just save money; it also
takes some of the sting out of commuting.
[主要原因是，很多人每天都依賴公共交通工具，所以即使只是一些小小的節省和方便，累
積起來也可以有很大分別。人們不用每一程都逐次付款，出行時也會更安心。從這個角度來
看，交通通行證不只是省錢；它同時也能減輕通勤帶來的那種磨人感。]

components.english: For example, if a student or office worker has to take the bus and the train every day, a monthly pass can make life much easier. They do not have to think so carefully about every single fare, and travelling starts to feel like part of a smooth routine rather than a constant drain on their wallet.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個學生或上班族每天都要搭巴士和地鐵，那麼一張月票就可以令生活輕鬆得多。他們不用每次出門都那麼仔細地盤算車費，而出行也會開始更像是一種順暢的日常節奏，而不是對荷包的持續消耗。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a student or office worker has to take the bus and
the train every day, a monthly pass can make life much easier. They do not have
to think so carefully about every single fare, and travelling starts to feel like part
of a smooth routine rather than a constant drain on their wallet.
[例如，如果一個學生或上班族每天都要搭巴士和地鐵，那麼一張月票就可以令生活輕鬆得
多。他們不用每次出門都那麼仔細地盤算車費，而出行也會開始更像是一種順暢的日常節
奏，而不是對荷包的持續消耗。]

components.english: So overall, I’m in favour of transportation passes, because they make mobility feel more affordable, more predictable, and more humane.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我是支持推行交通通行證的，因為它能令出行變得更可負擔、更有預算，也更有人性。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So overall, I’m in favour of transportation passes, because they
make mobility feel more affordable, more predictable, and more humane.
[所以，整體來說，我是支持推行交通通行證的，因為它能令出行變得更可負擔、更有預算，
也更有人性。]

components.english: On top of that, I think transportation passes can also benefit society as a whole by encouraging people to use public transport more often.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為交通通行證能透過鼓勵人們更常使用公共交通，為整個社會帶來好處。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think transportation passes can also benefit society as
a whole by encouraging people to use public transport more often.
[除此之外，我也認為交通通行證能透過鼓勵人們更常使用公共交通，為整個社會帶來好

components.english: If public transport becomes easier and more economical to use, more people are likely to leave the car at home and travel in a shared system instead. That can ease congestion, reduce pressure on roads, and make the city move a little more smoothly. In other words, a transport pass can act like a gentle push in the right direction.
components.chinese: 如果公共交通變得更容易使用，也更划算，那麼更多人就可能會把私家車留在家裡，轉而使用共享的交通系統。這樣可以紓緩擠塞、減輕道路壓力，也讓整個城市運作得更順一點。換句話說，交通通行證就像一個溫和但有效的推力，把大家推向更好的方向。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: If public transport becomes easier and more economical to use,
more people are likely to leave the car at home and travel in a shared system
instead. That can ease congestion, reduce pressure on roads, and make the city
move a little more smoothly. In other words, a transport pass can act like a
gentle push in the right direction.
[如果公共交通變得更容易使用，也更划算，那麼更多人就可能會把私家車留在家裡，轉而
使用共享的交通系統。這樣可以紓緩擠塞、減輕道路壓力，也讓整個城市運作得更順一點。
換句話說，交通通行證就像一個溫和但有效的推力，把大家推向更好的方向。]

components.english: For instance, if a city offers a reasonably priced transport pass, some commuters may begin using public transport not only for work, but also for errands, social activities, or weekend outings. Over time, that can help public transport become people’s first instinct rather than their last option.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個城市提供價格合理的交通月票，一些通勤人士就可能不只是在上班時使用公共交通，連買東西、社交活動，或者週末外出時也會更願意使用。久而久之，公共交通就更有機會成為人們的第一反應，而不是最後才考慮的選擇。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a city offers a reasonably priced transport pass, some
commuters may begin using public transport not only for work, but also for
errands, social activities, or weekend outings. Over time, that can help public
transport become people’s first instinct rather than their last option.
[例如，如果一個城市提供價格合理的交通月票，一些通勤人士就可能不只是在上班時使用
公共交通，連買東西、社交活動，或者週末外出時也會更願意使用。久而久之，公共交通就
更有機會成為人們的第一反應，而不是最後才考慮的選擇。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say transportation passes are a smart idea when they are well designed. The key, though, is fairness: if the price is too high or the coverage is too limited, the pass loses its appeal. But when it is affordable and practical, it can make everyday travel lighter for individuals and better for the city.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說只要設計得好，交通通行證是一個很聰明的做法。不過，關鍵在於公平性：如果價格太高，或者涵蓋範圍太窄，它的吸引力就會大打折扣。但如果它既實惠又實用，那麼它就能令個人的日常出行變得更輕鬆，也令整個城市變得更好。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say transportation passes are a smart idea when
they are well designed. The key, though, is fairness: if the price is too high or
the coverage is too limited, the pass loses its appeal. But when it is affordable
and practical, it can make everyday travel lighter for individuals and better for
the city.
[總結來說，我會說只要設計得好，交通通行證是一個很聰明的做法。不過，關鍵在於公平
性：如果價格太高，或者涵蓋範圍太窄，它的吸引力就會大打折扣。但如果它既實惠又實用
，那麼它就能令個人的日常出行變得更輕鬆，也令整個城市變得更好。]

question.english: How has transportation improved in recent years?
question.chinese: 近年來，交通有哪些改善？

source_lines.text: Topic: How has transportation improved in recent years?
[近年來，交通有哪些改善？]

components.english: I think transportation has improved quite a lot in recent years, especially in terms of convenience, speed, and overall ease of use.
components.chinese: 我認為，近年來交通其實改善了不少，尤其是在便利性、速度，以及整體使用體驗方面。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think transportation has improved quite a lot in recent years,
especially in terms of convenience, speed, and overall ease of use.
[我認為，近年來交通其實改善了不少，尤其是在便利性、速度，以及整體使用體驗方面。]

components.english: One big change is that getting around feels much smoother than it used to. In many places, including Hong Kong, transport is now better connected, so people can move from one part of the city to another with less confusion and less wasted time. Journeys feel more joined up than before. In that sense, travelling has become less like solving a puzzle and more like following a clear path.
components.chinese: 其中一個很大的改變是，現在出行比以前順暢得多了。在很多地方，包括香港，交通接駁都做得更好，所以人們從城市的一邊去到另一邊時，會少一點混亂，也少一點浪費時間。整個行程比以前更有連貫感。從這個角度來說，出行已經不再那麼像在解謎，反而更像沿着一條清晰的路前進。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: One big change is that getting around feels much smoother than
it used to. In many places, including Hong Kong, transport is now better
connected, so people can move from one part of the city to another with less
confusion and less wasted time. Journeys feel more joined up than before. In
that sense, travelling has become less like solving a puzzle and more like
following a clear path.
[其中一個很大的改變是，現在出行比以前順暢得多了。在很多地方，包括香港，交通接駁
都做得更好，所以人們從城市的一邊去到另一邊時，會少一點混亂，也少一點浪費時間。整
個行程比以前更有連貫感。從這個角度來說，出行已經不再那麼像在解謎，反而更像沿着一
條清晰的路前進。]

components.english: For example, in the past, some trips felt more tiring because people had to make awkward transfers or spend more time figuring out which route made the most sense. Now, many journeys feel more direct, so even if the distance is the same, the experience feels lighter.
components.chinese: 例如，以前有些路程之所以令人覺得累，不是因為真的太遠，而是因為要轉車轉得很麻煩，或者還要花很多時間去想到底走哪條路最合理。現在，很多行程都感覺更直接了，所以即使距離一樣，整體體驗也會顯得輕鬆一些。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in the past, some trips felt more tiring because people
had to make awkward transfers or spend more time figuring out which route
made the most sense. Now, many journeys feel more direct, so even if the
distance is the same, the experience feels lighter.
[例如，以前有些路程之所以令人覺得累，不是因為真的太遠，而是因為要轉車轉得很麻煩
，或者還要花很多時間去想到底走哪條路最合理。現在，很多行程都感覺更直接了，所以即
使距離一樣，整體體驗也會顯得輕鬆一些。]

components.english: So, one clear improvement is that transportation now feels more efficient and much less mentally draining than before.
components.chinese: 所以，其中一個很明顯的進步，就是現在的交通比以前更有效率，也更少消耗人的心力。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear improvement is that transportation now feels more
efficient and much less mentally draining than before.
[所以，其中一個很明顯的進步，就是現在的交通比以前更有效率，也更少消耗人的心力。]

components.english: On top of that, I think technology has made transportation much easier to manage in daily life.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為，科技讓日常出行變得更容易掌握了。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think technology has made transportation much
easier to manage in daily life.
[除此之外，我也認為，科技讓日常出行變得更容易掌握了。]

components.english: These days, people can check routes, arrival times, and traffic conditions on their phone before they even leave home. That makes a bigger difference than people sometimes realise. It gives people more control over their time and helps them avoid unnecessary stress. In other words, transport has not only become faster on the road; it has also become smarter in the hand.
components.chinese: 如今，人們甚至在出門前，就可以先在手機上查看路線、到站時間，以及交通狀況。這種改變有時比人們想像中更重要。它讓人對自己的時間更有掌控，也能避免很多不必要的壓力。換句話說，交通的進步不只是體現在路上更快了，也體現在手上的工具更聰明了。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: These days, people can check routes, arrival times, and traffic
conditions on their phone before they even leave home. That makes a bigger
difference than people sometimes realise. It gives people more control over their
time and helps them avoid unnecessary stress. In other words, transport has not
only become faster on the road; it has also become smarter in the hand.
[如今，人們甚至在出門前，就可以先在手機上查看路線、到站時間，以及交通狀況。這種改
變有時比人們想像中更重要。它讓人對自己的時間更有掌控，也能避免很多不必要的壓力。
換句話說，交通的進步不只是體現在路上更快了，也體現在手上的工具更聰明了。]

components.english: For instance, if I need to decide whether to take the train, a bus, or another route, I can usually work that out in a minute or two instead of just guessing and hoping for the best. That makes everyday travel feel more predictable and a lot less frustrating.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我需要決定應該坐鐵路、巴士，還是走另一條路，我通常只要一兩分鐘就可以判斷好，而不用像以前那樣靠猜，然後碰運氣。這會令日常出行變得更可預測，也少很多令人煩躁的感覺。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if I need to decide whether to take the train, a bus,
or another route, I can usually work that out in a minute or two instead of just
guessing and hoping for the best. That makes everyday travel feel more
predictable and a lot less frustrating.
[例如，如果我需要決定應該坐鐵路、巴士，還是走另一條路，我通常只要一兩分鐘就可以
判斷好，而不用像以前那樣靠猜，然後碰運氣。這會令日常出行變得更可預測，也少很多令
人煩躁的感覺。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say transportation has improved by becoming more connected, more user-friendly, and more intelligent. It is still not perfect, of course, especially during rush hour, but compared with the past, travelling now feels much more manageable.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說近年的交通進步主要在於它變得更連接、更容易使用，也更聰明了。當然，它仍然不是完美的，尤其是在繁忙時間，但和以前相比，現在的出行確實讓人覺得更容易應付。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say transportation has improved by becoming more
connected, more user-friendly, and more intelligent. It is still not perfect, of
course, especially during rush hour, but compared with the past, travelling now
feels much more manageable.
[總結來說，我會說近年的交通進步主要在於它變得更連接、更容易使用，也更聰明了。當
然，它仍然不是完美的，尤其是在繁忙時間，但和以前相比，現在的出行確實讓人覺得更容
易應付。]

question.english: What is your take on introducing transportation passes?
question.chinese: 你對推行交通月票或乘車通行證有甚麼看法？

source_lines.text: Topic: What is your take on introducing transportation passes?
你對推行交通月票或乘車通行證有甚麼看法？

components.english: Personally, I think introducing transportation passes is a very good idea overall, because they make daily travel simpler, cheaper, and less stressful for ordinary people.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為整體上推行交通月票或乘車通行證是一個很好的做法，因為它能令一般人的日常出行變得更簡單、更便宜，也更少壓力。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think introducing transportation passes is a very good idea
overall, because they make daily travel simpler, cheaper, and less stressful for
ordinary people.
[就我個人而言，我認為整體上推行交通月票或乘車通行證是一個很好的做法，因為它能令
一般人的日常出行變得更簡單、更便宜，也更少壓力。]

components.english: The main reason is that many people rely on public transport every single day, so even small savings and small conveniences can add up in a big way. Instead of paying for every journey one by one, people can travel with more peace of mind. In that sense, a transport pass does not just save money; it also takes some of the sting out of commuting.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，很多人每天都依賴公共交通工具，所以即使只是一些小小的節省和方便，累積起來也可以有很大分別。人們不用每一程都逐次付款，出行時也會更安心。從這個角度來看，交通通行證不只是省錢；它同時也能減輕通勤帶來的那種磨人感。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that many people rely on public transport
every single day, so even small savings and small conveniences can add up in a big
way. Instead of paying for every journey one by one, people can travel with more
peace of mind. In that sense, a transport pass does not just save money; it also
takes some of the sting out of commuting.
[主要原因是，很多人每天都依賴公共交通工具，所以即使只是一些小小的節省和方便，累
積起來也可以有很大分別。人們不用每一程都逐次付款，出行時也會更安心。從這個角度來
看，交通通行證不只是省錢；它同時也能減輕通勤帶來的那種磨人感。]

components.english: For example, if a student or office worker has to take the bus and the train every day, a monthly pass can make life much easier. They do not have to think so carefully about every single fare, and travelling starts to feel like part of a smooth routine rather than a constant drain on their wallet.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個學生或上班族每天都要搭巴士和地鐵，那麼一張月票就可以令生活輕鬆得多。他們不用每次出門都那麼仔細地盤算車費，而出行也會開始更像是一種順暢的日常節奏，而不是對荷包的持續消耗。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a student or office worker has to take the bus and
the train every day, a monthly pass can make life much easier. They do not have
to think so carefully about every single fare, and travelling starts to feel like part
of a smooth routine rather than a constant drain on their wallet.
[例如，如果一個學生或上班族每天都要搭巴士和地鐵，那麼一張月票就可以令生活輕鬆得
多。他們不用每次出門都那麼仔細地盤算車費，而出行也會開始更像是一種順暢的日常節
奏，而不是對荷包的持續消耗。]

components.english: So overall, I’m in favour of transportation passes, because they make mobility feel more affordable, more predictable, and more humane.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我是支持推行交通通行證的，因為它能令出行變得更可負擔、更有預算，也更有人性。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So overall, I’m in favour of transportation passes, because they
make mobility feel more affordable, more predictable, and more humane.
[所以，整體來說，我是支持推行交通通行證的，因為它能令出行變得更可負擔、更有預算，
也更有人性。]

components.english: On top of that, I think transportation passes can also benefit society as a whole by encouraging people to use public transport more often.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為交通通行證能透過鼓勵人們更常使用公共交通，為整個社會帶來好處。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think transportation passes can also benefit society as
a whole by encouraging people to use public transport more often.
[除此之外，我也認為交通通行證能透過鼓勵人們更常使用公共交通，為整個社會帶來好

components.english: If public transport becomes easier and more economical to use, more people are likely to leave the car at home and travel in a shared system instead. That can ease congestion, reduce pressure on roads, and make the city move a little more smoothly. In other words, a transport pass can act like a gentle push in the right direction.
components.chinese: 如果公共交通變得更容易使用，也更划算，那麼更多人就可能會把私家車留在家裡，轉而使用共享的交通系統。這樣可以紓緩擠塞、減輕道路壓力，也讓整個城市運作得更順一點。換句話說，交通通行證就像一個溫和但有效的推力，把大家推向更好的方向。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: If public transport becomes easier and more economical to use,
more people are likely to leave the car at home and travel in a shared system
instead. That can ease congestion, reduce pressure on roads, and make the city
move a little more smoothly. In other words, a transport pass can act like a
gentle push in the right direction.
[如果公共交通變得更容易使用，也更划算，那麼更多人就可能會把私家車留在家裡，轉而
使用共享的交通系統。這樣可以紓緩擠塞、減輕道路壓力，也讓整個城市運作得更順一點。
換句話說，交通通行證就像一個溫和但有效的推力，把大家推向更好的方向。]

components.english: For instance, if a city offers a reasonably priced transport pass, some commuters may begin using public transport not only for work, but also for errands, social activities, or weekend outings. Over time, that can help public transport become people’s first instinct rather than their last option.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個城市提供價格合理的交通月票，一些通勤人士就可能不只是在上班時使用公共交通，連買東西、社交活動，或者週末外出時也會更願意使用。久而久之，公共交通就更有機會成為人們的第一反應，而不是最後才考慮的選擇。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a city offers a reasonably priced transport pass, some
commuters may begin using public transport not only for work, but also for
errands, social activities, or weekend outings. Over time, that can help public
transport become people’s first instinct rather than their last option.
[例如，如果一個城市提供價格合理的交通月票，一些通勤人士就可能不只是在上班時使用
公共交通，連買東西、社交活動，或者週末外出時也會更願意使用。久而久之，公共交通就
更有機會成為人們的第一反應，而不是最後才考慮的選擇。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say transportation passes are a smart idea when they are well designed. The key, though, is fairness: if the price is too high or the coverage is too limited, the pass loses its appeal. But when it is affordable and practical, it can make everyday travel lighter for individuals and better for the city.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說只要設計得好，交通通行證是一個很聰明的做法。不過，關鍵在於公平性：如果價格太高，或者涵蓋範圍太窄，它的吸引力就會大打折扣。但如果它既實惠又實用，那麼它就能令個人的日常出行變得更輕鬆，也令整個城市變得更好。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say transportation passes are a smart idea when
they are well designed. The key, though, is fairness: if the price is too high or
the coverage is too limited, the pass loses its appeal. But when it is affordable
and practical, it can make everyday travel lighter for individuals and better for
the city.
[總結來說，我會說只要設計得好，交通通行證是一個很聰明的做法。不過，關鍵在於公平
性：如果價格太高，或者涵蓋範圍太窄，它的吸引力就會大打折扣。但如果它既實惠又實用
，那麼它就能令個人的日常出行變得更輕鬆，也令整個城市變得更好。]

question.english: What are the impacts of Uber on transportation in your country?
question.chinese: Uber 對你國家的交通帶來了甚麼影響？

source_lines.text: Topic: What are the impacts of Uber on transportation in your country?
Uber 對你國家的交通帶來了甚麼影響？

components.english: In Hong Kong, I’d say Uber has changed transport quite a lot, mainly by making point-to-point travel feel more flexible and more convenient.
components.chinese: 在香港，我會說 Uber 確實頗大程度地改變了交通，主要是令點對點出行變得更靈活，也更方便。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, I’d say Uber has changed transport quite a lot, mainly by
making point-to-point travel feel more flexible and more convenient.
[在香港，我會說 Uber 確實頗大程度地改變了交通，主要是令點對點出行變得更靈活，也更
方便。]

components.english: The main reason is that it has changed what people expect from a ride. People now tend to want something they can book quickly on their phone, track easily, and arrange without too much hassle. In that sense, Uber has helped push transportation in a more app-based and on-demand direction.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，它改變了人們對「叫車」這件事的期待。現在大家通常都想要一種可以很快在手機上預約、很容易掌握行程，而且安排起來不太麻煩的服務。從這個角度來說，Uber 的確把交通推向了一個更依賴應用程式、也更即時的方向。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that it has changed what people expect from
a ride. People now tend to want something they can book quickly on their
phone, track easily, and arrange without too much hassle. In that sense, Uber
has helped push transportation in a more app-based and on-demand direction.
[主要原因是，它改變了人們對「叫車」這件事的期待。現在大家通常都想要一種可以很快在
手機上預約、很容易掌握行程，而且安排起來不太麻煩的服務。從這個角度來說，Uber 的確
把交通推向了一個更依賴應用程式、也更即時的方向。]

components.english: For example, if someone is going out late at night, heading to the airport, or simply does not want to stand by the roadside waiting, Uber can feel like a much smoother option. Once people get used to that kind of convenience, it is hard to go back.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人深夜出門、要趕去機場，或者只是單純不想站在路邊等車，那麼 Uber 往往會讓人覺得順暢得多。人一旦習慣了這種方便，其實就很難再回到以前那種方式。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone is going out late at night, heading to the
airport, or simply does not want to stand by the roadside waiting, Uber can feel
like a much smoother option. Once people get used to that kind of convenience,
it is hard to go back.
[例如，如果一個人深夜出門、要趕去機場，或者只是單純不想站在路邊等車，那麼 Uber 往
往會讓人覺得順暢得多。人一旦習慣了這種方便，其實就很難再回到以前那種方式。]

components.english: So, one clear impact is that Uber has raised the bar for convenience and changed the way many people think about everyday travel.
components.chinese: 所以，其中一個很明顯的影響，就是 Uber 提高了人們對方便程度的要求，也改變了很多人看待日常出行的方式。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear impact is that Uber has raised the bar for
convenience and changed the way many people think about everyday travel.
[所以，其中一個很明顯的影響，就是 Uber 提高了人們對方便程度的要求，也改變了很多人
看待日常出行的方式。]

components.english: That said, I do not think the impact is entirely simple or entirely positive, because Uber has also brought more debate into the transport system.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為它帶來的影響是完全單純、或者完全正面的，因為 Uber 同時也把更多爭議帶進了交通系統裡。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think the impact is entirely simple or entirely
positive, because Uber has also brought more debate into the transport system.
[不過，我並不認為它帶來的影響是完全單純、或者完全正面的，因為 Uber 同時也把更多爭
議帶進了交通系統裡。]

components.english: Once a service like that becomes popular, people start asking bigger questions about fairness, safety, and regulation. It is no longer just about whether the app is convenient. It also becomes a question of how ride-hailing should fit into the wider transport system. In other words, Uber has not only changed how people travel; it has also changed the conversation around transport.
components.chinese: 當這類服務愈來愈受歡迎時，人們就會開始問一些更大的問題，例如公平、安全，以及應該怎樣監管。事情已經不只是「這個 app 方不方便」那麼簡單了；它還變成了一個關於網約車應該如何融入整體交通系統的問題。換句話說，Uber 不只是改變了人們怎樣出行，也改變了整個社會怎樣談論交通。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Once a service like that becomes popular, people start asking
bigger questions about fairness, safety, and regulation. It is no longer just about
whether the app is convenient. It also becomes a question of how ride-hailing
should fit into the wider transport system. In other words, Uber has not only
changed how people travel; it has also changed the conversation around
transport.
[當這類服務愈來愈受歡迎時，人們就會開始問一些更大的問題，例如公平、安全，以及應
該怎樣監管。事情已經不只是「這個 app 方不方便」那麼簡單了；它還變成了一個關於網約
車應該如何融入整體交通系統的問題。換句話說，Uber 不只是改變了人們怎樣出行，也改
變了整個社會怎樣談論交通。]

components.english: For instance, even traditional taxis are now more closely tied to app-based booking than before, which shows that Uber has nudged the whole system to adapt rather than just adding one extra option on the side.
components.chinese: 例如，就連傳統的士現在也比以前更緊密地和手機預約連在一起，這就說明 Uber 並不只是額外多提供了一個選擇，而是的確推動整個系統開始調整自己。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, even traditional taxis are now more closely tied to
app-based booking than before, which shows that Uber has nudged the whole
system to adapt rather than just adding one extra option on the side.
[例如，就連傳統的士現在也比以前更緊密地和手機預約連在一起，這就說明 Uber 並不只
是額外多提供了一個選擇，而是的確推動整個系統開始調整自己。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say Uber has made transport in Hong Kong feel more modern, more flexible, and more user-driven. At the same time, it has stirred up important questions about how the system should be run. In the end, its impact has been a bit like dropping a stone into still water: it began with one service, but the ripples have spread much more widely.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說 Uber 令香港的交通感覺上變得更現代化、更靈活，也更以使用者為中心；但與此同時，它也激起了不少關於整個系統應該如何運作的重要問題。說到底，它的影響有點像把一塊石頭扔進平靜的水面：起點也許只是一項服務，但漣漪卻擴散得遠遠超出了它本身。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say Uber has made transport in Hong Kong feel
more modern, more flexible, and more user-driven. At the same time, it has
stirred up important questions about how the system should be run. In the end,
its impact has been a bit like dropping a stone into still water: it began with one
service, but the ripples have spread much more widely.
[總結來說，我會說 Uber 令香港的交通感覺上變得更現代化、更靈活，也更以使用者為中心
；但與此同時，它也激起了不少關於整個系統應該如何運作的重要問題。說到底，它的影響
有點像把一塊石頭扔進平靜的水面：起點也許只是一項服務，但漣漪卻擴散得遠遠超出了
它本身。]

question.english: Do you think traffic jams would be reduced if people could travel on public transport for free?
question.chinese: 如果人們可以免費乘搭公共交通工具，你認為交通擠塞會減少嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think traffic jams would be reduced if people could travel on
public transport for free?
如果人們可以免費乘搭公共交通工具，你認為交通擠塞會減少嗎？

components.english: Yes, to some extent, I do think traffic jams would be reduced if public transport were free.
components.chinese: 是的，某程度上我認為，如果公共交通是免費的，交通擠塞的確會減少。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, to some extent, I do think traffic jams would be reduced if public
transport were free.
[是的，某程度上我認為，如果公共交通是免費的，交通擠塞的確會減少。]

components.english: The main reason is that cost does matter. If buses, trains, or trams were free, some people would be more willing to leave their cars at home, especially for daily commuting. For many people, if one option is cheaper, easier, and less stressful, that option starts to look much more attractive. In that sense, free public transport could tempt at least some drivers out of their cars.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，費用這件事的確很重要。如果巴士、火車或電車都變成免費，有些人就會更願意把私家車留在家裡，尤其是在每天上班上學的情況下。對很多人來說，只要其中一個選擇更便宜、更方便，而且壓力更小，那個選擇自然就會變得更吸引。從這個角度來看，免費公共交通的確可以把至少一部分司機從車裡拉出來。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that cost does matter. If buses, trains, or
trams were free, some people would be more willing to leave their cars at home,
especially for daily commuting. For many people, if one option is cheaper, easier,
and less stressful, that option starts to look much more attractive. In that sense,
free public transport could tempt at least some drivers out of their cars.
[主要原因是，費用這件事的確很重要。如果巴士、火車或電車都變成免費，有些人就會更
願意把私家車留在家裡，尤其是在每天上班上學的情況下。對很多人來說，只要其中一個選
擇更便宜、更方便，而且壓力更小，那個選擇自然就會變得更吸引。從這個角度來看，免費
公共交通的確可以把至少一部分司機從車裡拉出來。]

components.english: For example, if someone usually drives to work simply because fuel, parking, and transport costs add up in a complicated way, then a free and convenient train service might persuade them to switch. If enough people make that same choice, the roads would start to breathe a little more easily.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人平時開車上班，只是因為油費、泊車費和交通開支算起來太麻煩，那麼一個免費而方便的鐵路服務就可能說服他改搭公共交通。如果有足夠多的人都作出同樣選擇，馬路的壓力就的確能鬆一口氣。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone usually drives to work simply because fuel,
parking, and transport costs add up in a complicated way, then a free and
convenient train service might persuade them to switch. If enough people make
that same choice, the roads would start to breathe a little more easily.
[例如，如果一個人平時開車上班，只是因為油費、泊車費和交通開支算起來太麻煩，那麼
一個免費而方便的鐵路服務就可能說服他改搭公共交通。如果有足夠多的人都作出同樣選
擇，馬路的壓力就的確能鬆一口氣。]

components.english: So yes, free public transport could help reduce traffic congestion, because it would encourage at least some people to switch from private cars to shared transport.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，免費公共交通的確可以幫助減少交通擠塞，因為它會鼓勵至少一部分人由私家車轉用公共交通。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, free public transport could help reduce traffic congestion,
because it would encourage at least some people to switch from private cars to
shared transport.
[所以，是的，免費公共交通的確可以幫助減少交通擠塞，因為它會鼓勵至少一部分人由私
家車轉用公共交通。]

components.english: That said, I do not think it would solve the problem completely.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為這樣就能把問題徹底解決。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think it would solve the problem completely.
[不過，我並不認為這樣就能把問題徹底解決。]

components.english: Traffic jams are not caused by ticket prices alone. They also come from road design, the number of cars on the road, delivery vehicles, school runs, and people’s preference for privacy, comfort, and flexibility. If public transport is free but still crowded, slow, or poorly connected, many people will continue to drive anyway. A free seat on a bad system is still a bad ride.
components.chinese: 交通擠塞並不只是由車費造成的。它同時也和道路設計、路上的車輛數量、送貨車、接送小孩，以及人們對私隱、舒適和靈活性的偏好有關。如果公共交通雖然免費，但仍然擠迫、緩慢，或者接駁很差，那很多人最後還是會繼續開車。免費坐上一個不理想的系統，始終還是一趟不理想的旅程。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Traffic jams are not caused by ticket prices alone. They also
come from road design, the number of cars on the road, delivery vehicles, school
runs, and people’s preference for privacy, comfort, and flexibility. If public
transport is free but still crowded, slow, or poorly connected, many people will
continue to drive anyway. A free seat on a bad system is still a bad ride.
[交通擠塞並不只是由車費造成的。它同時也和道路設計、路上的車輛數量、送貨車、接送
小孩，以及人們對私隱、舒適和靈活性的偏好有關。如果公共交通雖然免費，但仍然擠迫、
緩慢，或者接駁很差，那很多人最後還是會繼續開車。免費坐上一個不理想的系統，始終還
是一趟不理想的旅程。]

components.english: For instance, if a parent has to drop off a child, then get to work, and the bus route is indirect and unreliable, free travel probably will not change much. In that case, convenience beats price.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一位家長要先送孩子，再趕去上班，而巴士路線又繞路又不可靠，那麼即使免費乘車，情況大概也不會改變太多。在這種情況下，方便始終比價錢更有分量。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a parent has to drop off a child, then get to work,
and the bus route is indirect and unreliable, free travel probably will not change
much. In that case, convenience beats price.
[例如，如果一位家長要先送孩子，再趕去上班，而巴士路線又繞路又不可靠，那麼即使免
費乘車，情況大概也不會改變太多。在這種情況下，方便始終比價錢更有分量。]

components.english: Overall, I would say free public transport could reduce traffic jams to a certain extent, but it is not a magic wand. If governments really want to clear the roads, public transport must be not only free, but also reliable, comfortable, and well-connected.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說免費公共交通確實可以在某程度上減少交通擠塞，但它並不是一支魔法棒。如果政府真的想讓道路變得更暢順，公共交通不只要免費，還要可靠、舒適，而且接駁完善。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I would say free public transport could reduce traffic
jams to a certain extent, but it is not a magic wand. If governments really want
to clear the roads, public transport must be not only free, but also reliable,
comfortable, and well-connected.
[總結來說，我會說免費公共交通確實可以在某程度上減少交通擠塞，但它並不是一支魔法
棒。如果政府真的想讓道路變得更暢順，公共交通不只要免費，還要可靠、舒適，而且接駁
完善。]

question.english: Why do you think more and more people prefer to travel by plane?
question.chinese: 你認為為甚麼愈來愈多人較喜歡坐飛機旅行？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do you think more and more people prefer to travel by plane?
你認為為甚麼愈來愈多人較喜歡坐飛機旅行？

components.english: I think the biggest reason is speed, because air travel saves people an enormous amount of time.
components.chinese: 我認為，最大的原因就是速度，因為坐飛機可以替人省下非常多時間。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the biggest reason is speed, because air travel saves people an
enormous amount of time.
[我認為，最大的原因就是速度，因為坐飛機可以替人省下非常多時間。]

components.english: These days, time has become a kind of luxury. People are busy with work, study, and family commitments, so they usually want the quickest way to get from one place to another. A plane can turn what would once have been a tiring, drawn-out journey into something much shorter and more manageable. In that sense, flying does not just shorten the trip; it almost makes the world feel smaller.
components.chinese: 現在這個時代，時間本身就像一種奢侈品。人們忙於工作、學業，以及家庭責任，所以通常都會想用最快的方法去到另一個地方。飛機可以把一段本來很累、很漫長的路程，變成短得多、也容易應付得多的事情。從這個角度來看，坐飛機不只是縮短了旅程，幾乎也把整個世界都縮小了。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: These days, time has become a kind of luxury. People are busy
with work, study, and family commitments, so they usually want the quickest
way to get from one place to another. A plane can turn what would once have
been a tiring, drawn-out journey into something much shorter and more
manageable. In that sense, flying does not just shorten the trip; it almost makes
the world feel smaller.
[現在這個時代，時間本身就像一種奢侈品。人們忙於工作、學業，以及家庭責任，所以通常
都會想用最快的方法去到另一個地方。飛機可以把一段本來很累、很漫長的路程，變成短得
多、也容易應付得多的事情。從這個角度來看，坐飛機不只是縮短了旅程，幾乎也把整個世
界都縮小了。]

components.english: For example, if someone only has a few days of holiday, they are far more likely to choose a flight than spend a huge chunk of their break on the road. If you can leave in the morning and be in another country by lunchtime, the choice starts to make itself.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人只有幾天假期，他當然會更傾向選擇坐飛機，而不是把大半個假期都花在路上。如果你早上出發，中午左右就已經到達另一個國家，那個選擇很多時候幾乎是自己在說話。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone only has a few days of holiday, they are far
more likely to choose a flight than spend a huge chunk of their break on the
road. If you can leave in the morning and be in another country by lunchtime,
the choice starts to make itself.
[例如，如果一個人只有幾天假期，他當然會更傾向選擇坐飛機，而不是把大半個假期都花
在路上。如果你早上出發，中午左右就已經到達另一個國家，那個選擇很多時候幾乎是自己
在說話。]

components.english: So, one very clear reason people prefer planes is that modern life moves quickly, and flying fits that rhythm perfectly.
components.chinese: 所以，人們偏好坐飛機的一個很明顯原因，就是現代生活節奏本來就很快，而飛行正好非常貼合這種節奏。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one very clear reason people prefer planes is that modern life
moves quickly, and flying fits that rhythm perfectly.
[所以，人們偏好坐飛機的一個很明顯原因，就是現代生活節奏本來就很快，而飛行正好非
常貼合這種節奏。]

components.english: On top of that, I think more people fly now because air travel feels more accessible and more normal than it used to.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為，現在更多人坐飛機，是因為飛行比以前更容易接觸，也更像一種日常化的選擇。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think more people fly now because air travel feels
more accessible and more normal than it used to.
[除此之外，我也認為，現在更多人坐飛機，是因為飛行比以前更容易接觸，也更像一種日
常化的選擇。]

components.english: In the past, flying often felt like something special or even slightly out of reach. Now it feels much more woven into ordinary life. Booking is easier, travel information is everywhere, and people are more used to the idea of going abroad for holidays, work, or study. Once flying stops feeling like a grand event and starts feeling like a practical option, naturally more people will choose it. A plane may not always be the most charming way to travel, but it is very often the most efficient.
components.chinese: 在以前，坐飛機很多時會讓人覺得是一件很特別，甚至有點遙不可及的事情。現在，它卻更像是日常生活的一部分。訂票更方便了，旅遊資訊到處都有，而人們也更習慣為了放假、工作，或者讀書而出國。當坐飛機不再像一件很隆重的大事，而開始像一個實際可行的選項時，自然就會有更多人選擇它。飛機未必總是最有味道的旅行方式，但很多時候，它的確是最高效率的。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: In the past, flying often felt like something special or even
slightly out of reach. Now it feels much more woven into ordinary life. Booking
is easier, travel information is everywhere, and people are more used to the idea
of going abroad for holidays, work, or study. Once flying stops feeling like a
grand event and starts feeling like a practical option, naturally more people will
choose it. A plane may not always be the most charming way to travel, but it is
very often the most efficient.
[在以前，坐飛機很多時會讓人覺得是一件很特別，甚至有點遙不可及的事情。現在，它卻
更像是日常生活的一部分。訂票更方便了，旅遊資訊到處都有，而人們也更習慣為了放假、
工作，或者讀書而出國。當坐飛機不再像一件很隆重的大事，而開始像一個實際可行的選項
時，自然就會有更多人選擇它。飛機未必總是最有味道的旅行方式，但很多時候，它的確是
最高效率的。]

components.english: For instance, a family planning a short trip, a student going overseas, or a businessperson attending meetings abroad will usually see flying as the most sensible option. Even if it costs a bit more than another form of transport, the time saved often makes it feel worthwhile.
components.chinese: 例如，一個家庭安排短途旅行、一位學生要到海外，或者一位商務人士要去外地開會，通常都會把飛行看成最合理的選擇。即使它有時比其他交通方式稍微貴一點，但省下來的時間往往已經足以令人覺得很值得。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a family planning a short trip, a student going
overseas, or a businessperson attending meetings abroad will usually see flying
as the most sensible option. Even if it costs a bit more than another form of
transport, the time saved often makes it feel worthwhile.
[例如，一個家庭安排短途旅行、一位學生要到海外，或者一位商務人士要去外地開會，通
常都會把飛行看成最合理的選擇。即使它有時比其他交通方式稍微貴一點，但省下來的時
間往往已經足以令人覺得很值得。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say more and more people prefer to travel by plane because it combines speed, convenience, and reach. In a world where people want to go farther in less time, flying often feels like the obvious answer.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，愈來愈多人喜歡坐飛機旅行，是因為它結合了速度、方便，以及跨地域的能力。在一個大家都想用更少時間走得更遠的世界裡，飛行很多時候就像一個再自然不過的答案。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say more and more people prefer to travel by plane
because it combines speed, convenience, and reach. In a world where people
want to go farther in less time, flying often feels like the obvious answer.
[總結來說，我會說，愈來愈多人喜歡坐飛機旅行，是因為它結合了速度、方便，以及跨地域
的能力。在一個大家都想用更少時間走得更遠的世界裡，飛行很多時候就像一個再自然不
過的答案。]

question.english: What kinds of obstacles do you think some local governments have to face when they develop transportation infrastructure?
question.chinese: 你認為地方政府在發展交通基建時，通常要面對哪些障礙？

source_lines.text: Topic: What kinds of obstacles do you think some local governments have to
face when they develop transportation infrastructure?
[你認為地方政府在發展交通基建時，通常要面對哪些障礙？]

components.english: I think one of the biggest obstacles is the sheer cost and complexity of the work, because transportation infrastructure is never cheap, quick, or simple.
components.chinese: 我認為，其中一個最大的障礙就是工程本身龐大的成本和複雜性，因為交通基建從來都不便宜、不快速，也不簡單。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one of the biggest obstacles is the sheer cost and complexity of
the work, because transportation infrastructure is never cheap, quick, or simple.
[我認為，其中一個最大的障礙就是工程本身龐大的成本和複雜性，因為交通基建從來都不
便宜、不快速，也不簡單。]

components.english: Building roads, rail lines, bridges, or stations usually takes a huge amount of money, long-term planning, and coordination between many different departments. On top of that, local governments often have to work within tight budgets, limited land, and existing urban layouts that were never designed for modern traffic demands. In other words, they are often trying to repair the roof while still living in the house.
components.chinese: 興建道路、鐵路、橋樑，或者車站，通常都需要非常龐大的資金、長期規劃，以及不同部門之間的協調。除此之外，地方政府往往還要在緊絀的預算、有限的土地，以及原本就不是為現代交通需求而設計的城市結構之中工作。換句話說，他們很多時候就像是一邊住在屋裡，一邊還要修屋頂。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Building roads, rail lines, bridges, or stations usually takes a
huge amount of money, long-term planning, and coordination between many
different departments. On top of that, local governments often have to work
within tight budgets, limited land, and existing urban layouts that were never
designed for modern traffic demands. In other words, they are often trying to
repair the roof while still living in the house.
[興建道路、鐵路、橋樑，或者車站，通常都需要非常龐大的資金、長期規劃，以及不同部門
之間的協調。除此之外，地方政府往往還要在緊絀的預算、有限的土地，以及原本就不是為
現代交通需求而設計的城市結構之中工作。換句話說，他們很多時候就像是一邊住在屋裡，
一邊還要修屋頂。]

components.english: For example, if a city wants to build a new rail line, it may have to deal with land acquisition, underground utilities, traffic diversion, and construction delays all at once. Even before the first train runs, the government may already be facing rising costs and public complaints about noise, dust, and disruption.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個城市想興建新的鐵路線，它可能同時要處理收地、地下管線、交通改道，以及工程延誤等問題。甚至在第一班列車真正行駛之前，政府可能就已經要面對成本上升，以及市民對噪音、灰塵和施工影響的抱怨。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a city wants to build a new rail line, it may have to
deal with land acquisition, underground utilities, traffic diversion, and
construction delays all at once. Even before the first train runs, the government
may already be facing rising costs and public complaints about noise, dust, and
disruption.
[例如，如果一個城市想興建新的鐵路線，它可能同時要處理收地、地下管線、交通改道，以
及工程延誤等問題。甚至在第一班列車真正行駛之前，政府可能就已經要面對成本上升，以
及市民對噪音、灰塵和施工影響的抱怨。]

components.english: So, to me, one major obstacle is that transport projects are expensive and technically difficult, and the challenges start long before the public sees any benefit.
components.chinese: 所以，對我來說，其中一個主要障礙就是交通工程既昂貴又技術複雜，而且很多挑戰在市民真正感受到好處之前就已經開始了。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to me, one major obstacle is that transport projects are
expensive and technically difficult, and the challenges start long before the
public sees any benefit.
[所以，對我來說，其中一個主要障礙就是交通工程既昂貴又技術複雜，而且很多挑戰在市
民真正感受到好處之前就已經開始了。]

components.english: Another major obstacle is public resistance, because people usually want better transport in the long run, but not always the disruption that comes with it in the short run.
components.chinese: 另一個主要障礙就是公眾阻力，因為人們通常都希望長遠有更好的交通，但未必願意承受短期內隨之而來的干擾。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another major obstacle is public resistance, because people usually
want better transport in the long run, but not always the disruption that comes
with it in the short run.
[另一個主要障礙就是公眾阻力，因為人們通常都希望長遠有更好的交通，但未必願意承受
短期內隨之而來的干擾。]

components.english: Infrastructure development is often a balancing act between public interest and public patience. Some residents may worry about noise, traffic changes, environmental damage, or the loss of familiar neighbourhood spaces. Others may feel that the project is unfairly benefiting one area while burdening another. So even when the project makes sense on paper, it can still run into political pressure, local opposition, and endless debate. Progress may look straightforward on a map, but in real life it has to pass through people’s lives.
components.chinese: 基建發展很多時候其實是一場在公共利益和公眾耐性之間取得平衡的過程。有些居民可能會擔心噪音、交通改變、環境破壞，或者熟悉社區空間的流失。另一些人則可能覺得，這個項目把好處給了某一區，卻把負擔丟給另一區。所以，即使工程在紙面上看來很合理，它仍然可能遇上政治壓力、本地反對聲音，以及沒完沒了的爭論。進步在地圖上看起來也許是一條直線，但在現實裡，它必須穿過人們的生活。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Infrastructure development is often a balancing act between
public interest and public patience. Some residents may worry about noise,
traffic changes, environmental damage, or the loss of familiar neighbourhood
spaces. Others may feel that the project is unfairly benefiting one area while
burdening another. So even when the project makes sense on paper, it can still
run into political pressure, local opposition, and endless debate. Progress may
look straightforward on a map, but in real life it has to pass through people’s
lives.
[基建發展很多時候其實是一場在公共利益和公眾耐性之間取得平衡的過程。有些居民可
能會擔心噪音、交通改變、環境破壞，或者熟悉社區空間的流失。另一些人則可能覺得，這
個項目把好處給了某一區，卻把負擔丟給另一區。所以，即使工程在紙面上看來很合理，它
仍然可能遇上政治壓力、本地反對聲音，以及沒完沒了的爭論。進步在地圖上看起來也許是
一條直線，但在現實裡，它必須穿過人們的生活。]

components.english: For instance, if a government plans to widen a road or build a new station, shop owners may worry about losing business during construction, nearby residents may complain about years of inconvenience, and environmental groups may raise concerns about long-term damage. In that case, the real battle is not only engineering; it is also persuasion.
components.chinese: 例如，如果政府打算擴闊道路，或者興建新車站，商戶可能會擔心施工期間生意受影響，附近居民可能會抱怨多年不便，而環保團體也可能會對長遠破壞提出質疑。在這種情況下，真正的挑戰不只是工程本身，也同樣是說服工作。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a government plans to widen a road or build a new
station, shop owners may worry about losing business during construction,
nearby residents may complain about years of inconvenience, and environmental
groups may raise concerns about long-term damage. In that case, the real battle
is not only engineering; it is also persuasion.
[例如，如果政府打算擴闊道路，或者興建新車站，商戶可能會擔心施工期間生意受影響，
附近居民可能會抱怨多年不便，而環保團體也可能會對長遠破壞提出質疑。在這種情況下，
真正的挑戰不只是工程本身，也同樣是說服工作。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say local governments often face two big obstacles when developing transportation infrastructure: practical difficulty and human resistance. In essence，good transport may move people more easily, but building it often means pushing through money problems, political pressure, and public frustration first.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說地方政府在發展交通基建時，通常要面對兩大障礙：實際執行上的困難，以及來自人的阻力。總結來說，良好的交通固然能讓人更方便地流動，但在把它建成之前，往往要先穿過資金問題、政治壓力，以及公眾的不滿。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say local governments often face two big obstacles
when developing transportation infrastructure: practical difficulty and human
resistance. In essence，good transport may move people more easily, but
building it often means pushing through money problems, political pressure,
and public frustration first.
[總結來說，我會說地方政府在發展交通基建時，通常要面對兩大障礙：實際執行上的困難
，以及來自人的阻力。總結來說，良好的交通固然能讓人更方便地流動，但在把它建成之前
，往往要先穿過資金問題、政治壓力，以及公眾的不滿。]

question.english: Why do some people prefer to travel abroad rather than travel in their own country?
question.chinese: 為甚麼有些人比起在自己國家旅行，更喜歡出國旅行？

source_lines.text: Why do some people prefer to travel abroad rather than travel in their own country?
為甚麼有些人比起在自己國家旅行，更喜歡出國旅行？

components.english: I think one big reason is that travelling abroad gives people a stronger sense of novelty and adventure.
components.chinese: 我覺得其中一個很大的原因是，出國旅行通常能帶來更強的新鮮感和冒險感。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one big reason is that travelling abroad gives people a stronger
sense of novelty and adventure.
[我覺得其中一個很大的原因是，出國旅行通常能帶來更強的新鮮感和冒險感。]

components.english: When people travel in their own country, however beautiful it may be, the language, food, customs, and surroundings can still feel relatively familiar. By contrast, going abroad can feel like stepping into a different world. People get to hear a different language, see different streets, and experience a different rhythm of life. In that sense, foreign travel often feels less like a short break and more like opening a new chapter for a while.
components.chinese: 當人們在自己國家旅行時，不管那地方有多美，語言、食物、習俗，以及周圍環境很多時仍然會讓人感到相對熟悉。相比之下，出國旅行則常常像是走進另一個世界。人們可以聽到不同的語言、看到不同的街道，也感受到另一種生活節奏。從這個角度來說，外國旅行很多時不只是短暫休息，更像是暫時打開了人生裡新的一章。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When people travel in their own country, however beautiful it
may be, the language, food, customs, and surroundings can still feel relatively
familiar. By contrast, going abroad can feel like stepping into a different world.
People get to hear a different language, see different streets, and experience a
different rhythm of life. In that sense, foreign travel often feels less like a short
break and more like opening a new chapter for a while.
[當人們在自己國家旅行時，不管那地方有多美，語言、食物、習俗，以及周圍環境很多時仍
然會讓人感到相對熟悉。相比之下，出國旅行則常常像是走進另一個世界。人們可以聽到不
同的語言、看到不同的街道，也感受到另一種生活節奏。從這個角度來說，外國旅行很多時
不只是短暫休息，更像是暫時打開了人生裡新的一章。]

components.english: For example, someone from Hong Kong may enjoy local trips, of course, but a visit to Japan, Korea, or Europe may feel far more exciting because almost everything, from the signs on the street to the meals on the table, feels fresh to them.
components.chinese: 例如，一個來自香港的人當然也可以享受本地旅行，但如果去日本、韓國，或者歐洲，他往往會覺得更加興奮，因為幾乎所有東西，從街上的招牌到餐桌上的食物，對他來說都帶著一種新鮮感。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone from Hong Kong may enjoy local trips, of
course, but a visit to Japan, Korea, or Europe may feel far more exciting because
almost everything, from the signs on the street to the meals on the table, feels
fresh to them.
[例如，一個來自香港的人當然也可以享受本地旅行，但如果去日本、韓國，或者歐洲，他往
往會覺得更加興奮，因為幾乎所有東西，從街上的招牌到餐桌上的食物，對他來說都帶著一
種新鮮感。]

components.english: So, in many cases, people choose to travel abroad because it offers a stronger feeling of discovery.
components.chinese: 所以，在很多情況下，人們選擇出國旅行，是因為那能帶來更強烈的探索感。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in many cases, people choose to travel abroad because it offers
a stronger feeling of discovery.
[所以，在很多情況下，人們選擇出國旅行，是因為那能帶來更強烈的探索感。]

components.english: Another reason is that some people see travelling abroad as a better way to truly switch off from everyday life.
components.chinese: 另一個原因是，有些人會把出國旅行看成一種更能真正抽離日常生活的方法。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another reason is that some people see travelling abroad as a better
way to truly switch off from everyday life.
[另一個原因是，有些人會把出國旅行看成一種更能真正抽離日常生活的方法。]

components.english: If people stay in their own country, they may still feel mentally tied to work, family duties, or everyday routines. The scenery changes, but the mental baggage sometimes comes along for the ride. Going abroad, however, creates more distance, both physically and psychologically. It is easier to feel that you have left ordinary life behind instead of merely stepping a few streets away from it.
components.chinese: 如果人們仍然留在自己國家旅行，他們有時在心理上仍然會和工作、家庭責任，或者日常節奏連在一起。風景也許變了，但心裡的包袱很多時還是跟著一起上路。相比之下，出國會拉開更大的距離，不論是身體上的，還是心理上的。那樣一來，人會更容易真正感覺自己把平凡生活留在身後，而不只是離開了幾條街而已。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: If people stay in their own country, they may still feel mentally
tied to work, family duties, or everyday routines. The scenery changes, but the
mental baggage sometimes comes along for the ride. Going abroad, however,
creates more distance, both physically and psychologically. It is easier to feel that
you have left ordinary life behind instead of merely stepping a few streets away
from it.
[如果人們仍然留在自己國家旅行，他們有時在心理上仍然會和工作、家庭責任，或者日常
節奏連在一起。風景也許變了，但心裡的包袱很多時還是跟著一起上路。相比之下，出國會
拉開更大的距離，不論是身體上的，還是心理上的。那樣一來，人會更容易真正感覺自己把
平凡生活留在身後，而不只是離開了幾條街而已。]

components.english: For instance, if someone takes a domestic trip, they may still answer messages, think about errands, or feel that home is just around the corner. But once they are in another country, the break often feels more complete, almost as if the noise of ordinary life has been turned right down.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人只是在本地旅行，他可能仍然會回訊息、惦記著一些瑣事，或者總覺得家其實就在不遠處。但一旦去了另一個國家，那種休息很多時就會顯得更完整，幾乎像是把平日生活的嘈雜聲一下子調低了很多。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if someone takes a domestic trip, they may still answer
messages, think about errands, or feel that home is just around the corner. But
once they are in another country, the break often feels more complete, almost as
if the noise of ordinary life has been turned right down.
[例如，如果一個人只是在本地旅行，他可能仍然會回訊息、惦記著一些瑣事，或者總覺得
家其實就在不遠處。但一旦去了另一個國家，那種休息很多時就會顯得更完整，幾乎像是把
平日生活的嘈雜聲一下子調低了很多。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say some people prefer travelling abroad because it gives them both novelty and distance. It offers the feeling of stepping outside the familiar and seeing life from a wider angle, which is exactly what many people are looking for when they travel.
components.chinese: 總結來說，外國旅行之所以吸引，很多時不只是因為地方更遠，而是因為它更容易讓人覺得自己真的走出了原本的生活，去看一看更大的世界。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say some people prefer travelling abroad because it
gives them both novelty and distance. It offers the feeling of stepping outside
the familiar and seeing life from a wider angle, which is exactly what many
people are looking for when they travel.
[總結來說，外國旅行之所以吸引，很多時不只是因為地方更遠，而是因為它更容易讓人覺
得自己真的走出了原本的生活，去看一看更大的世界。]

question.english: Do you think travelling to another country can change the way that people think?
question.chinese: 你認為去另一個國家旅行會改變人們的想法嗎？

source_lines.text: Do you think travelling to another country can change the way that people think?
你認為去另一個國家旅行會改變人們的想法嗎？

components.english: Yes, absolutely, I think it can, because travelling to another country often pulls people out of their usual bubble.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對會，我認為旅行到另一個國家確實能改變人的想法，因為它往往會把人從自己原本熟悉的小圈子裡拉出來。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, absolutely, I think it can, because travelling to another country
often pulls people out of their usual bubble.
[是的，絕對會，我認為旅行到另一個國家確實能改變人的想法，因為它往往會把人從自己
原本熟悉的小圈子裡拉出來。]

components.english: The main reason is that when people are surrounded by different languages, customs, habits, and values, they begin to realise that their own way of doing things is not the only way. That can be very eye-opening. A person who has only ever looked at life through one window suddenly finds themselves standing in a different room altogether. In that sense, travel can make people more open-minded, more curious, and less quick to judge.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，當一個人置身於不同的語言、習俗、生活方式，以及價值觀之中時，他就會開始明白，自己原本那一套做事方式並不是唯一的方式。這種體驗往往非常開眼界。一個原本只透過一扇窗看世界的人，忽然發現自己站進了另一個完全不同的房間。從這個角度來看，旅行能令人變得更開放、更有好奇心，也不會那麼急著去批評別人。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that when people are surrounded by
different languages, customs, habits, and values, they begin to realise that their
own way of doing things is not the only way. That can be very eye-opening. A
person who has only ever looked at life through one window suddenly finds
themselves standing in a different room altogether. In that sense, travel can
make people more open-minded, more curious, and less quick to judge.
[主要原因是，當一個人置身於不同的語言、習俗、生活方式，以及價值觀之中時，他就會開
始明白，自己原本那一套做事方式並不是唯一的方式。這種體驗往往非常開眼界。一個原本
只透過一扇窗看世界的人，忽然發現自己站進了另一個完全不同的房間。從這個角度來看，
旅行能令人變得更開放、更有好奇心，也不會那麼急著去批評別人。]

components.english: For example, someone from Hong Kong who visits a country with a much slower pace of life may come back rethinking what success really means. They may start to question whether being constantly busy is truly a badge of honour, or whether a calmer, more balanced life actually makes more sense.
components.chinese: 例如，一個來自香港的人如果去了生活節奏慢得多的國家旅行，回來之後可能就會重新思考，成功到底是甚麼意思。他可能會開始懷疑，永遠忙個不停是否真的值得被視為一種光榮，還是其實一種更平靜、更平衡的生活才更合理。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone from Hong Kong who visits a country with a
much slower pace of life may come back rethinking what success really means.
They may start to question whether being constantly busy is truly a badge of
honour, or whether a calmer, more balanced life actually makes more sense.
[例如，一個來自香港的人如果去了生活節奏慢得多的國家旅行，回來之後可能就會重新思
考，成功到底是甚麼意思。他可能會開始懷疑，永遠忙個不停是否真的值得被視為一種光榮
，還是其實一種更平靜、更平衡的生活才更合理。]

components.english: So yes, I would say travelling abroad can definitely change the way people think, because it widens their perspective and gently shakes loose some of their old assumptions.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說到外國旅行的確可以改變人的想法，因為它能拓闊人的眼界，也能慢慢鬆動一些原本根深蒂固的舊觀念。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say travelling abroad can definitely change the
way people think, because it widens their perspective and gently shakes loose
some of their old assumptions.
[所以，是的，我會說到外國旅行的確可以改變人的想法，因為它能拓闊人的眼界，也能慢
慢鬆動一些原本根深蒂固的舊觀念。]

components.english: That said, I do not think travelling changes everyone in the same way, because a lot depends on how deeply people engage with the place they visit.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為旅行會以同樣方式改變每一個人，因為這很大程度上取決於他們有多深入地接觸那個地方。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think travelling changes everyone in the same way,
because a lot depends on how deeply people engage with the place they visit.
[不過，我也不認為旅行會以同樣方式改變每一個人，因為這很大程度上取決於他們有多深
入地接觸那個地方。]

components.english: To be honest, some people travel but stay inside a kind of comfort bubble the whole time. They only visit tourist spots, eat familiar food, and interact very little with local people. If that happens, the trip may be enjoyable, but it may not really change their thinking very much. In other words, travelling can open the door, but people still have to walk through it.
components.chinese: 老實說，有些人雖然去旅行了，但整趟旅程都待在一個舒服的小泡泡裡。他們只去旅遊景點、只吃自己熟悉的食物，而且幾乎不怎樣和當地人接觸。如果是這樣，那趟旅行也許會很開心，但未必真的能大大改變他們的想法。換句話說，旅行可以替人打開一扇門，但人還是要自己走進去。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, some people travel but stay inside a kind of
comfort bubble the whole time. They only visit tourist spots, eat familiar food,
and interact very little with local people. If that happens, the trip may be
enjoyable, but it may not really change their thinking very much. In other
words, travelling can open the door, but people still have to walk through it.
[老實說，有些人雖然去旅行了，但整趟旅程都待在一個舒服的小泡泡裡。他們只去旅遊景
點、只吃自己熟悉的食物，而且幾乎不怎樣和當地人接觸。如果是這樣，那趟旅行也許會很
開心，但未必真的能大大改變他們的想法。換句話說，旅行可以替人打開一扇門，但人還是
要自己走進去。]

components.english: For instance, a person who takes the time to talk to locals, try unfamiliar food, use public transport, and notice how daily life works is far more likely to come home with a changed perspective. By contrast, someone who treats the whole trip like a photo opportunity may enjoy it, but remain more or less the same inside.
components.chinese: 例如，一個願意花時間和當地人交談、嘗試陌生食物、搭乘公共交通，並留意當地人日常生活怎樣運作的人，回來時就更有可能帶著改變了的視角。相反，一個把整趟旅行都當成打卡拍照機會的人，也許同樣會玩得開心，但內在其實可能沒有太大變化。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a person who takes the time to talk to locals, try
unfamiliar food, use public transport, and notice how daily life works is far
more likely to come home with a changed perspective. By contrast, someone
who treats the whole trip like a photo opportunity may enjoy it, but remain
more or less the same inside.
[例如，一個願意花時間和當地人交談、嘗試陌生食物、搭乘公共交通，並留意當地人日常
生活怎樣運作的人，回來時就更有可能帶著改變了的視角。相反，一個把整趟旅行都當成打
卡拍照機會的人，也許同樣會玩得開心，但內在其實可能沒有太大變化。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say travelling to another country can absolutely change the way people think, but only if they travel with open eyes and an open mind. The trip itself may move the body, but it is curiosity that really moves the mind.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說去另一個國家旅行當然可以改變人的想法，但前提是那個人要帶著開放的眼睛和開放的心去旅行。旅程本身也許移動的是身體，但真正能推動思想改變的，往往還是好奇心。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say travelling to another country can absolutely
change the way people think, but only if they travel with open eyes and an open
mind. The trip itself may move the body, but it is curiosity that really moves the
mind.
[總結來說，我會說去另一個國家旅行當然可以改變人的想法，但前提是那個人要帶著開放
的眼睛和開放的心去旅行。旅程本身也許移動的是身體，但真正能推動思想改變的，往往還
是好奇心。]

question.english: Is it faster to travel by public transportation or by car in your country?
question.chinese: 在你地方，乘搭公共交通工具比較快，還是自己開車比較快？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is it faster to travel by public transportation or by car in your country?
在你地方，乘搭公共交通工具比較快，還是自己開車比較快？

components.english: In a place like Hong Kong, I’d generally say public transportation is faster for most everyday journeys.
components.chinese: 在像香港這樣的地方，我整體上會說，對大多數日常出行來說，公共交通通常比較快。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In a place like Hong Kong, I’d generally say public transportation is
faster for most everyday journeys.
[在像香港這樣的地方，我整體上會說，對大多數日常出行來說，公共交通通常比較快。]

components.english: The main reason is that the city is dense, busy, and often quite crowded, so driving can easily turn into a waiting game. By contrast, public transport, especially the MTR, usually fits the rhythm of the city much better. It is quick, direct, and far less stressful than sitting in traffic. On top of that, when you drive, the trip does not really end when you arrive, because you still have to think about parking.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，這座城市人口密集、節奏快，而且很多地方都相當擠迫，所以開車很多時很容易變成一場「慢慢等」的過程。相反，公共交通，特別是港鐵，通常更配合整個城市的節奏。它夠快、夠直接，而且比坐在車龍裡輕鬆得多。除此之外，開車有時到了目的地也不代表事情完結，因為你之後還要煩惱泊車。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that the city is dense, busy, and often quite
crowded, so driving can easily turn into a waiting game. By contrast, public
transport, especially the MTR, usually fits the rhythm of the city much better.
It is quick, direct, and far less stressful than sitting in traffic. On top of that,
when you drive, the trip does not really end when you arrive, because you still
have to think about parking.
[主要原因是，這座城市人口密集、節奏快，而且很多地方都相當擠迫，所以開車很多時很容
易變成一場「慢慢等」的過程。相反，公共交通，特別是港鐵，通常更配合整個城市的節奏。
它夠快、夠直接，而且比坐在車龍裡輕鬆得多。除此之外，開車有時到了目的地也不代表事
情完結，因為你之後還要煩惱泊車。]

components.english: For example, if someone needs to go from one busy area to another, like from Mong Kok to Central, taking the train is usually the easier and quicker option. If they drive instead, they may spend a lot of time stuck on the road and then even more time looking for a parking space.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人要由一個繁忙地區去另一個繁忙地區，例如由旺角去中環，那乘搭鐵路通常會是更輕鬆、也更快的選擇。如果改為自己開車，他很可能會先花很多時間塞在路上，之後又再花時間找車位。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone needs to go from one busy area to another,
like from Mong Kok to Central, taking the train is usually the easier and
quicker option. If they drive instead, they may spend a lot of time stuck on the
road and then even more time looking for a parking space.
[例如，如果一個人要由一個繁忙地區去另一個繁忙地區，例如由旺角去中環，那乘搭鐵路
通常會是更輕鬆、也更快的選擇。如果改為自己開車，他很可能會先花很多時間塞在路上，
之後又再花時間找車位。]

components.english: So, for ordinary day-to-day travel, I would say public transport is usually faster and more practical.
components.chinese: 所以，對一般日常出行來說，我會說公共交通通常是更快，也更實際的。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, for ordinary day-to-day travel, I would say public transport is
usually faster and more practical.
[所以，對一般日常出行來說，我會說公共交通通常是更快，也更實際的。]

components.english: That said, I do not think a car is always slower in every situation.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為在所有情況下，私家車就一定比較慢。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think a car is always slower in every situation.
[不過，我也不認為在所有情況下，私家車就一定比較慢。]

components.english: If someone is going somewhere less convenient, travelling very late at night, or carrying children, shopping bags, or heavy things, then a car can actually make more sense.
